## Players In Print Vol. 1

This manual is no ordinary compilation of
direct response advertising and I dare say
only a handful of insiders could have put it
together. It focuses on two of the most
successful direct response marketers of the
21st Century, who at one time were mentor
and mentee, founder and protege, father and
son-in-law. Theirs is the story of the old lion
and the young lion. They bitterly parted ways
years ago and their characters stand in sharp
opposition to one another.!

# !

The old lion has been through countless
trials by fire that would have toppled a dozen
lesser men. Yet the old lion returns
undaunted after each setback. He’s a study
in contrast. At one moment he appears as the
the bare chested promoter who stands tall,
proud and unflappable on center stage. He’s
a brawler who rarely ducks a fight. In another
he’s the erudite pioneer and boundary
breaker who’s determined to pass his oeuvre
on to the entrepreneurial class and try to
save them the heartache he knows so well.!

# !

The young lion is seemingly his opposite in
everything but knowledge, talent and vision.
He deliberately keeps a low profile and other
than a 90 minute presentation he gave in
1997, he’s never uttered a word about
marketing in public nor given so much as an
interview. He’s taken and expanded upon the
knowledge he acquired at the right hand of
his former mentor and ex-father-in-law.
Around 2008, he devised a completely
original response booster in print that
continues to succeed in many markets.!

# !

Despite their differences, they share the
same trail blazing, industry disrupting
philosophy backed up by a voracious testing
methodology that leaves lesser marketers
breathless and bewildered. They’ve
succeeded across print, direct mail and TV,
while the Internet has intentionally played an
ancillary role. !

# !


# !


# !

Just how successful are they? !

# !

Since their companies are private and they
utilize mail order, they “make their money in
the dark,” to use the words of Lyle Stuart. But
through the data their own companies have
released, (including list rental divisions) in
combination with Gale’s Business Insights
database, here’s a thumbnail sketch:!

# !

The Tale of the Old Lion!

# !

• The old lion’s company, which was founded
in 1968 was estimated by Business Insights
to have gross billings of $573,000,000 in
2004 with 750 employees. This is the last
reported year in the database.!

# !

• The old lion took an indebted, basement-
run company to 4,000% growth and turned
it into an industry leader.!

# !

• His company reinvented a commodity
product in 2004 that sells for 10 times that
of competitors. He’s sold over 3 million of
them through print, direct mail and TV for
gross sales of over $1 billion.!

# !

• He’s mailed hundreds of millions of direct
mail promotions in North America and had
tens of thousands of display ad insertions.!

# !

• The old lion has a 40+ year track record as
one of the most successful and prolific print
ad copywriters with products in categories
including: supplements, health devices,
weight loss, exercise equipment, golf, flags,
numismatics, heaters, coolers, air purifiers,
costume jewelry, information products,
business opportunity, horoscopes, beauty
and rejuvenation, memorabilia, specialty
products, home study courses, gadgets,
cleaning products, cruises, art work
sweepstakes, gourmet food and politics.!

# !

• He devised the campaign that pulled $262
for every letter mailed and got between a
3% and 5% response rate (depending on
the list) for an average order of $6,000 to



## Players In Print Vol. 1

$8,000. By all accounts, the highest known
GPP (Gross profit per prospect) of any
promotion ever mailed. !

# !

• The old lion is a master at marketing and
selling $500 specialty goods off the page.
After 40 years of plying his trade, he
remains one of the foremost print ad
copywriters and the greatest exponent of
“Q&A copywriting.”!

# !

• At the time of this writing in late 2015, the
73-year-old legend, Ben Suarez, is
enduring his latest test of finishing a 15
month sentence at a minimum federal
corrections facility after being convicted of
obstruction during a a campaign finance
investigation. He will no doubt return.!

# !

The Tale of the Young Lion!

# !

• He began at age 15 by pushing a broom on
the factory room floor and worked his way
up through every position in the old lion’s
company as it grew by 4,000%. He became
the old lion’s mentee, protege and later
chief operating officer.!

# !

• The young lion is one of the greatest direct
response students in history with the most
capable of mentors. !

# !

• Upon parting ways with his mentor, he set
about a strategy of realizing the shortest
path to success: selling products which are
already selling en masse but with… !

# !

Better marketing, better copy, better story
and higher price points!

# !

• He went out on his own in 2000 and
created massively successful direct
response companies right out of the gate.!

# !

• He’s run thousands of print ads over the
last fifteen yeas and almost everyone !
knows his products but few know his name.!
• The young lion (and his mentor) are
masters of the remnant ad or ROP (run of
print) and continue to sell a vast array of
products off the page. !

# !

• He’s an aggressive back end marketer who
scientifically applies RFM (recency,
frequency and monetary value). !

# !

• The young lion turned a copywriting
footnote into a big idea that sold over $500
million worth of a product that directly
competes with his former mentor. The
personal dynamic of this one-time family
has shaped direct response as a whole.!

# !

• He founded a numismatics company that
sold $365,290,090 worth of products. (This
is a dated company figure from his list
rental division from September 2012.)!

# !

• He grew a supplements company from start
up to maturity in short order and sold it to
Twinlab in 2012 for a giant payday.!

# !

• The young lion is one of the foremost direct
response players who sells off the page
while building brand awareness and
pushing retail at the same time. His
understanding of retail as the endgame of
direct response helped get his products on
the shelves of thousands of chains stores in
short order before selling his company. This
is something his mentor has yet to achieve.!

# !

• His CTAs (calls to action) are the finest ever
crafted and genuinely employ urgency,
specificity, scarcity, social proof and greed.
DR on this level demands only one means
of response: the toll-free order hotline and
overflow hotlines. !

# !

• Rodney Napier packs more selling power
into a caption than any copywriter ever
seen, while using visually arresting, boldly
emotional images. His captions cover a
laundry list of direct response elements,
including: scarcity, overcoming objections,
social proof, restating deadlines, order



## Players In Print Vol. 1

options, event marketing, satisfaction
guarantee and pushing retail.!

# !

The “Canton Connection”!

# !

The Canton, Ohio area has long been a
hotbed of direct response talent. Besides Ben
Suarez and Rodney Napier, there’s Ben’s
brother, Richard Suarez, who founded the
$100 million a year company, Fitness Quest,
which he sold for a tidy sum in the 1990s.!

# !

Another Ohioan, Gary Halbert, played a key
role in getting Ben Suarez on the right footing
in the early days by directed him into the
astrology market and away from a doomed
product he fell in love with. Early classified
ads read: “Free Astrology Party Tonight…
Taurus Only.” These latter turned into the
2,500 word full page ads that gave his
company much needed cash. Suarez wrote a
dedication to Gary Halbert in the 2008 update
of his book, 7 Steps to Freedom. This book is
must reading for anyone in direct response.
More than just the nuts and bolts of Ben
Suarez’s NPGS (Net Profit Generation
System) is the valuable insight into the mind
and motivations of one of the most important
direct response players.!

# !

Tellingly of Rodney Napier’s low profile is
fellow Ohioan Dan Kennedy’s misattribution
of the Heat Surge to Ben Suarez. If Dan can
be tripped up by this, it’s worth paying
attention to. In a 2012 sales letter he wrote
“Mr. Suarez and his Amish heaters and his
Eden-Pure heaters - with which he competes
with himself.” And in a In a May 2011
newsletter, there’s a dissection of an “Amish
heater” ad but no mention of the founder,
Rodney Napier. This is not about taking a
swipe at Dan but rather about the ability to
make vast sums out in the open without
anyone necessarily knowing who you are.
And, Napier is not the only northeastern Ohio
marketer who flies below the radar.!

# !


# !

How this manual was compiled!
!

# !

An advertising compilation is only as
valuable as the knowledge that the
composite ads and products were reliable at
one time at generating response. Current
testing in an appropriate medium is worth a
thousand opinions. Last year’s advertising
control or hot product can fall on its face
today. Conversely, ads and products that
bombed cannot be completely written off,
since some could have been ahead of the
market. !

# !

This compilation consists of 195 annotated
ads beginning in 1975 and ending in 2015,
with most of the ads running within the last
ten years. Some of the ads were
blockbusters with hundreds of insertions
while others were one-offs. Trying to
understand why an ad (or product) didn’t
make it is almost as important as
understanding why one succeeded.!

# !

The insertion rates for each ad were
meticulously counted from the first verifiable
insertion to the last using a variety of
databases. Knowing the track records of
these ads and products with near certainty…!

# !

Elevates you to a whole new class as a
copywriter, marketer or founder! !
It is my hope this manual provides you with
an arsenal of new ideas and forces you into
new markets. At the same time, I do not want
your ad or product to be "EXHIBIT A" in an
FTC proceeding, or whatever regulatory
powers that be in the country where you live.
All of your advertising should be vetted by an
experienced advertising lawyer.!

# !

Yours for bolder response,!

# !

Lawrence

## The Origin Power Proof Element

Product origin has been a consistently relied
upon lead by Ben Suarez since 1974 when he
published the ad: “Why People In Vermont Are
Healthier, Less Overweight, Stay Young Longer,
Live Longer Than People Of Any Other State In
The Union.”! !
Around that time, his company’s revenue was
extremely volatile and he was a promotion or two
away from closing shop. In the hopes of
supplementing his only winner at the time,
“Astrology Today,” he resurrected an obscure
book that sold very well in the 1950s, titled “Folk
Medicine: A Vermont Doctor's Guide to Good
Health” by Dr. D.C. Jarvis (page 188). The ad was
a winner and ran for several years.!
The success of the “Vermont Folk Medicine” ad
inspired Suarez to turn to an even more powerful
set of proof elements based on origin with the
lead,“Fountain of Youth Discovered By Little
Known Civilization Over 2300 Years Ago.” ! !
Here’s the opener.! !
“They still exist isolated from the world where
they live to be 120 to 140 years old. They have no
cancer heart attacks or other major disorders to
speak of. They are active and fit to the very end.
Men father children at 100 years and older.
Overweight people are unheard of for they have
the perfect weight control system.! !
Why haven't you heard much about this
mysterious civilization? First, few people know
they exist. They are literally isolated from the rest
of the world by a series of impassable mountain
accessible only by foot through mountain passes
which narrow to 18 inches at some points. This
civilization originated in 330 B.C. when an army
division of Alexander The Great of Macedonia
broke away, took Persian wives and purposely
lost themselves in the vastness of the Himalayas.” !
ranges in the Himalayas. This small nation is
called Hunza. It is located in West Pakistan, 65
miles Northeast of Gilgit and 18 miles South of
the border of the U.S.S.R. The country is

# !

Not a bad lead, huh? It was written in 1977 and
reads respectably even by today’s copy
standards. (You can find this remarkable 3600+
word ad on page 189.) Of course, there was a
precedent for successful advertising leads
featuring the Hunzas and no doubt Suarez was
aware of them. ! !
J.I. Rodale, founder of Rodale Press, published
the book "Healthy Hunzas” back in 1948 and the
headline was “No cancer here.” That was followed
up in 1960 when the book “Hunza Land” was
released along with this advertising headline:
“Imagine yourself living past 90 and 100 in full,
vigorous health!”! !
The Hunza lead continues to this day.! !

# !

The Selling Power of Origin! !
Claims are a dime a dozen and lesser
copywriters make a lot of them. Take pain relief. A
typical headline with a barebones claim might go:
“Relieves pain in 10 minutes.” Now, look how Ben
Suarez takes this plain claim and ratchets up the
headline’s stopping power with origin. ! !
“Now Available In United States!
Product used in Japan for 71 years relieves pain
in 10 minutes without taking dangerous drugs”! !



## The Origin Power Proof Element

Imagine a prospect coming across this
headline. (The ad is on page 66.)! !
While he may glance at the ad, “Relieves pain
in 10 minutes,” the origin headline really grabs
him. Why? Because he’s likely tried numerous
pain relief products with fair to middling results.
Now, he’s presented with a potential solution from
Japan and that intrigues him because he has
certain preconceived notions about the Japanese
such as their longevity, low rates of obesity and
vigorous lifestyles. As marketers, we needn’t
concern ourselves about the accuracy of these
preconceptions, just be aware they exist. The
good news is they can do much of the heavy
lifting in selling.! !
Here are some more origin headlines from
Suarez and Napier before moving on to the big
origin marketing case study in “Secrets of Selling
Commodity Products for 10 Times More.”!
“Demand soars for joint health miracle !
discovered in Japan’s ‘feel good village’! !
Special report brings national attention to an
amazing molecule now reproduced in
breakthrough joint pill available in U.S. drugstores! !
____________________________! !
“Relieve Pain Without Dangerous Drugs! !
New Advanced Heating Pad From Canada!
Brings Pain Relief In As Fast As 30 Minutes”! !
Hospital studies show it relieves pain from
arthritis, back, bursitis, carpal tunnel, tendonitis,
hemorrhoids, migraines and more”!
____________________________! !
"Over 12 million people discover pain fighting
secret" from the island of Mallorca, Spain! !
Dubbed the 'Crown Jewel of Health and Well
Being,' European Balance Bracelet leads charge
among natural pain relief”! !

## Third Party Authority Proof Element

Analogous to the origin proof element, the third
party authority proof element does a lot of the
heavy lifting for us marketers, whether it’s in print,
direct mail, TV or on the Web. ! !
Take Clear-Cast, it’s a $38 gadget that
pulls in digital channels for free with no
monthly bills. That’s obviously a huge appeal
for consumers who may be sick of spending
hundreds a month on cable, satellite,
streaming media or a combination thereof. !
You could come up with many headline
approaches for such a mass market product.
Here was one of the early headlines that ran
as a full page in the USA Today on Friday,
June 22, 2012. (Appears on page 133.) !
“Public gets Free TV with no monthly bills” !
Obviously, Napier tested this in regional
publications before going national. And, the
ad fists squarely in the first stage of market
awareness, in which prospects have little or
no concept about the product, so the
copywriter can come out and tell it like it is,
AIDA style. !
Six months later Napier employed his
trademark “regional segmentation” headline
in a similar vein. (See page 132.) !
“New invention gives Illinoisans Free TV
and gets rid of cable or satellite bills” !
But as the early stage advertising works its
way through the market in a successful
campaign, it begins to lose some steam,
whether there’s intense competition or not.
Here’s the full lead Napier came up with two
years later. !
“Consumers rush to get free TV after
Supreme Court ruling !
U.S. households to get free TV with slick little
micro antenna device engineered to pull in all
over-the-air TV channels for free as law
forces network giants to broadcast all TV
signals regionally to all 50 states” !
party authority headline. Here, we have a
headline that references the Supreme Court,
not unlike an article we’d see in any news
publication. Yes, there are ample benefits
mentioned in the subheads but it’s the
authority of the Supreme Court that delivers
the readership for the ad. And, the higher the
readership, the higher the response.
This is certainly a different approach than
the earlier ads and it demonstrates the third

# !

The real beauty of this type of headline is it
escapes the predictable approach of making
obvious advertising claims. Look at the
benefit laden EdenPURE infrared portable
heater. It heats the room evenly, wall-to-wall
and floor-to-ceiling. It doesn’t get hot,
cannot start a fire and will not reduce
humidity or oxygen. And to top it off, it can
save up to 50% on home heating bills. All of
this adds up to an eye-grabbing lead and in
fact, all of this has been used under the
endorsement of Bob Villa, the American
home improvement television show host. !
But what if we wanted to sidestep a lead
with a direct advertising claim and opt for
one using a third party with authority? !
“Farmers' Almanac predicts cold, frosty,
wet winter for most of the United States !
Just in time EdenPURE announces
the world's most advanced heater” !
It’s brilliant simplicity that reads like a
news headline. While the reader may not be
interested in buying a Farmers’ Almanac,
she’s certain to read a few paragraphs to see
what the prediction is for the coming winter.

## The 3-Prong Headline Template

Many of Ben Suarez’s and Rodney Napier’s
most successful ads follow a specific template.!
By the time you’ve gone halfway through this
manual, you’ll know it cold!
Suarez’s Secrets of selling !
specialty products for $500 and up! !
____________________________! !
“19 U.S. outbreaks of E. coli since 1995 and all
avoidable with the new EdenPURE... read on ! !
Your water filter isn’t killing bacteria ! !
Filters inefficient, chlorine dangerous but you can
kill up to 99.9% of disease in your water”!
____________________________! !
The template has three distinct parts. Either a
prehead, headline and subhead or a headline
followed by two subheads. The components of the
template are usually separated by short horizontal
line breaks to maximize readability.! !
The prehead above references the numerous E.
coli and water contamination outbreaks that
routinely affect municipal water supplies in the
U.S. while the headline confronts the prospect
with a sneaking suspicion he may have harbored
for a while. The subhead lands the claim: “kill up
to 99.9% of disease in your water.”! !
This was no one-off ad, Suarez excels at selling
a vast array of health related devices off the page!
and the EdenPURE portable water filter goes for
$397 delivered! !
Organisms potentially present in untreated
domestic water,” including bacteria, protozoa and
viruses, and you’ll be alarmed enough to read on
(See page 79). Suarez remains one of the
foremost copywriters at selling specialty products
in the $500 range. Here’s a different lead for the
same product which saw numerous insertions.!
How do you sell a $397 product off a full page !
display ad? One look at the table of “Living !

# !

____________________________! !
“U.S. Government Health Officials report over !
1 million people get sick annually !
from drinking water! !
Disease may be lurking In your water !
making you sick! !
Protect your family with great tasting, healthy
water NO filters, NO plumbing, NO bulkiness,
Completely Portable”!
____________________________! !
And to remove all doubt about the viability of
selling water related gadgets by the truckload —
with the right sales pitch, of course — here’s
another for “Magic Living Water Ceramics.”!
____________________________! !
“Purge Drinking Water Of Bad Taste !
And Smell In 30 Seconds ! !
Lower Chlorine Taste And Smell, Prolong The Life
Of A Pot Of Coffee, Increase High Oxygen !
Content, Lengthen Bloom Life Of Flowers ! !
Readers Of This Publication Get A $75 Discount”!
____________________________! !
The headline employs the classic “claim-time-
frame” structure, the first subhead gives us a
laundry list of benefits, and the second subhead
uses a universal money saving appeal, often
found in the 3-prong headline template.!
____________________________!
MAJOR DEVELOPMENT ! !
“New lotion gets collagen and hyaluronic acid into
the skin without $1,000 injections ! !
Helps renew wrinkled, sagging and thin skin !
to a youthful appearance ! !
Reduces wrinkles in 15 seconds”!
____________________________!



## The 3-Prong Headline Template

A Ben Suarez ad usually has the look and feel
of a news story or an important press release, so
it’s no surprise many ads begin with the “Major
Development” prehead in varying font sizes.! !
Here’s a fat, 51-word lead for the Slim-n-Burn
diet supplement. (See page 108.)!
____________________________! !
MAJOR DEVELOPMENT! !
“Dieters’ Worst Problems: Solved ! !
Two Clinical Trials, Six Toxicity Studies And
Two U.S. Patents Bring You The Best Weight
Loss Supplement Of The Century ! !
Guaranteed Weight And Body Fat Reduction,
Guaranteed Boost In Metabolism, Regulates
Appetite Plus You Are Guaranteed To See Your
Waist And Your Hips Just Melt Away”!
____________________________! !
The 3-prong headline template has a winning
track record for getting prospects to read long
copy ads. Now, take a look at Rodney Napier’s
spin on it using the allure of captions.!
This lead demonstrates the “high art” of regional
segmentation and easily a dozen other direct
response lessons. The caption beneath the photo
of the armed guards with a dolly full of vault bags
serves as the prehead. (See page 148.)!
____________________________! !
individual Vault Bags full of money that everyone
is trying to get. That’s because each Vault Bag is
known to contain over 100 U.S. Gov’t issued
coins some dating back to the early 1900s. !
“BISMARCK AREA RESIDENTS CASH IN:
Pictured above and protected by armed guards
are the Overstuffed Money Bags containing 10

# !

State zip codes determine who gets !
free Silver coins! !
Vault Bags loaded with U.S. Gov’t issued coins
are up for grabs as thousands of U.S. residents
stand to miss the deadline to claim the money;
now any resident of North Dakota who finds their
zip code listed below gets to claim the bags of
money for themselves and keep all the valuable
coins found inside by covering the Vault Bag fee
within the next 2 days”!
____________________________! !
!
Is that a lead or is that a lead? Perhaps, this ad
explains how Napier’s World Reserve Monetary
Exchange sold 2,519,242 units at a $145 average
selling price for a total of $365,290,090 in gross
sales. It’s all here: regional segmentation, greed,
scarcity, urgency and social engineering. ! !
The headline, “State zip codes determine who
gets free Silver coins” lifts this ad out of the realm
of selling and into that of qualification and
compliance. What goes through the prospect’s
head when he see this ad? Almost certainly, “Do I
qualify based on my North Dakota Zip Code?”! !
So, he consults the table with the “State Zip
Code Distribution List” and, of course, finds his
zip code listed among the 72 others in the
Bismarck area. Now, that he’s eligible for the offer,
he can go back and read the copy in depth.! !
Napier excels at writing riveting caption copy
beyond anything print ad copywriters could have
conceived, even as recently as the year 2000.
The captions include a laundry list of direct
response elements, including: scarcity,
overcoming objections, social proof, restating
deadlines, order options, event marketing,
satisfaction guarantee and pushing retail.! !
One of the big takeaways from a Napier ad is
the use of captions as the segue to reading the
body copy. While people may not read all the
copy, they almost always read the captions.!



## Secrets of Selling Commodity (Specialty) Products for 10 Times More

When Ben Suarez unveiled his first print ads for
the EdenPure Models 500 and 1000 portable
heaters in October 2004, it was a milestone in
direct response. Why? Because for decades,
space heaters were prosaic, seasonal items sold
in hardware stores and later at big box retailers.
The portable heater certainly didn't appear to be a
product that lent itself to direct response.!
! !

# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !

The breakthrough 2005 EdenPure ad (pg. 11)! !
Ben Suarez proved otherwise. His heaters were
being offered for 10 to 12 times the typical retail
price and he reaped all the benefits of selling by
mail order. One of Suarez’s point men, Julius
Toth, is the brains behind EdenPure. (You can find
his own company’s flagship product, the
EcoWasher, on page 181.) If you polled a random
consumer on the street corner, she’d tell you,
“surely EdenPure (a brilliant product name) must
be the superior portable heater.” But from the get-
go, product specs confirm that Suarez’s
EdenPure, and later Napier’s Heat Surge, are
little different in output, safety and efficiency than
most $50 ceramic heaters from brick and mortar
stores or the plethora sold through Amazon.com.
Both consume 1,500 watts of electricity and
produce 5,118 BTUs of heat.! !
So, how did Suarez pull off this little act of
alchemy that no one had dared try before?! !
What Suarez really figured out was how to use
direct response to sell “cuddling by the fireplace.”
way for your family to feel warm in any season
and save the expense of heating unused areas.” !
!
And now for the textbook caption from the same
ad: “The 2014 EdenPURE® Personal and Classic
Heaters are safe around children and pets. The
Classic includes our new ‘smart sensor’ and child-
safety lock protects your family and automatically
shuts off the heater if there is a problem.” This
caption sits beneath a photo of a smiling toddler
kneeling between two EdenPure units.!
Here’s copy from the “Farmer’s Almanac” ad !
(pg. 15) that highlights it: “Using the EdenPURE
Classic’s programmable thermostat is the best

# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !

!
! !
This photo and caption combo conveys product
safety better than a thousand words of copy ! !
But the question remains: “How’s it possible to
sell a commodity product for ten times that of your
competitors?” The answer is two-fold. First, Ben
Suarez took a page from the 100-year-old
playbook of Claude Hopkins whose advertising
brought Schlitz Beer from fifth place to tied for first
in mere months. Hopkins talked up the purity of
Schlitz and provided an exhaustive description of
the brewing process: from the cooled plate glass
rooms where the brewing took place to the air
tight cauldrons in which the beer was filtered and
sterilized. But how was this different than the
process of Schlitz’s competitors? Not one iota.
What made this strategy work like gangbusters
(both then and now) was the advertiser was the
first and only one to come out and elaborate on
the process, or what Eugene Schwartz would
later refer to as the mechanism.! !
Additionally, the EdenPure case study highlights
the use of premium price as a powerful proof
element. The thinking from the prospect’s point of
view is: “If a regular widget costs X, and the
higher priced one sells for 10 times X, then the
higher priced widget must be clearly superior.” !
Obviously, it’s not possible to pull this pricing
strategy off with just any product, but being aware !



## Secrets of Selling Commodity (Specialty) Products for 10 Times More

of it opens a universe of opportunities.! !
The Power of the “Marketing Moat”! !
Warren Buffett coined the term, “economic
moat,” referring to a business' ability to maintain
an advantage over its competitors, in order to
protect its long-term profits and market share. In
the marketing realm, we can achieve the same
end through product positioning and long copy
advertising. We create our marketing moat
through copy that: taps our market’s deepest
desires, solves its pressing problems, presents
stone cold logic, and paints a picture of a future
that can only be satisfied through buying our
products. If the copy has done this successfully, it
leaves no room to “go exploring” for alternatives,
online or off. The marketing moat is “anti-search”
by its nature and direct response advertisers must
consciously build it into their ads.! !

# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !

! !

# !


# !

The art of selling “cuddling by the fireplace”! !
When Suarez published his first EdenPure
promos in mass market publications in 2004, he
left few escape routes for interested prospects,
despite the comparatively exorbitant prices of
$297 for the Model 500 and $397 for the Model
1000. Here’s how Suarez built an “anti-search
moat” into the copy. Let’s start with safety, since
that’s a major objection many consumers have to
space heaters. Heres the sub-head from the “New
Advanced Portable Heater” ad from February of
2005: “Does not get hot, cannot start a fire and
will not reduce humidity or oxygen.”! !
Aside from the headline, the captions are the
most read parts of a print ad because our eyes
are naturally drawn to pictures. Now, look what
Suarez has done with this picture-caption combo
— both above and beneath the photo! He shows
the family nesting in the living room complete with
baby on one side of the heater, toddler on the
other, and kitty on top. Suarez has largely
vaporized the decades long fire fear associated
with portable heaters. But the consummate copy
pro, Suarez, does not stop there — he hammers
the objection head on in the copy:!
Now for the caption: “Cannot start a fire; a child
or animal can touch or sit on it without harm.” !

# !

“The number one cause of residential fires in
the United States is portable heaters. But the
EdenPure cannot cause a fire. That is because
the quartz infrared heating element never gets to
a temperature that can ignite anything. The
outside of the EdenPure only gets warm to the
touch so that it will not burn children or pets. Pets
can sleep on it when it is operating without harm.”! !
Now, when you wedge this powerful photo
caption combo next to an Ogilvyesque "How it
works” demonstration box, you realize…! !
It’s no accident Ben Suarez has sold 3 million
EdenPure space heaters since 2006.! !
And at the long-time minimum unit price of $297
that approaches $900 million in gross sales.!
You’ll see this referenced in the 2015 ad, “Never
Be Cold Again with the Cadillac of Heaters.” (See
page 16). Suarez Corporation is a private
company, so it’s impossible to know their exact
numbers, but Gale’s Business Insights Essentials
helped corroborate this by estimating S.C.I.’s
gross billings at $573 million in 2004. ! !
Now, imagine what’s going through Rodney
Napier’s mind as he watches the stunning
success of his former mentor and ex-father-in-law
in a market that’s not supposed to be direct
response friendly. He decides it’s time to take a
stab at it in 2007:! !
“Public slashing heat bills! !
Free giveaway launches new Heat Surge mobile
fireplace that actually uses less energy than a
coffee maker. Leave it on all night, every night
and never be cold again.”!
!



## Secrets of Selling Commodity (Specialty) Products for 10 Times More

Napier’s “Amish fire place” is now one of the
best known origin marketing case studies but in
the infancy of this campaign, the Amish were a
mere footnote — a footnote that quickly morphed
into a half-billion dollar business. Here’s the
mention deep in the body copy of this 2007 ad:! !

# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !

! !

# !


# !


# !

!
! !

# !


# !

Mere mortal predecessor to the Amish magic! !
“The Heat Surge company has even arranged for
readers to get their Fireless Flame Power Units
encased in real Amish built solid wood fireplace
mantles. They are being handmade in the USA in
the heart of Amish country. They are even hand
rubbed, stained and varnished. You just can’t find
custom made Amish heirloom furniture like this in
the national chain stores.”! !
The half billion dollar footnote!
!
It didn’t take Napier long to seize on this
passage of copy and turn it into the big idea we
know today. Within months the ubiquitous ads!
with Amish craftsman laboring away in their
workshops hit publications coast to coast. Why
was the Amish angle added to Julius Toth’s
brilliant EdenPure creation such a game changer?
Because in the blink of an eye, the prospect takes
in powerful sub-conscious stimuli combined with
visual evidence of quality, old world craftsmanship
and yesteryear’s values preserved by the Amish.! !
This combination of instantly apprehensible
images, powerful copy and an irresistible offer
began to work its magic on the market. Napier’s
own numbers from his list rental business shows
1,044,125 Heat Surge units sold for an
average unit of sale of $460. That comes out
to over $480 million. But these figures were
last updated in September of 2012, so we
can only imagine where the tally stands now.! !
In February of 2009, The New York Times did
the best favor any major publication can do for a
marketer — it published a half-page article about
the Heat Surge entitled: “Amish Space Heater: Is
That an Oxymoron?” Here’s the opener: “For a
people who make up less than a tenth of a
percent of the country’s population, the Amish
loom large in the American imagination as simple-
living, God-fearing country folk who keep the pre-
industrial past alive. We know them as farmers
and skilled craftspeople, with high standards and
an unfussy sensibility that they bring to the barns
they raise, the baked goods they sell at
greenmarkets and the quilts and furniture they
produce.” (The NYT article is on page 1.)!
Early Amish ad featured in New York Times
article and later the “ad within the ad” in 2012! !
Unless it’s a hatchet job or a ride along on an
FTC raid, there’s rarely such a thing as bad
publicity and The Times even graciously added a
reprint of the early ad,”Amish mantle and miracle
invention help home heat bills hit rock bottom.”
The article became the inspiration for the 2012
ad: “What’s the deal with the Amish fireplace, are
they really made by the Amish?” This “ad within
the ad” format (page 4) looks and reads like an
article and it can capture heaps of readership for
products and brands that are already well known
to the market. The ad also targets many of the



## Secrets of Selling Commodity (Specialty) Products for 10 Times More

lingering objections consumers have had to the
product and meets them out in the open.
Interestingly, the founder, Rod Napier, isn’t
mentioned anywhere in The Times article and
unlike Ben Suarez, he seems to prefer it this way. !
!
From the direct response perspective, it’s the
captions in this campaign that really help drive
response (from page 8): “CAUGHT ON CAMERA:
Photos reveal Amish secrets of the miracle
fireplace mantles. Hand Built, Amish Secrets,
Hand Rubbed, Save Money and Quality
Furniture.” It’s hard to pack more selling power
into a caption than Napier does and you’ll learn
more about how to create captions that sell from
one of his ads than you would from a dozen
advertising text books.!
Interestingly, the Amish concept has taken on a
life of its own and led to a variety of unusual
appeals. Take this 2009 ad near the height of the
recession. It plays on the reader’s heartstrings
over the plight of the out of work Amish. There’s a
picture of an Amish father and young boy holding
hands walking toward a covered bridge and the
caption reads: ! !
“OUT OF WORK: With time on his hands, this
Amish craftsman takes a walk with his son as he
weighs his options. As consumers demand lower
prices, the Amish refuse to cut quality but are
willing to give in on prices to keep working.”! !
market in the tank they are using their time to
build these miracle fireplace mantles.” Brilliant.
The bottom line for the customer is he gets a
great deal while doing a good turn for the Amish.!
Napier’s irresistible “Freemiums”!
The ad is loaded with reason why advertising
that struck a chord in 2009. “Many of these
(Amish) carpenters were building new homes
before the recession. Now with the housing

# !

While using a “freemium” to push the main
product is nothing new, Napier’s taken it to a new
level. One of the biggest drivers for sales of the
Heat Surge has been the frequent “free heater”
offer. It’s elegantly simple. Example: “Heat Surge
is rewarding eligible county readers who beat the
48-hour order deadline with a FREE Mini-Mate
companion fireplace when they claim the new
Accént EV.2 fireplace with Amish-crafted mantle
for just $349 plus shipping.” This is not to be
confused with the risk free trial offer, which was
the source of much mischief some years ago. As
the ad states, the respondent is getting the heater
for free but must pay $349 for the mantle.
Readership goes off the charts for with this free
offer headline because what bonafide prospect
would bail on an ad that offers a free heater?! !
This is a seductive marketing strategy that gave
Heat Surge an advantage over EdenPure for a
time — almost unthinkable. And Napier didn’t stop
with Amish fireplaces, he used the same strategy
in his World Reserve Monetary Exchange to pitch
coins and currency using a free safe as the lure:
“Surplus armored safes set for massive free
public giveaway.”! !
“7 smart places to stash your cash” was a
barnburner that saw a three year run in Forbes
that coincided with the recession and consumer
banking worries. Of course, #7 on this list was
“Buy a good safe.”



## Secrets of Regional Segmentation


# !

When Rodney Napier brought state and
regional segmentation based on zip codes to print
advertising, it was an incisive, new response
booster and an unexpected milestone in DR. Prior
to Napier there were no national direct response
advertisers using zip codes as a response
mechanism in print — this was something
traditionally associated with direct mail.!
Ben Suarez’s official carrier featuring
segmentation by state! !
Let’s take a look at a typical regional
segmentation headline: “State zip codes
determine who gets free Silver coins.” This flips
the sales equation on its ear. It’s no longer about
whether the reader wants the free Silver coins but
whether he qualifies. Now, when we add the sub-
head we get the sense this marketing approach is
night and day different from traditional DR: ! !
“Valuable U.S. Gov’t issued Silver coins are
actually being handed over to Nebraska residents
who find their zip code listed below, but only
residents who beat the deadline are getting the
bags of money filled with over 100 U.S. Gov’t
issued coins just by covering the Vault Bag fee
within the next two days.”! !
From “I’ll think about it.” to “Am I eligible?”! !
This has left the boundaries of traditional direct
response and has the feel of a public notice for a
limited offering. The first question this provokes in
the prospect is: “Am I even eligible for this offer?”
This fits in with the overall Napier sales strategy
of urgency and compliance. The prospect cannot
order willy-nilly — she’s given detailed instructions
in the CTA (Call To Action) including: what number
to call, when to begin calling, what to do if the
lines are busy and having the unique “claim code”
available when she calls.! !

# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !

State segmentation headline for ClearCast! !
Before Napier stumbled onto the big idea that
would turn the Amish into a surprise sales force,
he launched the response concept based on
frigid, cold and frost zones to push his new Heat
Surge devices. Respondents were given a
specific hotline to call based on which zone they
lived in and a specific time to begin calling. It’s not
just about control and compliance but the
perception that the order process has been
intentionally designed to handle a genuine, high
demand product and offer. While the zone
concept is still effective for national insertions,
state and city segmentation offers have led the
way since 2008.!
“Punishment and Reward”! !



## Secrets of Regional Segmentation

Segmentation has proven its response boosting
power for the coin and currency market,
memorabilia and hefty specialty goods such as
portable heaters, air conditioners and safes. The
CTA on the previous page is a textbook example
of punishment and reward using segmentation.
“Wisconsin Residents: Get U.S. State Silver Bar
free.” This is the carrot and it’s touted not just in
the headline but the body copy, captions and call
to action. This multi-readership path has worked
time and again to amp up response. And since
the offer is for State of Wisconsin Vault Bricks,
there is complete congruence in offer, premium
and product. When the offer is engineered this
well, paying the $285 for the set of five silver bars
seems almost like an afterthought, especially
when “non Wisconsin Residents must remit $134
per State Silver Bar.”! !
Segmentation is not merely a mechanism for
reward and punishment but offers a potent
synergy when combined with scarcity and/or
greed. Here’s an example: ! !
“MT zip codes determine who gets unsearched
bags of old U.S. Gov’t issued coins. Bags of
unsearched Gov’t issued coins are actually being
handed over to MT residents who find their zip
code listed below, but only those callers who beat
the 48 hour order deadline are getting the Vault
Bags loaded with rarely seen U.S. coins dating
back to the 1800s.”! !
The dream of getting a hold of a valuable Indian
Head penny or a Buffalo nickel in the “unsearched!
vault bag” is a powerful inducement that catapults
this offer beyond the normal numismatics market.!
So much so the toll free number is posted in the
subhead: “Listed below are the Billings area zip
codes that get to claim the Unsearched Vault
Bags. If you find the first two digits of your zip
code below immediately call: 1-888-745-2589.”! !
Knock-offs get it wrong most of the time! !
When a company runs thousands of full page
insertions over the years, there’s bound to be a
few knock-off artists. Anything is fair game for
them, including products, ads and offers.
Fortunately, they get it wrong more often than
it’s had multiple insertions, so the things they’ve
gotten right have kept them in business. !
they get it right. The ad on page 187 is a blatant
knock-off of product and offer for an infrared
heater. Because the copy is so tepid the ad is a
pale shadow of a Heat Surge ad. Nevertheless,

# !

Notably, the prominent mail order company,
Institute for Vibrant Living has given the Napier
format a few spins around the block, with fair to
middling success. Normally, it takes IVL 40 pages
of magalog copy to sell it’s “All Day Energy
Greens,” so it’s hard to pull off with a mere full
page, unlike a diet pill or joint health pill in which
the mechanism is easy for the copywriter to
explain and the reader to grasp. Moreover, when
the prospect thinks of a supplement she can pick
up at her local chain store or by a mail order
purchase, there’s not much reason for elaborate
CTAs and segmentation. Normally, a hotline and
(perhaps) a claim code are all the alt-health print
advertiser needs. This makes sense to the reader.! !

# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !


# !

! !
Social “proof” is not always believable! !
The Universal Physicians wrinkle cream ads
have had a handful of insertions. They do a good
mimic of a Napier ad but the absence of a real
CTA is telling. Moreover, if the phone lines are
really jammed, then an overflow hotline or two
would be all the more convincing. Little things like
this trip up amateur advertisers and telegraph this
to the prospect. Keyview Labs memory pill ad (pg.
184) is a reasonable homage from a very large
print advertiser. Finally, Julius Toth’s ad on pg.
181 is a textbook “Suarez-like” ad and no wonder
since Toth was instrumental in building EdenPure
to over a billion dollar division. It’s a stellar
example of how to expand a brand while pushing
retail and selling by mail order all on one page.!


THE NEW YORK TIMES, THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 12, 2009 N

By STEVEN KURUTZ

# F


# .,. ·

OR a people who make up less than a
tenth of a percent of the country's
population, the Amish loom large in
the American imagination as simple
living, God-fearing country folk who
keep the pre-industrial past alive. We
know them as farmers and skilled
craftspeople, with high standards and an unfussy
sensibility that they bring to the barns they raise,
the baked goods they sell at greenmarkets and the
quilts and furniture they produce.
But the Amish Jmve not traditionally been cel
ebrated for their space heaters. Which likely ex
plains the bewildered reactions provoked by ad
vertisements for a product called the Heat Surge
Roll-n-Glow Fireplace, which seem to have been
turning up everywhere in the last few months. Un
d~r a headline proclaiming the heater's power to
"help home heat bills hit rock bottom" - and de
picting it, in one version of the ad, as an "Amish
man's new miracle idea" - a photograph shows
what appear to be Amish men and women in a rus
tic workshop, making fireplace-style electric heat
er$ with artificial flames flickering inside. The ad
copy describes "soft-spoken Amish craftsmen"
w!io are "working their fingers to the bone" to cre
ate the wood mantels framing the fake fire, as their
"entire communities" strain to "keep up with de
mands."
A MIRACLE? The Heat Surge Roll-n-Glow Fireplace, left, is a small sensation, appearing on YouT ube and in infomercials and ads, right. A port
able h"'.ater in a wood frame, it wears its Amish pedigree proudly, though only half of it (the mantel) is Amish; the rest (the heater) is from China.
· And demand does not appear to be slowing, at
least according to Josette Holland, described in
some of the ads as a "home makeover expert to the
rich and famous." Not only are the heaters "the
best way to dress up every room," Ms. Holland is
quoted as saying, they are also "the latest home
decorating sensation."
·. The ads, which have been around for more
than a year but have been running with increasing
frequency this winter, have become something of a
sensation themselves, turning the Roll-n-Glow into
a next-wave Clapper. Appearing in publications as
varied as National Geographic, Popular Meehan-

## Hearth and home come


## together as fake flames warm


## the cold winter nights. Or not.

ics, Parade and The New York Times, and on local
TV stations across the country in the form of an
infomercial, they have given rise to much word-of
mouth speculation and online commentary.
Web sites and biogs have debated the merits
of the product and parsed the curious and some'
times byzantine wording of the ad copy, which de
scribes the heater as being given away for free,
though to get one customers must buy a $298 oak
mantel (cherry is $338; sold alone, the heater is
$249). The infomercial, meanwhile, has given rise
to a number of spoofs on YouTube.
The aspect of the ads that seems to have
stirred the most interest is the claims they malce
about the involvement of the Amish.
. In December 2007, soon after the ads first ap
peared, a reader of the product review site
alternativeconsumer.com suggested that there
was something absurd about the idea of an Amish
man inventing a "miracle" space heater, consider
ing that the Amish have traditionally been wary of
modern technology.
The reader's post (under the heading "Heat
Surge fireplace - what's up with that?") also
pointed out that close reading of the ads reveals
that the craftsmen make only the wood mantels;
the heater itself is actually "a work of engineering
genius from the China coast."
In more than 540 responses to that post that
have gone up in the months since, other readers
have argued about subjects like the willingness of
the Amish to use electric power (they "do not use
electricity in any forin," one wrote; they typically
avoid the grid but "will and do generate their own
electric," another corrected) and whether the peo
ple in the ads were really Amish (they don't "allow
themselves· to be photographed"; many do "allow
their picture to be taken:• depending on their sect).
Some objected to what they saw as the manu
facturer's exploitation of the Amish; others, like a
reader who posted last month, believe "the Amish
are the ones doing the exploiting" and "are getting
a good laugh out of this one.''
Opinion about other elements of the Roll-n
Glow's marketing strategy has also been divided.
"I just purchased one of these electric fireplaces
and I love it! I" a woman named Cindy wrote, add
ing th~.t~f!~e likes "the way the flame relaxes me."
Anoth~(woman, Darlene Swick, was less satisfied
and disputed claims that the Roll-n-Glow heater
could lower home energy costs; "My husband and
I purchased two of these Heat Surge fireplaces and
we have''Seen NO savings on our electric bill. We
turned our thermostat down to 68 degrees for the
month, and our electric bill went up.''
Lee Devlin, an engineering consultant in
Greeley, Colo., who maintains a blog that often cov
ers energy matters, said he was skeptical about
some of what the ads said, for example that the
Roll-n-Glow takes about the same amount energy
to operate as a coffee maker. Mr. Devlin said that
while the statement was accurate, it was an ap
ples-to-oranges comparison because a coffee mak
er runs for a brief period while a space heater is
often kept on for hours.
Jim Nanni, the manager of the appliance and
home improvement group of Consumer Reports,
said the first thing to remember when considering
a space heater is that regardless of the brand, elec
tric heat costs up to two and a half times more than
natural gas or heating oil. The Roll-n-Glow, which
earned a Good Housekeeping seal of approval in
November, is "essentially a portable electric heat
er:• Mr. Nanni said. "What you're looking at in
terms of the difference is aesthetics.''
As it turns out, this argument is basically the
one made by the people at Heat Surge. "If someone
would come to me and say, 'I need a heater and I
want to spend as little as possible,"' said David
Baker, vice president of the company, which is
based in Canton, Ohio, "I would say go to a local
big-box store and buy one for $29.99. Our heater
represents a fireplace rather than just some space
heater."
,;·;~~
Mr. Baker said the "miracle" that Heat Surge
advertisements so often refer to is the realism of
the fake flame. "It's important the flame doesn't
come off as cheesy:• he said. "Our fireplace has the
most realistic flame on the market.''
Seen in person, the Roll-n-Glow's flame does
have a mesmerizing realism, if not quite a camp
fire crackle, and the wood mantel feels substantial.
Still, Mr. Baker credits the Roll-n-Glow's suc
cess in large part to the association with the Amish
- a partne.rship that he said began in 2007 soon af
ter an executive at Arthur Middleton, Heat Surge's
parent company, befriended an Amish builder.
The company was looking to sell electric fire
places and decided it would be advantageous if
Amish worlcers made the mantels instead of for
eign ones. Or, as Mr. Baker put it: "It became clear
through test marketing the American population is
infatuated with - and understands the quality of
- an Amish product."
Holmes County, southwest of Canton, is home
.t o a large Amish community and known as a furni
ture-making hub. Several local workshops soon be
gan churning out the mantels, Mr. Baker said, in an
operation that - contrary to the image cultivated
by the Heat Surge ads, which show a horse-drawn
buggy hauling space heaters - is modern. "People
always ask, 'How can an Amish guy build an elec-
tric heater?'" Mr. Baker said. "Well, we do have
electricity in our shops.''
H.e was also quick to rebut the rumor, circulat
ing on several Web sites, that the Amish don't real
ly make the mantels, but are simply used as a mar
keting gimmick: "They are made by the Amish, ,
every one of them.''
Bob Gibbs, an Ohio state senator who toured a
Heat Surge workshop last year, said that he had
seen Amish craftspeople at work on the mantels,
though he had also seen non-Amish workers in oth
er areas of the factory.
But there have been more serious concerns.
Since 2007, the Better Business Bureau of Canton
has received 237 complaints against Heat Surge,
many of them related to misleading advertising
and customer service issues; the company cur
rently has an F rating from the bureau.
Mr. Baker said Heat Surge has since changed
the wording in its ads (the headline no longer
boasts of the Amish man's miracle idea, for exam
ple, and the bit about the coffee maker has been
modified). He also explained that many of the com-.
plaints were because the company didn't initially
have enough workers to meet the demand.
Amanda Pietze, a spokeswoman for the bu
reau, confirmed this. "They had a lot more re- "
sponse to their product than they were anticipat
ing," she said, adding that the number of com
plaints has decreased in recent months.
Perhaps the greatest evidence of the Roll-n
Glow's success is that it has spawned an imitation:
"Amish-style" space heaters are now available on '
the Web site electricfireplaces.com, starting at
$399. Mr. Baker said even he was somewhat sur
prised at the way the heater had captured the pub- ·'
lic's attention.
"I think a lot of it goes back to the irony of the ,
Amish and an electric fireplace," lie said. "How can .
that be? It's like skintight baggy pants.''
Does its success portend a flood of Amish
related home products? "The future of Heat Surge
will be dependent on new products and proposi
tions:• Mr. Bake!' said. "The Amish are bringing us
new ideas every day."

# What’s the deal with the Amish fireplace


# Are they really made by the Amish?

___________________________________________________________ _____ •. ____ «

# How a safe, portable fireplace made cheap, dangerous space heaters obsolete


# I f you’ve read a newspaper or magazine in the past few

years there’s no doubt you’ve heard of the world famous
Heat Surge Amish Fireplace that slashes heat bills, while
keeping everyone warm.
And you’ve probably wondered, what the heck do the Amish
have to do with electric fireplaces?
The Amish are known for their superior craftsmanship. And
people who can afford it insist on buying Amish built homes and
Amish crafted furniture because of the high quality and because
they’re known to last.
And if you thought you could never afford the luxury of Amish
made, keep reading, because we’ll tell today’s readers how to get
an extraordinary $149 Coupon for a brand spanking new 2013
model of the world famous Amish fireplace that makes any room
look like a million bucks.
But before we do, here’s a fascinating never-before-told story
straight from Heat Surge about the frugal Amish and how they
are saving folks big money on heating bills. Our journey begins in
our country’s heartland, a little town in Ohio.
In early 2006, John T. White, a fifty-seven year old farmer, coin
collector, writer and all around jack-of-all-trades, was asked by
his longtime friend to help solve a really big problem.
They just wouldn’t accept that it had to cost an arm and a leg to
heat a home safely to stay warm and comfortable. Though space
heaters have been around a long time, many are dangerous, out­
dated and down right ugly. So they set out to revolutionize the
way we heat our homes and make staying warm affordable.
It just so happened that Mr. White’s farm was near a large
Amish community. And over time, Mr. White became friends
with his Amish neighbors and discovered something. The Amish
were much more than just farmers and carpenters. They were in­
genious inventors, shroud in simple clothes.
One of the men that Mr. White had befriended just so happened
to be a master Amish craftsman who owned a successful wood­
working business.
Amish craftsmen, experienced in building homes, know that a
built-in fireplace can add over $7,000 to the value of a home.
Altogether they envisioned a modern fireplace, not a simple
space heater, but a fireplace for zone heating. One that was every
bit as beautiful and inviting as a fire burning in a hearth without
the hassle, mess, danger or smoke of a wood burning fireplace. A
fireplace that would be clean, safe, efficient, and beautiful. The
Amish craftsman dreamt up a real wood mantle and hearth to
create the fireplace, making each one a lasting piece of heirloom
furniture.
Initially, the Amish craftsman set out to make just one, a pro­
totype. Though Mr. White wanted all the fireplace mantles to be
Amish made, he thought the Amish could never keep up with ac-
---------------------------------------- tual production work.
Fact: Amish communities are self-
sufficient. That often means off
the electrical grid. But with fuel
powered generators, electricity
can be used sparingly for work.
Fact: While Amish do not like
the vanity of photography, they
understand the educational
value it can provide to outsid­
ers interested in their work.
But the stars must have
been aligned. Because of the
economic down turn in the
housing market, hundreds of
Amish craftsmen were left
with little work. This meant
the entire wood working
of the mantles and hearths could actually be built right here in
America’s heart land.
So it was settled. The Amish would hand build all the mantles,
and over the next couple of months they put nearly everything on
the line to get it done. While the Amish were procuring the lum­
ber and assembling their community of wood-working craftsmen,
Heat Surge worked with teams of engineers from across the Pa­
cific to create the heart of the fireplace, the micro-furnace and
thermal heat exchanger.
Mr. White and his friend obsessed over every detail, insisting
the flames needed to flicker just right and the hand rubbed finish
needed to be 100% even, top to bottom. Over time seven new pat­
ents have been awarded by the U.S. Patent Office. What they did
was create a product that capti­
vated people; something that no
one in the room would be able to
take their eyes off of.
The Amish then equipped each
one with special casters, giv-
_______________________ ing the fireplace its ‘Roll-n-Glow’
name because it can easily roll
from room to room; something that had never been done before.
There was no question that they had made the finest heating
appliance ever seen.
In July 2006, the very first advertising announcement featur­
ing this Roll-n-Glow Fireplace ran in the Kansas City daily news­
paper. Even though it was the dead of summer and over 90° that
day, it was the phone lines at Heat Surge® in Ohio that were on
fire and they knew everything was about to change.
By 2007, thousands of people were calling in every day. Calls
had to be routed all over the country and Amish craftsmen in two
separate communities were working their fingers to the bone just
to keep up.
There’s no doubt that Mr. White and his friend had a hit of
a lifetime with the Heat Surge Amish fireplace. Tragically,
Mr. White saw only a glimpse of what Heat Surge would become.
In October 2007 a massive heart attack took his youthful life, but
he never had the chance to see how the idea had become the sav­
ing grace for consumer heat bills.
And now, with nearly 2 million Heat Surge Roll-n-Glow®
Fireplaces in homes worldwide, it’s a smashing success for
consumers.
Stores all over the country could only dream of having the
world-famous Heat Surge Amish Fireplace but, of all the national
retailers only Sears was trusted enough to get it.
“I’d reckon Sears is the oldest and
most trusted in the country,” said
Melvin Byler, an Amish craftsman who

# Sears

builds for Heat Surge.
Now, other stores are pass­
ing off fake Amish ‘looking’
heaters. But they’re not the
real deal and they are actually
hurting the Amish reputation.
Melvin, who is proud of
his work, doesn’t like it when
others are dishonest. Here’s
what he had to say, “No one
should accept an imitation. If
it doesn’t have the Heat Surge
name on it, it’s not real Amish.
I repeat, if it doesn’t have the
Heat Surge name on it, they
are making a fool out of you.”
He’s talking about the cheap
imitations that are made to
look like Heat Surge but are
being peddled at Lowes®,
Home Depot®, Costco®, and
Walmart®. Those fakes use
cheap particle board, they’re
not all real wood and they’re
not Amish built.
According to the avalanche
of consumer reviews, people
absolutely swear by their real
Heat Surge Amish fireplaces,
repeatedly saying, “...looks ab­
solutely beautiful,” “...it’s sav­
ing me money," and “...keeps
me warm head to toe, floor to
ceiling.”
And everyone knows it’s
safe. Infact Heat Surge recent­
ly received an “Excellent Rat­
ing” in Surface Temperature and Fire Safety tests because it is
actually safe to the touch.
Knowing how much people love their Amish fireplaces, Heat
Surge had to approach the revolutionary redesign for 2013 with
great care. Not only have we made them slimmer and dare we say
sexier, but we made every
feature people love about
them even better. It’s the sec­
ond evolution and that’s why
they are called the EV-2’s.
The EV-2’s are the only
low-cost heating appliances
with Hybrid-Thermic™ heat
technology. No other heater
has it.
And the new 2013 EV-2
model line up is making a big
splash.
The Roll-n-Glow is the
original Amish fireplace that
started it all. This year it’s

## Roll-n-Glow

(shown in dark oak)
been completely modernized 29 w x 27"H x 9 5"D
with every new EV-2 feature

# and Storage Hearth

including a huge list of up- 33.5-w x n"H x 12"D
grades: the Hybrid-Thermic heating system, new Amish hand­
made real wood mantle, new Hi-Def Fireless Flame® technology
with multi-colored LEDs, and the
new authentic sound of crackling
firewood. Plus it’s thinner and light­
er than ever before, so it’s even eas­
ier to roll from room to room. Pic­
tured with the Roll-n-Glow is the
new Storage Hearth, which not only
puts the Roll-n-Glow up on a pedes­
tal but also provides an enormous
drawer for blankets, shoes or even
DVD movies.
The Portrait EV-2 also has all
these new features but its full sur­
round wood frame makes it mount to any wall, easier than even a
flat screen TV.
The Accent EV-2 is about the size of a night stand or end table
so it fits anywhere. But it packs the .
same EV-2 Hybrid-Thermic heating
technology, and provides the same
amount of heat as its big brother.
The Roommate EV-2 is the first and
only Heat Surge model that features
just a hint of the glowing flames, but,
outputs the same amount of bone
soothing heat as its siblings because
it uses the same signature Hybrid-
Thermic heating technology.
Of course, the wood-working on
all of these new EV-2 models are
crafted by the Amish using nothing
but real American wood.
And they all save you a lot money the same way. “Folks all over
the country are zone heating and saving big money. They just set
their home thermostat to 59° - and
bam! - the Heat Surge maintains a
comfortable 74° room temperature
all winter long, no matter what’s
going on outside. And the best part
is it can run for a full 12-hour day
or 12-hour night for just about a
buck,” said Dave O’Neil, the analyst

# How Hybrid-Thermic™ heat works

This is the revolutionary Hybrid-Thermic miracle heater
found on board all 2013 Heat Surge EV-2 Amish
fireplace models. It’s an engineering genius so
advanced, it actually uses a micro-furnace
from the tech savvy valley of Shenzhen,
China and a thermal heat exchanger to per­
form its miracles. This modern marvel uses
just a trickle of electricity and saves you
money based on the U.S. average that says
it uses only about 9tf of electricity an hour on
the standard setting, yet it produces up to
an amazing 4,606 BTUs on the high setting,
providing bone soothing heat everywhere.
02012 HS P6195A OF18558R-1

# Heating accounts for 63% of your bills

According to the U.S. Department of Energy, heating accounts for 63%
of all your utility bills including 18% for water heating and a whopping
45% for space heating. That amounts to big money. But folks using the
Hybrid-Thermic heat technology found
only in Heat Surge Amish fireplaces
are zone heating and saving a boat load
of money on their heating bills. That's
because you get 74s of bone-soothing
room heat even when the home thermo­
stat is turned down to 59e. The Heat
Surge has earned the coveted Under­
writers Labs © certification and is protected by a full one year limited
warranty and 30-day money back satisfaction guarantee.

# 4



(UMS) – Everyone hates high
heat bills. But we’re all sick and
tired of turning the thermostat
down and freezing our buns off.
That’s why Sears knew they
had a home-run on their hands
with the first-ever low-cost appli-
ance with Hybrid-Thermic™ heat
technology. And no other heater
anywhere has it.
The brand-new portable L.E.D.
Heat Surge HT sips so little
energy, you can run it for a full
12-hour day or night for just about
a buck.
This modern marvel, hailed
as the zone heating ‘Miracle
Heater,’ caused such a frenzy at
Sears stores, one shopper refused
to leave until she got one. Since
there were none in stock, store
managers were forced to hand
over the store’s only floor model,
against store policy.
So today, immediate action is
being taken to give more people,
more ways to get them.
Beginning at 8:30 a.m., today’s
readers are allowed to phone in or
snag one online. Heat Surge even
posted a 2-Day Double Coupon
which has been reprinted on this
page for today’s readers to use.
By using this coupon, everyone
who calls is being rewarded with
$227. That makes this remark-
able new home appliance a real
steal at just $299. Since this is a
Double Coupon Deal, it not only
gives you an extraordinary dis-
count, but also entitles you to free
shipping and handling, totaling
$227 off. So now everyone has a
fair shot at getting one.
“Folks are saving money every-
where by zone heating with the
new Heat Surge HT,” said Kris
Rumel, the company’s analyst
tracking the impact Heat Surge is
having on consumer heat bills.
This all started when Sears
wanted the world-famous ‘Amish
Miracle Heater’ that everyone was
asking for.
The shy but now famous Amish
craftsman said Sears made the
most sense. “I’d reckon they’re
the oldest and most trusted in the
country,” one of the Amish crafts-
men known as Melvin said.
“We’ve been saving folks
money, big money. And we know
it because we’re hearin’ about it,”
he said.
“They know about our qual-
ity. No particle board, just real
wood. Fully-assembled cabinets
by our hands and we’re making
them right here in the good ole
USA,” Melvin said.
Director of Technology David
Martin explained, “The Heat
Surge HT is a revolutionary
appliance that can easily roll from
room to room. But we didn’t want
it to look like some metal box that
just sits there. So we turned to
our Amish craftsmen and now
each one is made to look like a
sleek, slim fireplace that has no
real flames.”
“The peaceful flicker of the
‘Fireless Flame’ is so beautiful,
everyone thinks it’s real, but it’s
totally safe to the touch. All you do
is just plug it in,” Martin said.
People from Maine to Missis-
sippi and even Florida and Ari-
zona are flocking to get them
because they are finally able to
give their central heat a rest dur-
ing this long, frigid winter.
According to the avalanche of
consumer reviews, people absolute-
ly swear by them, repeatedly say-
ing, “it saves money,” “looks beauti-
ful,” and “keeps you warm head to
toe, floor to ceiling.” And the word
is getting out. That’s why people
are clamoring to get them.
But Martin said right now the
real problem is making sure the
Amish craftsmen can keep up with
the lingering winter rush.
That’s why the Double Coupon
expires in two days.
So for readers hoping to get the
new Heat Surge for themselves
and take care of gifts for others,
there is good news.
You can use the 2-Day Double
Coupon more than once. But there
is a catch. You can only get away
with it for the next two days from
the date of today’s publication by
calling the National Appliance
Center at 1-800-618-8510.
Then, when it arrives, you’ll be
rushing to turn down that ther-
mostat. Just plug it in, watch
your heat bills hit rock bottom,
and never be cold again. (cid:78)

MODERN MARVEL:
The revolutionary
Heat Surge HT micro-furnace fits in any room
because it’s packed inside a small Amish-built
cabinet that measures just 25" high, 18" wide,
and 12" deep.
This is the revolutionary Heat Surge HT, the first-ever appliance with Hybrid-Thermic™
heat technology. Hybrid-Thermic heat technology is an engineering genius so ad-
vanced, it actually uses a micro-furnace from the Coast of China and a thermal heat
exchanger to perform its miracles. The thermal heat exchanger acts like the rays of
the sun to heat you, the kids, the pets and everything else. The micro-furnace then
heats all the surrounding air. Together, this Hybrid-Thermic heat technology warms
both you and the air around you, taking care of all the cold spots. In fact, it actually
produces bone-soothing heat to help you feel good.
This modern marvel uses L.E.D. technology and just a trickle of electricity and saves
you money based on a U.S. average that says it uses only about 9¢ of electricity an hour
on the standard setting, yet it produces up to an amazing 4,606 British Thermal Units
(BTU’s) on the high setting. But here’s the big surprise. It’s not just a metal box that
belongs in a basement. The Heat Surge HT is a showpiece in any room. That’s because
it has the ambiance
of a real fireplace,
but it has no real
flames. Its Fireless
Flame® technology
makes it safe to
the touch.
The portable
Heat Surge HT
comes installed in
a genuine Amish-
built wood cabinet
made in the heart-
land of Ohio. They
are hand-rubbed,
stained, and var-
nished. When it
arrives, all you do
is just plug it in.

technology safely heats a room fast-
er and more effectively than typical

new proprietary Convecta-red Heat™

test rays of the light spectrum with a
convection wave that when working
together produce ultra-efficient heat.

ceived certification of Underwriters

■฀RARELY SEEN U.S. COINS LEAVE VAULT:
These packages containing Vault Bags loaded with valuable Indian Head
coins issued by the U.S. Gov’t nearly 100 years ago are being moved from the private vaults of the World Reserve for immedi-
ate distribution to Oklahoma residents.

## Vault Bags loaded with rarely seen U.S. Gov’t issued coins are actually being handed over to Oklahoma residents who find their county listed


## below, but only those who beat the 48 hour deadline are getting the Vault Bags of nearly 100 year old Gov’t issued coins for just $ 59

OKLAHOMA - The phones are ring-
ing off the hook.
That’s because for the next 48 hours
Vault Bags loaded with rarely seen In-
dian Head coins issued by the U.S.
Gov’t nearly 100 years ago are being re-
leased to Oklahoma residents who find
their county highlighted in black on the
Distribution Map printed in today’s
publication.
Everyone is rushing to get the Vault
Bags because each one is loaded with
nearly a quarter pound of rarely seen
Indian Head coins dating all the way
back to the early 1900s.
“It’s hard to tell how much these
heavy Vault Bags could be worth some-
day. That’s because after they were filled
with U.S. Gov’t issued coins, the bags
were sealed for good,” said Timothy J.
Shissler, Director of Vault Operations
for the private World Reserve.
Since this advertising announcement
can’t stop collectors from hoarding all
the rarely seen coins they can get, the
World Reserve has imposed a strict
limit of 10 Vault Bags per resident.
That’s why it’s important that resi-
dents call the National Distribution Hot-
lines beginning at precisely 8:30am this
morning.
Everyone who does is getting the
Vault Bags loaded with rarely seen
coins issued by the U.S. Gov’t nearly
100 years ago for just $ 59 and shipping
and handling, which is a real steal since
just one scarce and highly collectible In-
dian Head coin alone could be worth up
to hundreds of dollars in collector value.
And here’s the best part. Everyone who
claims four Vault Bags before all the
money is gone is getting free shipping
and free handling.
“We’re bracing for all the calls be-

# U.S. Gov’t Issued coins go to residents in 74 OK counties

VALUABLE: ISSUED BY THE U.S. GOV’T RARELY SEEN:
NEARLY 100 YEARS AGO YEAR VARIES 1913-1938
■ U.S. GOV’T ISSUED COINS: These bags are loaded with rarely seen Indian Head coins
that everyone is rushing to get for just $ 59. That’s because everyone who beats the 48-hour
deadline to claim four bags is getting free shipping and free handling.
Who gets the Vault Bags: Shown below
are the Oklahoma counties that get the Vault
Bags. If you live in one of the counties high-
lighted in black immediately call the National
Distribution Hotline at: 1-866-211-7348
How much are the Vault Bags worth:
Coin values always fluctuate and there are
never any guarantees, but here’s why resi-
dents are rushing to claim the Vault Bags
before they’re all gone. After the Vault Bags
were loaded with rarely seen coins issued
by the U.S. Gov’t they were sealed for good.
That means there’s no telling what you’ll find
until you sort through all the coins. So you
better believe at just $ 59 and shipping the
Vault Bag fee is a real steal, since just one
scarce and highly collectible Indian Head
coin alone could be worth up to hundreds
of dollars in collector value.
Why is the Vault Bag fee so low: Because
thousands of U.S. residents have missed
the deadline to claim the money, the World
Reserve has re-allocated Vault Bags that
will be scheduled to be sent out in the next
48 hours. That means the money is up for
grabs and now any resident who finds their
county highlighted in black on the Distribu-
tion Map printed in today’s publication gets
to claim the Vault Bags of money for them-
selves and keep all the U.S. Gov’t issued
coins found inside. Each Vault Bag load-
ed with nearly a quarter pound of rarely
seen Indian Head coins is set at $ 78 for
residents who miss the deadline, but for
those who beat the 48-hour deadline the
Vault Bag fee is just $ 59 and the best part is
everyone who claims four Vault Bags before
all the money is gone is getting free ship-
ping and free handling as long as they call
the National Distribution Hotline before the
deadline ends.
cause there are just hours left for res-
idents to get the Vault Bags,” Shissler
said.
So, Oklahoma residents who find
their county highlighted in black on
the Distribution Map below need to im-
mediately call the National Distribu-
tion Hotlines before the 48-hour dead-
line ends to get the Vault Bags. If lines
are busy keep trying, all calls will be
answered. ■

# How to get the bags of U.S. Gov’t issued coins

Shown to the left in black are the
Oklahoma counties that get the
Vault Bags. If you live in one of the
counties highlighted in black imme-
diately call the National Distribution
Hotline before the 48-hour deadline
ends at: 1-866-211-7348

■ ON THEIR WAY: New Heat Surge Mini-Glo Efficiency Heaters will soon be delivered to lucky Tulsa area residents who beat the 48-hour order deadline and live in one of the zip
code areas listed in today’s publication. Everyone’s getting the Upstairs-Downstairs Efficiency Combo of two Heat Surge Mini-Glo Efficiency Heaters because they only use pen-
nies an hour of electricity to drastically slash home heat bills.

# New heaters go to Oklahoma residents by zip code


# Brand new heaters are being delivered to all Tulsa area residents who find the first 2 digits of their zip code listed below and


# call before the 48 hour order deadline ends to get the new Mini-Glo Heaters to drastically slash home heat bills

OKLAHOMA – The Toll Free Hot-
lines are ringing off the hook.
That’s because Tulsa area residents
who find the first two digits of their zip
code listed in today’s publication are
getting new heaters that quickly put a
stop to high heat bills forever.
Tulsa area residents who get the
new Heat Surge® Mini-Glo Efficiency
Heaters by calling the Toll Free Hot-
lines before the 48-hour order deadline
ends will never have to pay high heat
bills again.
“We all know that freezing cold
weather is setting in and heat bills are
going to skyrocket this winter. That’s
why I’ve given the directive to give the
first Tulsa area residents who call in
the next 48 hours a massive discount
on our brand new Mini-Glo Efficiency
Heaters,” said David Martin, General
Manager of U.S. based Heat Surge.
And here’s the best part. Everyone
who calls the Toll Free Hotline is get-
ting a massive discount allowing them
to claim the new heaters for just $ 149
and shipping and handling as long as
they call before the 48-hour deadline
ends.
Demand for Heat Surge heaters has
skyrocketed to stardom ever since con-
sumer reviews surfaced showing just
how much money ultra-efficient zone
heat saves people on heat bills which
is why this announcement is being so
widely advertised.
According to the avalanche of con-
sumer reviews for the original Heat
Surge heaters, people absolutely swear
by them, repeatedly saying, “it saves
money,” “looks beautiful,” and “keeps
you warm and cozy.” That’s why Tul-
sa area residents will be scrambling to
get them starting at precisely 8:30am
this morning.
In fact, it gives you 74° of bone-
soothing room heat even when the
home thermostat is turned down to
59°. That means you can zone heat and
never be cold again. And since it only
uses about the same energy per hour
as a coffee maker you’ll save a ton on
your heat bills, too.
“We’re bracing for all the calls and
to make sure everyone gets their new
Heat Surge Mini-Glo Efficiency Heater
before the harsh winter weather hits,
UPS® drivers have been instructed to
make home deliveries anywhere in the
United States,” Martin said.
“Just remember, to get your new
heater you must find the first two dig-
its of your zip code listed in today’s
publication and be one of the first Tul-
sa area readers to call the Toll Free
Hotline at 1-888-414-3591 before the
48-hour deadline ends,” he said. ■
This is the brand new Heat Surge Mini-Glo Efficiency Heater. This modern marvel is being
called a miracle because its micro design easily goes from room to room and uses just a
trickle of electricity so you can turn down your thermostat and use its ultra-efficient zone
heat to save a ton of money on heat bills based on a U.S. average that says it uses only
pennies of electricity an hour, yet it produces up to an amazing 3,685 British Thermal Units
(BTU’s) delivering a full 1,200 watts on the
standard setting. The Heat Surge Mini-Glo
is a showpiece in any room. That’s because
it has the ambiance of a real fireplace, but
it has no real flames. Its Fireless Flame ®
technology makes it safe to the touch.
At just 13.44"W x 9.43"H x 6.63"D and
weighing only 12lbs, the new Heat Surge
Mini-Glo Efficiency Heater is ultra portable
and comes fully assembled so when it
arrives, all you do is just plug it in.

Listed below are the Tulsa area zip codes that get the new heaters. If you find the
first two digits of your zip code listed below immediately call the Toll Free Hotline at: 1-888-414-3591
