# New sweeper purifies air in your


# iiome wliiie sweeping your fioors


# The only robotic sweeper with a UV chamber that kills


# germs and sanitizes and freshens the air in your home


# Robot Does All The Work While You Read, Relax Or Play

By John Whitter, Media Services
Now you can let the new
innovative BioTech Robotic
Sweeper clean your floors
while you take a nap, read
or relax.
The amazing BioTech
Robotic Sweeper will clean all
your floors including ceramic
tile, hardwood, vinyl and most
carpeting while it purifies the
air in your home.
Countless years of research
and technology have been com-
bined to create this fantastic
sweeper that is unlike anything
else on the market today
This revolutionary auto-
matic sweeper utilizes a high
speed dirt extraction brush on
the bottom, a side brush for
cleaning along walls and cor-
ners, an ultra-violet light to
help with disinfection, and it
even has a compartment for
the option of adding your
favorite fragrance to the air as
it cleans.
And, the BioTech Robotic
Sweeper never needs bags
thus saving you money. A
handy dust bin holds the
dust, dirt and debris until you
empty it.
With its unique compact
shape, the BioTech Robotic
Sweeper easily cleans under
beds, tables and other furni-
ture where standard, upright
vacuums cannot reach.
Once the unit is turned
on, it automatically follows a
five-step repetitive movement
to ensure that your entire
room is completely cleaned.
You can also very easily and
quickly select a specific
cleaning pattern that fits your
needs because the BioTech
Robotic Sweeper comes with
a remote control.
The BioTech Robotic
Sweeper, has the added ben-
efit of internal photo sensors
that prevent it from falling
down stairs or any other drop
off areas.
When your designated
cleaning time is completed,
your BioTech Robotic Sweeper
will automatically move to a
lighter area of the room before
it stops. This prevents the unit
from stopping under any of
your furniture and makes it
easy to find.
The BioTech Robotic
Sweeper is being called the
"Ingenious Sweeper."
It actually has a pro-
grammed "brain" that uses its
bumper sensor and its infrared
sensor to detect furniture and
other objects. With the patent-
ed revolutionary bumper your
fine wood furniture will aiwaj^s
be protected.
This self-propelled, self-
navigating Robotic Sweeper
will clean your floors while
you do other, more exciting
things with your time.
The BioTech Robotic
Sweeper can go from hard
flooring to carpeting effort-
lessly and when it has fin-
ished the timed setting, it
automatically turns itself off.
Or you may choose to use
the remote control to turn it
on and off or direct it to a cer-
tain area you want cleaned.
Unlike some convention-
al sweepers, the revolution-
ary filtered dust receptacle in
the BioTech Robotic Sweeper
collects and keeps pet hair,
dust and other debris inside
the chamber instead of circu-
lating it back into the air.
Then all of the remaining
exhaust air from the Robotic
Sweeper passes through a
UV (ultraviolet) chamber that
kills germs and purifies and
freshens the air as it is
released back into your home,
tviost conventional sweepers
release exhaust air that is
filled with dust back into your
home. That is why cleani/ig
experts recommend dusting
your furniture after you
sweep. But this is not neces-
sary with the BioTech Robotic
Sweeper due to the efficient
UV purifying chamber.
It has been said that by the
year 2020 every home will have
at least one robot. But, you
don't have to wail until 2020.
You can own the BioTech
Robotic Sweeper that actually
helps you with your housework
today at a major discount.
One of the lead develop-
ers of the Robotic Sweeper for
BioTech Research is Julius
Toth. fvlr.Toth is one of the
nation's prominent experts on
cleaning, sanitizing and air
purification. He has pioneered
many advanced healthy home
technologies and has authored
many cleaning and sanitation
publications and is known as
"the Cleaning Professor." A
recent interview with Mr. Toth
revealed the following:
Q. How does the BioTech
Robotic Sweeper purify the
air while sweeping fioors?
A. Unlike many conven-
tional sweepers, all of the
exhaust air from the Robotic
Sweeper passes through a
UV (ultraviolet) chamber that
kills germs and purifies and
freshens the air you breathe.
A major advantage of our
Robotic Sweeper is that it trav-
els throughout the entire room
purifying the air you breathe.
Unlike room air cleaners that
only clean the air directly
around them, it purifies the air
everywhere it travels saturating
your rooms with clean air. And
everyone knows clean air
helps with allergies.
Q. Can you use the
BioTech Robotic Sweeper
on tiie and wood floors?
A. Most definitely. In fact,
this remarkable robotic sweep-
er is fantastic for cleaning all of
your tile, wood and vinyl floor-
ing. And it's so easy.
You can virtually take a
nap while it cleans for up to 1
hour and 20 minutes. Then just
recharge it and the Robotic
Sweeper is ready when you
need it to tackle the next job.
And the BioTech Robotic
Sweeper will not scratch or
harm your expensive hard-
wood or tile flooring.
Q. is the BioTech Robot-
ic Sweeper safe to use
around children and pets?
A. Yes. The BioTech
Robotic Sweeper is designed
with a series of safety meas-
ures. When if is picked up off
the floor or should it get stuck
on a toy, it will stop cleaning
and start beeping. Of course
adult supervision is recom-
mended with all appliances
and you don't have to chase
this Robotic Sweeper around
the room to turn it off. Just
use the handy remote.
Q. Is there anything
extra to purchase in the
future for maintenance of
this Robotic Sweeper?
A. No, there really is no
need to purchase additional
maintenance products. The
BioTech Robotic Sweeper is
very easy to maintain.
There are no bags to
replace and a Hand Brush is
Included to brush any dirt from
the filter. Plus, the filter and
dust bin can be rinsed with
water if needed, A handy illus-
tration booklet shows you step-
by-step how .easy it really is to
care for this handy robot.
Q. What types of carpet-
ing can be cleaned with the
BioTech Robotic Sweeper?
A. This Robotic Sweeper
will work on almost all indoor
and outdoor dry carpeting. It
is not recommended for use
on long fiber carpeting such
as shag carpeting or on any
wet surfaces.
End of Interview.
Today, BioTech Research
is offering the Robotic Sweep-
er to readers at a major
authorized discount. The
BioTech Robotic Sweeper retails
for $279. But readers are author-

A truly unique aspect

never have to set the clock.

for you when you plug it in
and even adjusts for Day-
light Savings Time.

ture is the dual battery
back-up. If the power goes

You will never be late
again!
And you can listen to

lection on your Wedge.

ing technology, I spoke to

man.

the Wedge Sound System
from the ground up. All costs

produce such lifelike sounds?
A. Our special engi-
neering team has developed a
revolutionary design enabling
the Wedge to fill a room with
crystal clear sound.
The Wedge has the
perfect balance of power
flowing to its speakers so the
sound is crystal clear with
the volume on low or high.
Q. How easy is it to
set up the Wedge Sound
System?
A. The Wedge is high-
ly advanced but is a super
easy-to-use sound system.
All the fine tuning has been
done for the consumer so all

and enjoy the Wedge’s crys-
tal clear sound.
No figuring out where
to place multiple speakers
and no confusing wires to
hook up.

By John Whitehead, Media Services
(Canton, OH) People across
the nation are lining up to
get the advanced additive
Mileage Spikewhich sci-
entific tests show increases
fuel mileage over 29%.
Some scientific tests on
f\/1ileage Spike'" have shown
increases as high as 46.9%
and an increase in the num-
ber of additional miles per
tankful of 302 more miles.
These scientific tests
show tvlileage Spike " can
provide you a free tankful of
gas for nearly every third
fill-up.
fvlileage Spike'" also in-
creases horsepower by 10%
to 21% and increases oil
pressure dramatically. It cuts
engine wear and makes en-
gines run cooler by 15 to 20
degrees. It doubles the life of
an engine and reduces
maintenance costs.
tvlileage Spike " is a product
of BioTech Research", one of
the nation's most innovative
companies.
For more information on
the sensational new prod-
uct, tvlileage Spike'", here
is my interview with John
Whitakre, Chief of Re-
search and Development
for BioTech Research".
Q. What does a person
have to do to use Mileage
Spike" to get these sen-
sationai results of over
29% mora fuel mileage?
A. You simply add it to
your oil. You add 6 oz. to 8
oz. the first time. Then,
each oil change after that,
you add a small amount for
maintenance.
You can add Mileage
Spike " yourself by pouring
it into your oil fill spout on
your engine or taking it to
whatever service changes
your oil and have them add
it to your oil when they
change oil in your vehicle.
You can never change oil
too often. So the easiest
thing to do would be take it
to a quick lube shop, have
your oil changed and give
them Mileage Spike'" to
add to your oil.
Q. Is Mileage Spike" an-
other kind of oil treatment?
A. No. Mileage Spike'" is
not an oil treatment or a lu-
bricant. It is a highly ad-
vanced metal treatment.
Mileage Spike'" penetrates 3
to 5 microns into the surface
area of the moving parts of
your engine to treat the
metal. This treatment re-
duces friction in your engine
much better than oil or any
other oil additive of the past.
Also, unlike many other oil
treatments. Mileage Spike'"
is totally safe to use for your
engine. It does not contain
petroleum distillates, Teflon-,
graphite or any type of film or
coating like many other addi-
tives that can build up on en-^
gine parts and change toler-
ances and clog oil lines.
Q. Is Mileage Spike"
safe to use?
A. It is totally safe to
use. Mileage Spike™ is
made from a very advanced
derivative of a certain veg-
etable oil. Therefore, it can-
not harm anything.
Q. Will Mileage Spike"
void vehicle warranties?
A. Absolutely not. It is to-
tally safe to use in an engine.
Not only will it not härm an
engine, but it brings great
benefits to an engine.
Q. Why does Mileage
Spike" increase mileage
and horsepower so much?
A. When you reduce fric-
tion in an engine, it then re-
quires less power from
gasoline and oxygen to turn
the engine and generate
horsepower. Less friction
Mileage Spike" is a product of Btólik4l R^earch^ one of
ttw natic^'s most Innovative ciinipanles.

# How it works:

1. You simply add
Mileage Spike'" to
your oil.
2. Mileage Spike'
ttien penetrates 3 to
5 microns into all
the moving parts of
your engine.
3. Mileage Spike'"
tlien reduces
friction in your en-
gine by 400%.

4. The reduced friction re-
quires less power to turn
your engine's moving parts
and requires less fuel to

5. The reduced friction cools
your engine by 15% to 20%
which brings in cooler air
which has more oxygen
which provides more power
with less fuel.
6. Your vehicle now gets over
29% better fuel mileage and
has 10% to 21 % more horse-
power.
7. Your engine now runs
quiet and bums less oil. Your
engine life is now doubled.

## MttfAGE SPIKF" RESULTS INCREASE WITH TIME

also reduces engine wear,
reduces maintenance costs
and makes the engine run
cooler. Cooler engines also
get better gas mileage be-
cause the air that mixes with
gas is denser, has more
oxygen and therefore cre-
ates more power with less
gas. The longer Mileage
Spike'" is in your running en-
gine, the gas mileage and
other benefits increase.
Q. How much does
Mileage Spike" reduce
friction?
A. In tests on friction
testing machines. Mileage
Spike™ reduced friction over
400% better than the best
engine oil on the market.
Mileage Spike™ beats all
engine oils and additives.
Some of the friction tests
are pretty sensational.
The best test of engine
friction reduction is a piece
of equipment called the
Bearing Break Machine.
The Bearing Break Ma-
chine is like a motor that
you turn the crank to get it
going. The more friction in
the bearing break machine,
the easier it is to stop it
from turning. That is be-
cause the friction is helping
you stop the machine from
turning. The less friction in
the Bearing Break Machine,
the harder it is to stop it
from turning.
In one friction test using
the Bearing Break Machine,
only two pounds of weights
were needed to stop the
machine from turning due
to friction using the best
motor oil. After we added
Mileage Spike'", we would
have men who weighed
over 200 pounds stand on
the machine and they could
not stop it from turning.
Q. hlow much can
Mileage Spike" cut en-
gine wear?
A. Based on our scientif-
ic tests, we can safely say
th&t Miieage Spike'" will ex:
tend the life of an engine at
least 2 times and probably
a lot more.
Q. How much will
Mileage Spike™ increase
gas mileage in a vehicle
as shown in your scientif-
ic tests?
A. Our scientific tests
show an average increase
of 29.5% increase in gas
mileage for both city and
highway drivirig combined.
In general it aippears that
the biggest gains come
from city driving because
city driving puts a great
deal more emphasis on en-
gine friction with stops and
starts to overcome inertia.
Also, city driving causes en-
gines to run hotter. As men-
tioned, Mileage Spike'"
greatly reduces friction and
greatly reduces engine
temperature. Also, it ap-
pears that the biggest gas
guzzlers like SUV's get the
biggest increases. This is
also because large engines

minutes-plentytimetomow
uptohalfanacre.
The G-tech is an easy-

Unlike the rotary mower you

which rips the grass blades
from the side, a cylinder
mower evenly cuts the grass
straight across the top. This

You may remember see-
ingorevenusingtheoldreel
push mowers of yesteryear.
They were widely used
before the gas-powered

use and convenience pre-
vailedandtheold,heavyreel
mowersendedupintheback
ofshedsoratgaragesales.
And that was truly unfor-
tunate because the reel, or
cylinder mowers are by far
superiortoanyrotarymower.
The horizontally mounted
blades capture and cut the
grass in its natural upright
state, shearing the grass
rather than ripping it. It cuts
like scissors, steel to steel,
creatingamanicuredlookfor
the best looking lawn in the

Still to this day, cylinder
mowers are used at profes-
sional and local golf courses
tokeeptheirvastamountsof

great.
Ifyoucomparethecutof
a cylinder mower and rotary

at home with a store bought
clipperkit.

You never have to prime

dangerouscarbonmonoxide.

healthiestpeople.

ers, and have no gas smell.

age.

ping has announced the release

, back guarantee and a one-year

Combined with the $122

the new Energy Mi/er® is sure
.to be the best investment of the
year.
If you've ever won ied about
Vour monthly budget, looked
for ways to save money or sim­
ply w atched your electric meter
spin uncontrollably, the Ener­
gyMizer® is for you.
The EnergyMizer® is for
every homeowner, be it a
house, a condo or a mobile
home. The EnergyMizer® is
great for renters loo - because
ft is completely portable and
can move from location to lo­
cation.
installation of the Encr-
gyMizer* is as simple as plug­
ging in two book-sized units in
your home. I here is no need
tq hire an expensive electrician.
The deadline to claim your
EnergyMizeG has been set and
- is quickly approaching. It is
. imperative that you respond to­
day to claim your Ener-
gyMizer® and start saving 8%-
20% on your electric bill each
and every month.
Homeowners everywhere
arc calling to get in on this 'free
money" opportunity. Here are
some of the most asked ques­
tions about the EnergyMizer®:
What is EnergyIMizer®?
The EnergyMizcC is a tech­
nologically advanced energy re­
duction system that can save up
to 20% on your electricity hills
each and every month. By
making appliances run more ef­
ficiently. the EnergyMizer will
reduce electrical waste, line
losses, consumption, and surge
charges.
The EnergyMizer® also acts
as a micro surge protector and
conditioner by flattening out the
regular peaks and valleys that
are inherent in electricity,
l astly, the EnergyMi/cr® acts
us a filter that reduces the harm­
ful Electromagnetic Fields
(EMF). This ensures a safer,
more protected environment in
your home.
Does it really work?
The EnergyM i/er® uses a
state-of-the-art technology to
actively monitor and improve
the power usage in your home.
Our technology reduces active
power and Kilowatt demands,
while providing voltage stabi­
lization. In other words, the
EnergyMizer49 is able to supply
the load with consistent voltage
during the ongoing power
surges that occur frequently in
your home - resulting in energy
savings of up to 20%. An ad­
ditional benefit is a longer lifes­
pan on all electrical equipment
due to the elimination of the
spikes and surges inherent in
electricity.
We can confidently say:
1. With two simple plug-in
units, the EnergyMizer® is the
easiest way to save money on
your electric bills without
changing your habits. Ener­
gyMizer® takes less than 2 min­
utes to install in most eases.
2. With our Risk-Free 90-
tee, you’ve got nothing to lose.
Plus, there is a 6-year manufac­
turer's warranty against defects.
3. With our new pricing. En­
ergy Mizer® will pay for itself
in months, leaving you extra
money on your household
budget each and every month.
These guarantees show we
completely support the huge
benefit of the EnergyMizer®.
How can I tell if it's working?
You need look no further
than your monthly electric bill.
After a period of consistent use,
you'll see a drop in your elec­
tric usage, thereby saving you
money. Simply compare your
monthly usage wdth the average
provided on your electric bill.
If you have a Kill A Watt1
measuring device, you’ll notice
a definite drop in amps used
with the use of EnergyMizer®.
This translates into
day Satisfaction Guarantee and
one-year 8% Savings Guaran­
less electric
used and a lower electric hill.
Does it affect my appliances?
Your appliances wall run as
usual. EnergyMizer® works to
erase the dirty power used dur­
ing start-up of motors in your
appliances.
In fact, using EnergyMizer®
will help to extend the life of
your appliances by protecting
them from surges commonly
found m electricity.
Why use the EnergyMizer®?
The EnergyMizer® flattens
out the peaks and valleys inher­
ent in normal energy consump­
tion, resulting in reduced kilo­
watt demand and consumption
while improving equipment ef­
ficiency. In addition, as elec­
trical equipment and appliances
age, they consume more power
in order to deliver the same
service. Since there is a variety
of equipment and appliances in
every household, each with dif­
ferent power anil current re­
quirements, this affects the volt­
age and current consumption.
The EnergyMizer*9 is able, with
the appropriate configuration,
to compensate for those ineffi­
ciencies.
Why do we provide two En­
ergyMizer® units for each
home installation?
One way to explain the way
in which electricity Hows into
a typical residence is to com­
pare it to the plumbing that ex­
ists in the home. (We don’t
condone the mixture of water
and electric - hut it makes for
an easy explanation!) Both
electricity and water enter the
home through a set of
wires/pipes and exit the home
once they are distributed
throughout the home.
In the case of water, the
main line feeds (he home via
your well or city water supply.
This water line is under pres­
sure and is then reduced down
to match the requirements of
the pipes that distribute the flow
ol water through the faucets and
spigots. That series of pipes
then returns that water with no
resistance through the various
drains all around the home.
In the case of electricity, the
power originates in the utility
line at a higher voltage and then
is reduced down as it comes
through the power meter and
electrical panel. The panel dis­
tributes the power through the
breakers and outlets throughout
the house, supplying the vari­
ous lights, appliances and other
sources requiring electric.
Within the.house, the electricity
is separated into two “legs"
(A&B) like the hot and cold
lines on a water system. While
hot and cold are separate in dif­
ferent pi|>es, in the case of elec­
tricity the A&B legs are sepa­
rated by the breakers that are
located in the electrical panel.
The A&B legs ultimately flow
from the same single source of
power that flows into the utility
meter.
All the circuits are con­
nected to circle back to the
point of origin, so the various
breakers and outlets act like ac­
cess points that tap into the
power feed. So, if one unit is
connected to the A pole and one
unit is connected to the B pole,
we are covering the entire elec-

# Organize Your Photos in Minutes


# SHOWCASE 500 OF YOUR CHERISHED PHOTOS IN ONE CONVENIENT LOCATION


# Uncover the Hidden Treasures in your home by


# organizing and preserving your photos

By John Whitehead, Media Services
Did you know the average
American family takes 1,000
photographs a year?
It’s TRUE! With the recent
advancements in photography,
there are very few choices
for storing your photographs in
an accessible and practical
manner!
That’s why William Keller
developed and designed an in­
novative, new way called “Hid­
den Family Treasures" to show­
case beloved photographs. Hid­
den Family Treasures makes it
easier to organize, preserve
and enjoy your photographs in
just minutes a day. Family trea­
sures will always be safe but
never be out of reach.
Conventional photo albums
take up room and are never
handy to view at any given time.
Hidden Family Treasures has a
unique storage system for up to
500 family photographs in one
convenient location. Its superior
storage system has been
awarded a United States
Patent.
Keep your photographs
protected, organized and ready
to be viewed at any time. By us­
ing Hidden Family Treasures in
your home, your family will en­
joy endless pleasure everyday
by enjoying yesterday’s memo­
ries.
Hidden Family Treasures is
the perfect way to display your
cherished photographs in your
home to preserve monumental
occasions such as weddings,
anniversaries, baptisms, open
houses and birthdays. It’s a
timesaving and practical way to
display photographs since it’s
easy to use by everyone while
protecting your precious'pho-
tographs.
Media Services interviewed
the creator of this one-of-a-kind
photograph heirloom, William
Keller. The interview follows:
Q. How did you come up
with the idea of Hidden Fami­
ly Treasures?
A. It was totally inspired to
bring back those special mo­
ments of yesterday with my
family and friends. After spend­
ing countless hours doing re­
search on today’s photograph
products, I was left very disap­
pointed.
I wanted something I would
be proud to display year round
in my home. But with so many
4”x6” and larger photographs
of all sizes, I also needed a con­
venient storage place to keep
my family memories alive and
assessable to share with my
family and friends. I started
making and selling Hidden
Family Treasures with some of
the elaborate units selling as
high as $800.00.
I didn’t want my grand­
children to find all of my
photographs slung into a box
and end up as a nameless
ancestor that they know nothing
about.
“Hidden Family Treasures” is
my solution. A place where you
can stroll down memory lane in
just minutes with friends and
loved ones. This beautiful, one-
of-a-kind keepsake is also per­
fect to pass down through gen­
erations to come with all your
special memories kept safely
inside.
Q. What is so unique
about Hidden Family Trea­
sures compared to the usual
photo album?
A. This one-of-a-kind keep­
sake is made of solid wood con­
struction for durable and daily
use. A beautiful, decorative
cover lets you display your fa­
vorite, large 8"x10” photo­
graph, artwork or even a mirror.
This beautiful cover is held
securely to the base by a spe­
cial hinge that attaches to the
141/8" tall x 141/2”deep x 15W’
wide swivel base for easy ac­
cessibility. An entire room of
family and friends can view your
pictures at the same time.
With the sturdy construction
of Hidden Family Treasures,
you can store up to 500 family
photographs of various sizes.
Plus, you can store your CD
Roms from your digital photos.
That way, everything you need to
view your family keepsakes will
be in one, convenient location.
Hidden Family Treasures
has such style and elegance it
can be displayed proudly in
any room of your house.
Q. Did you say 500 pho­
tographs? What holds the
photographs inside?
A. That’s right! That’s one
L

# ...to this!


## Store up to 500 favorite 4”x6” photographs In an


## e-x-p-a-n-d-a-b-l-e archival safe environment.

feature I love about Hidden
Family Treasures. It’s e-x-p-a-
n-d-a-b-l-e! You can add addi­
tional photo pages simply by
using the 3-ring holder that se­
curely holds multiple pages of
precious photographs.
You will receive 2 kinds of
photographic sheets with Hid­
den Family Treasures. One with
pockets for the standard 4 ”x6"
photographs and the other with
larger pockets for your favorite
enlargements.
Q. What steps can you
take to preserve these spe­
cial photographic memories?
A. How you store your pre­
cious, family photographic
memories will determine the life
expectancy of your pho­
tographs.
You should always keep
your photographs away from
excessive light, heat, and hu­
midity. This means storing your
photographs in the basement,
attic and garage is damaging
your precious memories.
Are your photographs still in
the film developing envelopes,
plastic baggies, manila folders
shoeboxes or just tossed into a
box?
All of these silent killers con­
tain acid, lignin and PVC. By
exposing your precious memo­
ries to these hazardous materi­
als, your photographs can
eventually discolor, fade and
even become brittle and crack
into pieces.
Even the acid from a ball
point pen can eat through pho­
tographs!
One of the best places to
stay in mint condition.
Q. Scrapbooking is one of
the hottest trends for pre­
serving photographs and
recording family history. Can
you add scrapbooking pages
to Hidden Family Treasures?
A. Yes! Actually, you can
add even more! With Hidden
Family Treasures, you can add
your favorite scrapbooking
pages by using the standard
size of
store your precious memories is
inside your own Hidden Family
Treasures. Up to 500 family
photographs can be stored in
one convenient, safe location.
All your photographs will be
kept safe, away from sunlight,
so your photographs will not
fade. Safe from hazardous ma­
terials, so your photographs
81 / 2 "x 11 ".
Along
with your precious scrapbook
pages, you also have plenty of
room to store special memen­
tos and keepsakes.
Q. What happens to a per­
son who has years of pho­
tographs that are completely
out of hand? How do you or­
ganize these family trea­
sures?
A.There are a lot of people in
this situation who do not keep
their precious memories orga­
nized. How do you get a handle
on this?
Simply start with the present
and work your way back into
time. Just setting a few minutes
every day or an hour on the
weekends will get your pictures
organized and preserve into the
Hidden Family Treasures
unique photograph display.
Q. Hidden Family Trea­
sures is a very prestigious
and innovative way to display
a lifetime of priceless memo­
ries. Are there other ways to
use Hidden Family Trea­
sures?
A. Yes! You can certainly
use Hidden Family Treasures
for displaying hobbies such as:
stamp collecting, coin collect­
ing, paintings and drawings,
certificates, memorabilia,
recipes, magazine covers and
articles. It’s a beautiful way to
display your most cherished
collections and memorabilia.
Hidden Family Treasures is
the consumer’s answer to orga­
nizing and preserving their pre­
cious, family photographs. And
what a better way to spend
quality, family time than enjoy­
ing photographs of loved ones
and friends.
End of interview.
Today, readers of this publi­
cation can get Hidden Family
Treasures at a major discount
when they order within 10 days
from the date of this publication.
Plus, to give their Hidden Fami­
ly Treasures even more senti­
mental value, readers of this
publication can also receive a
FREE custom engravable brass
plaque for each Hidden Family
Treasures ordered at this time.
Along with this major dis­
count and custom engraving,
readers of this publication are
approved to receive the follow­
ing special offer.
SPECIAL OFFER for cus-
tomers selecting Hidden Family
Treasures within the next 10
days.
International Home Shop­
ping is encouraging readers of
this publication to preserve their
family's memories by taking
more photographs. Internation­
al Home Shopping is giving
away a FREE Vivitar 35MM
High Focus Cameras (only
while supplies last) along with
the major discount of $50 off
Hidden Family Treasures while
supplies last.
To receive your FREE Vivitar
35MM High Focus Camera,
simply use the Special Read­
er’s Discount Coupon and reply
within the next 10 days, lf you
order after 10 days, you may
use the Special Reader’s Dis­
count Coupon at right to order
Hidden Family Treasures at the
regular price. Hidden Family
Treasures will become a family
heirloom of priceless memories
that can be passed through the
generations to come.

# 2014

• TEXAS RESIDENTS CASH IN: Bags of cash are up for grabs and Texas residents are being urged to call this number: 1-866-340-4756.
That's because for the next 3 days, thousands of Vault Bags loaded with real U.S. Gov't issued money are actually being released to Texas resi
dents who live in the counties listed below. Each Vault Bag is being loaded with U.S. currency from the vaults of the private World Reserve Mone
tary Exchange and the only thing county residents need to do to get the money is immediately call the Distribution Hotline for Texas residents to
cover just the $99 Vault Bag fee within the next 3 days.

# Cash in limbo goes to residents in 251 TX counties


# Bags loaded with real U.S. Gov't issued money are up for grabs as thousands of U.S. residents stand to miss the deadline


# to claim the money; now any resident of Texas living in a county listed below can claim the bags of money for themselves and keep


# all the valuable U.S. currency found inside by covering the $99 Vault Bagfee within the next 3 days

## ~


# How to get the money:


## If you live in one of the Texas counties listed above immediately call this number:


# 1-866-340-4756.


## Residents of Texas will be permitted to claim up to 10 Vault Bags of money beginning at precisely 8:30am this morning.

TEXAS - Texas residents who
find their county on the Distribu
tion List today will be running to
their phones.
That's because Vault Bags
loaded with real U.S. Gov't issued
money are actually being handed
over by the World Reserve to Texas
residents who beat the 3-day
deadline.
The only thing residents need to
do is look for their county on the
Distribution List printed in today's
publication. If their county is on the
list, they need to immediately call
the Distribution Hotline for Texas
residents before the 3-day dead
line ends to cover just the $99 Vault
Bag fee to get the money.
"This announcement is be
ing so widely advertised because
the money is in limbo until it is all
claimed. That's why we're allow
ing any resident of Texas who lives
in one of the 251 approved counties
to claim the money," said Timothy
J. Shissler, Director of Vault Opera
tions for the private World Reserve.
"Each valuable bag of money is
sealed with a U.S. Currency Secu
rity Seal. This guarantees that
the money in each Vault Bag has
not been tampered with when it
arrives," Shissler said.
"In fact, each Vault Bag is loaded
with 8 stunning estate wallets cus
tom made to hold, secure and pro
tect all 11 of the U.S. Gov't issued
notes pictured to the right with the
red stars: including one valuable
uncut sheet of four $2 Gov't issued
notes, three valuable never circu
lated U.S. Gov't issued $2 notes,
one rarely seen red seal $2 note
and three $1 blue seal silver certif
icate notes. With all these estate
wallets that protect the valuable
U.S. currency filling up each Vault
Bag, any resident lucky enough to
get one will be glad they did," said
Shissler.
"Although there are never any
guarantees, there's no telling what
these Vault Bags could be worth
some day because they contain
current uncut, old and rarely seen
U.S. currency. That's why at just
$99 per bag Texas residents are
sure to be claiming as many bags
of money they can get their hands
on," Shissler said.
And here's the best part. Resi
dents who claim the big green Vault
Locker pictured below loaded with
10 Vault Bags of U.S. Gov't issued
money will immediately have their
Vault Bag fee reduced to just $89
per bag, which is a real steal since
each Vault Locker is loaded with
110 valuable Gov't issued notes
pictured to the right with the red
stars.
"With all this money up for
grabs we're bracing for all the
calls and doing our best to make
sure Texas residents get through.
But, it is important that residents
find their county on the Distri
bution List and call the Distribu
tion Hotline for Texas residents
right away to beat the deadline.
Because when the bags of money
are gone, they're gone," Shissler
said.
"We know the hotlines will be
ringing off the hook because we're
allowing residents to claim up to 10
Vault Bags of money. But to make
sure residents of all 251 counties
on the Distribution List have a fair
chance to claim the money, we'll
be strictly enforcing the 10 bag
limit," he said.
Thousands of Texas residents
are expected to call to get the bags
of money.
So, Texas residents lucky enough
to find their county listed in today's
publication need to immediately
call the Distribution Hotline for
Texas residents before the 3-day
deadline ends to get the money.
So if lines are busy keep trying, all
calls will be answered.•
• GET 10 TIMES MORE MONEY: Pictured above is one of the valuable
Vault Lockers that everyone is trying to get. There's no telling how much
these Vault Lockers containing 10 Vault Bags loaded with real money could
be worth some day. That's because each Vault Locker is loaded with llO of
the valuable Gov't issued notes pictured to the right with red stars.
The Distribution Hotlines open at precisely 8:30am this morning for Texas residents who find their
county listed above. This release is authorized for Vault Bags containing U.S. Gov't issued money by the
World Reserve Monetary Exchange. Each Vault Bag is loaded with real U.S. Gov't issued money and can
only be claimed by Texas residents who live in one of the counties listed in today's publication and beat
the 3-day claim deadline to cover just the $99 Vault Bag fee for each bag of money. Each Vault Bag con
tains all 11 of the U.S. Gov't issued notes pictured below with a red star: including one valuable uncut
sheet of four $2 Gov't issued notes, three valuable never circulated U.S. Gov't issued $2 notes, one rarely
seen red seal $2 note and three $1 blue seal silver certificate notes. The valuable bags of money are being
released for the next 3 days. So, if lines are busy keep trying, all calls will be answered.

SPECIAL ADVERTISEMENT FEATURE ©2013 WORLD RESERVE MONETARY EXCHANGE INC. 8000 FREEDOM AVE., N. CANTON OH 44720
BISMARCK AREA RESIDENTS CASH IN:
Pictured above and protected by armed guards are the Overstuffed Money Bags containing
10 individual Vault Bags full of money that everyone is trying to get. That’s because each Vault Bag is known to contain over 100 U.S. Gov’t
issued coins some dating back to the early 1900s.

## Vault Bags loaded with U.S. Gov’t issued coins are up for grabs as thousands of U.S. residents stand to miss the deadline to claim


## the money; now any resident of North Dakota who finds their zip code listed below gets to claim the bags of money for themselves


## and keep all the valuable coins found inside by covering the Vault Bag fee within the next 2 days


# State zip codes determine who gets free Silver coins

NORTH DAKOTA - The phone lines are
ringing off the hook.
That’s because for the next 2 days Vault
Bags containing valuable U.S. Gov’t issued
coins are actually being handed over to Bis-
marck area residents who find their zip
code listed in today’s publication.
“Now that the bags of money are up for
grabs North Dakota residents are claiming
as many as they can get before they’re all
gone. That’s because after the Vault Bags
were loaded with over 100 U.S. Gov’t is-
sued coins the bags were sealed for good.
But, we do know that some of the coins date
clear back to the early 1900s, including: Sil-
ver, scarce, highly collectible, and currently
circulating U.S. Gov’t issued nickels, dimes
and quarter dollars, so there’s no tell-
ing what you’ll find until you sort through
all the coins,” said Timothy J. Shissler,
Chief Numismatist for the private World
Reserve.
The only thing residents need to do is find
their zip code on the Distribution List print-
ed in today’s publication. If their zip code
is on the list, they need to immediately call
the National Claim Hotline before the 2-day
order deadline ends.
Everyone who does is being given the
90% pure Silver Walking Liberty coin for
free just by covering the Vault Bags each
loaded with over 100 U.S. Gov’t issued coins
for only $ 99 each as long as they call before
the deadline ends.
Since this advertising announcement
can’t stop dealers and collectors from
hoarding all the valuable coins they can
get their hands on, the World Reserve had
to set a strict limit of ten Vault Bags per
resident.
“Coin values always fluctuate and there
are never any guarantees, but those who
get in on this now will be the really smart
ones. Just think what some of these coins
could be worth someday,” said Shissler.
Each Vault Bag is loaded in part with
highly sought after valuable collector coins
dating clear back to the 1900s including a
90% pure Silver Walking Liberty Half Dol-
lar, an Eisenhower Dollar, some of the last
ever minted U.S. Dollars, Kennedy Half
Dollars, Silver Mercury Dimes, rarely seen
Liberty ‘V’ Nickels, nearly 100 year old Buf-
falo Nickels and unsearched currently cir-
culating U.S. Gov’t issued nickels, dimes
and quarter dollars.
“We’re bracing for all the calls because
there are just hours left for residents to get
the Silver coins free,” he said.
So, Bismarck area residents lucky enough
to find their zip code listed in today’s publi-
cation need to immediately call the Nation-
al Claim Hotlines before the 2-day dead-
line ends to get the Silver coins free. If
lines are busy keep trying, all calls will be
answered.
■

# How to claim the bags of U.S. Gov’t issued coins:

Read the important information listed
below about claiming the Vault Bags. Then call the National Claim Hotline before the 2-day deadline ends at:
1-888-207-4579
FREE: WALKING LIBERTY VALUABLE:
RED BOOK COLLECTOR 90% PURE
VALUE $15 to $325 SILVER
Who gets to claim the bags of money: Thousands of U.S. residents stand to miss the deadline to claim the money. Now
Bismarck area residents who find their zip code listed in today’s publication and beat the 2-day deadline get to claim the bags
of money for themselves and keep all the U.S. Gov’t issued coins found inside.
I keep calling and can’t get through: That’s because each Vault Bag is guaranteed to contain a free Silver Walking
Liberty coin and just that one coin alone could be worth up to $325 in collector value. So thousands of residents are
calling to claim as many Vault Bags as they can get before they’re all gone. In fact, since each Vault Bag fee has been
reduced to just $ 99 nearly everyone is claiming at least three bags. So if lines are busy keep trying, all calls will be
answered.
How much are the Vault Bags worth: Coin values always fluctuate and there are never any guarantees, but here’s why
North Dakota residents are claiming as many Vault Bags as they can get before they’re all gone. After the Vault Bags were
loaded with over 100 U.S. Gov’t issued coins including: Silver, scarce, highly collectible, and currently circulating U.S. Gov’t
issued coins the bags were sealed for good. But we do know that some of the coins date back to the early 1900s. That
means there’s no telling what you’ll find until you sort through all the coins. So you better believe at just $ 99 the Vault Bag
fee is a real steal since the free Silver Walking Liberty coin alone could be worth between $15 to $325 in collector value.
Are the Silver Walking Liberty coins really Free: Yes. All Bismarck area residents who beat the 2-day deadline are
instantly being awarded a Silver Walking Liberty coin issued by the U.S. Gov’t between 1916-1947 free with each Vault
Bag they claim.
Why is the Vault Bag fee so low: Because thousands of U.S. residents have missed the deadline to claim the money
it’s being held in limbo at the vaults of the World Reserve and it has to be moved out in the next 2 days. That means the
money is up for grabs and now any resident who finds their zip code on the Distribution List below gets to claim the bags
of money for themselves and keep all the U.S. Gov’t issued coins found inside. Each Vault Bag fee was set at $ 149, but
residents who beat the 2-day deadline cover the reduced Vault Bag fee of just $ 99 as long as they call the National Claim
Hotline before the deadline ends at: 1-888-207-4579.

## How is all this possible:

This is all possible because thousands of U.S. residents stand to miss the
deadline to claim the money. Now Omaha area residents who find the first
two digits of their zip code listed in today’s publication and beat the 2-day
deadline get to claim the bags of money for themselves and keep all the
U.S. Gov’t issued coins found inside.

## Are the Silver coins really Free:

Y es. All Omaha area residents who beat the 2-day deadline are instantly
being awarded a free Walking Lady Liberty Silver coin issued by the U.S.
Gov’t between 1916-1947, a free 1964 50th anniversary John F. Kennedy
Silver coin and a free Benjamin Franklin Silver coin issued between 1948-
1963 with each Vault Bag they claim before the order deadline ends.

## What happens if I can’t get through:

Thousands of residents are calling to claim as many Vault Bags as they can
get before the deadline ends. That’s because each Vault Bag is guaranteed
to contain 3 free Silver coins issued by the U.S. Gov’t nearly 100 years
ago. So there’s no telling how much just one scarce coin alone, like those
shown to the left, could be worth in collector value. And since the Vault
Bag fee is just $ 149 everyone is claiming as many bags as they can get
before they’re all gone. So if lines are busy keep trying, all calls will be
answered.

## How much are the Vault Bags worth:

Coin values always fluctuate and there are never any guarantees, but
here’s why Nebraska residents are claiming as many Vault Bags as they
can get before they’re all gone. After the Vault Bags were loaded with over
100 U.S. Gov’t issued coins including: Silver, scarce, highly collectible, and
a big scoop of unsearched currently circulating U.S. Gov’t issued coins the
bagsweresealedforgood.Butwedoknowthatsomeofthecoinsdateback
to the early 1900s. That means there’s no telling what you’ll find until you
sortthroughallthecoins.Soyoubetterbelieveatjust $ 149smartcollectors
are snapping up all the Vault Bags they can get because there’s no telling
how much just one scarce coin alone could be worth in collector value.

## Why is the Vault Bag fee so low:

Because thousands of U.S. residents have missed the deadline to claim
the money the World Reserve has re-allocated Vault Bags that will be
scheduled to be sent out in the next 2 days. That means the money is
up for grabs and now any resident who finds the first two digits of their
zip code above gets to claim the bags of money for themselves and keep
all the U.S. Gov’t issued coins found inside. Each Vault Bag fee is set at
$ 199 for residents who miss the 2 day deadline, but for those who beat the
2-day deadline the Vault Bag fee is just $ 149 and shipping-handling as long
as they call the National Claim Hotline with this claim code UE714 before
the deadline ends at: 1-888-287-7537

# STEP 2.


## Call immediately to get your special restricted


## rolls of new Wyoming State Quarters

The special Toll Free Hotlines open at 8:30am today for Wyoming residents whose zip code
appears on the distribution list only. The World Reserve has authorized the release of the
special restricted rolls of new Wyoming state quarters to residents who beat the 48-hour order
deadline. If lines are busy, keep trying all calls will be answered. If you miss the deadline you’ll
be turned away from this offer and forced to wait for future announcements in this publication
or others, if any.
Only residents who find their zip code on the distribution list above and are one of the first
8,624 callers who beat the 48-hour order deadline for the custom-stitched, gold embossed
vault bags for just $ 11 and shipping are getting the special restricted rolls of new Wyoming
state quarters at face value. To get yours call the special Toll Free Hotline number below.

# 2011


# State


# Distribution


# List

## WHAT TO DO IF ALL LINES ARE BUSY

State residents are urged to pick up a Vault Box
of tlieir last remaining State Quarter Dollars. U.S.

remaining uncirculated U.S. State Quarter Dol­
lars FREE in secured Vault Boxes.
The private United States Commemorative
Gallery has a limited number of State Quarter
Dollars in their Maximum Security Vault that
have been left unclaimed by a small percentage
of customers.
Every Unclaimed State Quarter Dollar
prepared to be sent is in Brilliant, Uncirculated
condition. Only Uncirculated State Quarter Dol­
lars have increased in value as much as 400%
and could appreciate much more in the future.
Every State Resident is urged to take full advan­
tage of this FREE State Quarter Dollar release.
These uncirculated State Quarter DolUu's
were immediately shelled in special protective
capsules lo maintain their collector grade value.
Gallery Officials have decided to offer these
unclaimed U.S. State Quarter Dollars FREE to
State Residents to ensure the remaining Slate
Quarters get into the hands of the public.
Your Region has been specially selected to
receive these uncirculated State Quarter Dollars
FREE. You only need to remit the small price of
the protective capsules, sealing and rolling labor
costs for these unclaimed and Uncirculated State
Quarter Dollar Rolls.
Collector grade Uncirculated coins have the
highest value because they are In mint condition.
Standard grade Circulated coins are usually
damaged by rubbing surfaces or by other coins.
Also, acid fi-om people's hands causes coin
metal to corrode.
NOTE: Even coin rolling machines may
damage the condition of these coins. This is
why it is important to seal uncirculated coins in
protective capsules prior to completing the
secured Vault Rolls containing 12 coins each.
Specially trained Vault Administrators hand-pick
each coin and seal them into the protective
capsules while wearing gloves.

by banks and almost never ever seen in
circulation.

A 4
n n Friday, March 28, 2014
Association, which Fallin cur-
rently serves as chair.
“We are calling on some
leadership on behalf of the
governor,” Inman said. “The
governor was elected to lead
this state, and the governor
for the last several years said
she supports Common Core.”
He said a flurry of pressure
from a fringe element caused
the governor to cave in on her
support for Common Core.
“Her leadership is nonex-
istent on this issue,” Inman
said. “For the head of the or-
ganization that helped to cre-
ate these standards to turn
around let them die under her
watch is an embarrassment on
behalf of the governor.”
He said Fallin, who is seek-
ing a second term, is worried
about attracting a primary
opponent.
“We hope she will change
direction again and stand up
and be the leader she is being
paid to be,” Inman said.
Inman said his caucus has
mixed opinions about Com-
mon Core but said it was a
Republican-backed initiative.
Alex Weintz, a spokesman
for the governor, said Fallin
was not involved in creating
Common Core and was not
governor when the standards
were implemented in Okla-
homa.
Fallin had expressed sup-
port for Common Core in the
past.
“The standards were writ-
ten and developed prior to
her serving as governor or
as chairman of the NGA,”
Weintz said. “Gov. Fallin has
always supported higher
standards and increased rigor
in the classroom.”
Fallin has consistently op-
posed federal interference in
Oklahoma education, he said.
“It has become clear that
there are still widespread
concerns about Common
Core and potential federal in-
terference,” he said. “In light
of these concerns, the gov-
ernor has said she will work
with the Legislature to cre-
ate an appropriate substitute
that guarantees high academ-
ic standards and Oklahoma’s
independence in the process
of creating the standards.
“The governor will con-
tinue to lead on this issue and
work to improve schools. Rep.
Inman is trying to turn edu-
cation reform into a partisan
sideshow rather than focusing
on what’s best for Oklahoma’s
children and teachers.”
Meanwhile, Senate Presi-
dent Pro Tem Brian Bingman,
R-Sapulpa, said criticism of
Common Core is not con-
fined to a fringe element.
“We are hearing from par-
ents,” he said. “We are hear-
ing from some teachers. We
are hearing from pastors that
call their legislators. This is
not just a right-wing group
lashing out at the standards
of Common Core. You are
hearing it all over the coun-
try. People are not satisfied.”
Bingman in the past has
said he supported Common
Core.
“Legislators are hearing
from a broad-based group of
folks,” Bingman said.

# FALLIN

FROM A1
BROAD CRITICISM
Brian Bingman: The Senate president pro tem,
who in the past has said he supported Com-

# Owasso rally speakers


# criticize Common Core

BY RHETT MORGAN
World Staf Writer
OWASSO — A three-mem-
ber panel of school parents
and educators rallied against
Common Core standards
Thursday night at Friendship
Baptist Church.
Speakers who included
Kristal Picolet, the leader of
Owasso Against Common
Core, addressed a crowd of
about 50 people for about two
hours, detailing the group’s
reasons for disagreement
with the national education
reform measure.
Nikki Jones, a prekinder-
garten teacher in Tulsa Pub-
lic Schools, and Linda Mur-
phy, a veteran educator and
former Republican candidate
for state superintendent, also
made presentations that con-
cluded with a question-and-
answer session.
Picolet, whose group has
grown to more than 1,250
members in about three
months, called Common
Core a “train wreck.” She said
her third-grade son, enrolled
in Owasso Public Schools, al-
ready had taken more than 40
tests this school year.
“Local authority has been
stripped away,” said Picolet,
who said she moved away
from Colorado to escape
Common Core but now it’s in
Oklahoma schools, too.
“God gave these children to
us,” she said. “If we don’t pro-
tect them, who will?”
Common Core originated
out of a National Governors
Association efort to bolster
and standardize K-12 edu-
cation across the country.
Oklahoma adopted the pro-
gram in 2010, in part to boost
state schools’ chances in the
Obama administration’s Race
to the Top grant competition.
Common Core standards
were adopted by 45 states
and the District of Colum-
bia. Only Alaska, Minnesota,
Nebraska, Texas and Virginia
haven’t adopted the stan-
dards.
Opponents claim that the
standards represent a federal
intrusion into state and lo-
cal education policy and will
raise the amount of class-
room time devoted to testing.
In the Oklahoma Legisla-
ture, House Bill 3399, a mea-
sure that would repeal Com-
mon Core in the state, could
be heard by the full Senate
next week.
Picolet and Jones stressed
that standardized tests zap
creativity and stymie teach-
ing latitude.
Picolet brought a school
math sample from her son,
who arrived at the right sum
for his addition problem but
whose grade sufered be-
cause he failed to explain its
“associative property.”
Murphy said federal mon-
ey was used to entrap state
education systems through
grants given to two national
assessment consortia run by
top state school oicials via
the Partnership for Assess-
ment of Readiness for College
and Careers, and the Smarter
Balanced Assessment Con-
sortium.
She added that states are
collecting extensive personal
student data stored in data
banks without parental con-
sent and are ditching superi-
or state academic standards.
Two years ago, Jones
said she monitored third-
grade state testing and wit-
nessed students who were
so stressed from the exams
that they threw up and soiled
themselves.
Jones, who said she works
at a school where at least 90
percent of the students are at
or below poverty level, claims
that standardized testing
failed to take demographics
into account.
“The testing now is ridicu-
lous,” said Jones, who urged
parents to have their children
opt out of the tests. “It’s just
not worth it.”
Rhett Morgan 918-581-8395
rhett.morgan@tulsaworld.com
Kristal Picolet holds up her child’s test work as she speaks
against the Common Core during a rally at Friendship Baptist
Church in Owasso on Thursday evening.

## extensive student


## testing plays a


## central role.

Nikki Jones:
“The test-
ing now is
ridiculous,”
said the TPS
prekin-
dergarten
teacher.
FUNDING
Linda
Murphy:
The veteran
educator
questioned
how federal
grants were
used.

# THE DAILY GLOBE


# WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 29, 2004 • 7


## Benefit spaghetti


## dinner Sunday

MERCER — The Mercer School,
Fire Department, emergency medi-
cal technicians and Faith Lutheran
Church will host a benefit spaghetti
dinner on Sunday from 4 to 7 p.m.
at the Mercer Community Center.
There will be door prizes and raffles.
The benefit is for Adam and Car-
lye Kussard, whose home burned
down on Sept. 22. Adam is a volun-
teer firefighter and EMT in Mercer
and his sister, Carlye, is a junior at
Mercer School.
Everyone is urged to come to tho
church and support these two
young people. Or send monetary
donations to Associated Bank of
Mercer, PO Box 128, Mercer Wl
54547.

## American Legion


## Auxiliary update

At their recent fall luncheon,
members of the American Legion
Auxiliary to Post 424, Mercer-Mani-
towish Waters, welcomed new
members, heard a report on veter-
ans' hospitals and a story about
Camp Legion in Lake Tomahawk.
Proceeds from Ihc poppy sale
permitted gifts to eight different vet-
erans' organization. Members had a
float in the July 4 parade with a
World War II Monument theme,
prior to its recent debut in Washing-
ton. Mercer students assisted mem-
bers in planting geraniums at veter-
ans' graves in the cemeteries of Ihe
two towns.
An American flag will be donated
to third graders at Mercer and
Northland Lakeland schools before
Veterans Day.

## Use of Great Lakes


## water discussed

WEST ALLIS (AP) —An environ-
mental group official expressed sup-
port for much of a proposal to regu-
late large-scale diversion of Great
Lakes water, but said some
changes ate needed.
Cameron Davis, executive direc-
tor of the Lake Michigan Federation,
urged al a hearing Tuesday aban-
doning language that would give^
larger, water.use rs.irisidbthgiQfeal
Lakes basin a higher, threshold of
water removal before the governors
can step in.
Davis urged maintaining unani-
mous approval of all water deals by
governors and clarifying language
about requiring conservation prac-
tices for Ihose allowed to tap the
lakes.

## Woman gets probation,


## jail in newborn death

HIGHLAND CENTER (AP) — A
former prom queen will spend a
docade on probation, including a
year in county jail, for letting her
baby die minutes after she secretly
gave birth in her bedroom last year.
Circuit Judge Edward Leinewe-
ber said he thinks Kristin Scott can
be rehabilitated and will punish her-
self Ihe rest of her life for keeping
not only that pregnancy a secret,
but hiding an earlier one that pro-
duced a stillborn child.
"No amount of punishment can
bring one or both ol Ihose babies
back, but a serious focus on reha-
bilitation can salvage Miss Scott,"
the judge said in sentencing Scott
Tuesday.
Scott, 23, pleaded no contest in
a June plea bargain to charges of
child neglect causing death and
concealing a child's death. Prosecu-
tors dropped a charge of hiding a
corpse in exchange.
Scoll could have gotten 20 years
in prison.
Instead, Leineweber gave her
the 10 years ol probation and jail
time on the neglect count. He also
required her to undergo random
pregnancy tests to make sure any
future pregnancies are revealed.
She also must complete 250 hours
of community service and keep see-
ing a therapist.
The judge sentenced her lo five
years on probation on the conceal-
ment charge.

## High Court to decide if


## Nader goes on ballot

MADISON (AP) — The slate
Supreme Court agreed lo hear an
appeal seeking to keep indepen-
dent Ralph Nader on the Wisconsin
ballot for president, just hours after
a Dane County judge ruled he
should be kicked off.
The justices Tuesday put the
case on a fast track to a decision
wilh a deadline looming for munici-
pal clerks to begin mailing ballots to
absentee voters. That won't be pos-
sible until they know whether lo
include Nader on Ihe list of presi-
dential candidates.
The court's decision could have
other implications for the presiden-
tial race in Wisconsin. Some
Democrats still blame Nader's 2000
candidacy for President and fear he
could siphon votes away from their
nominee, John Kerry, this year.

# Ashland County authorities investigate drowning

By RALPH ANSAMI
Globe News Editor
ASHLAND —The Ashland County
Sheriff's Department is investigating a
drowning in a pontoon boating accident
last Friday.
Charles T. Nichols, 47, of Mosinee, was
one of seven people on hoard a home-
made pontoon boat that capsized in Gor-
don Lake at 10:43 p.m., according to Ash-
land County Sheriff John Kovach.
Two of the other six passengers man-
aged to swim to shore after the hoat had
capsized and called for help, while the
other passengers hung on to the over-
turned craft.
Emergency personnel then rescued
the four passengers who were clinging to
the overturned boat.
Nichols' body was recovered near the
pontoon at about 1:15 a.m.
Kovach said none of those involved in
the accident was wearing life preservers.
The Wisconsin Department of Nntural
Resources -is also investigating the inci-
dent.
The WDNR had recorded 10 boating
fatalities as of the early part of August.
Last year, there were 19 fatalities for the
entire boating season.
The first fatality of the boating saa-
son, which occurred in Iron County, is
still listed as "under investigation" by
the DNR. It also involved a. pontoon boat.
Ronald Spahn, 35, of DeForest, Wis.,
drowned on May 29.
WDNR investigators said Spahn, who
fell overboard on the pontoon on Mercer
Lake, was not wearing it life preserver
either.
The accident was discovered when
Spahn's 7-year-old son was seen next to
the pontoon, holding one of his dad's ten-
nis shoes, the DNR reported. The pon-
toon hnd drifted to shnre.
Rescue divers from Tomahawk later
located Spahn's body in deeper water.

# Cheney attends


# rally in Eau Claire

EAU CLAIRE (AP) — Vice
President Dick Cheney
unleashed a blistering attack'
Tuesday on John Kerry, calling
him indecisive and lacking in
resolve to lead the nation against
terrorism.
The Democratic presidential
nominee seems to adopt different
positions on the issues daily,
Cheney told about 500 support-
ers at a rally.
"Last week, John Kerry gave
us yet another position on the
war in Iraq," he said. "He
attacked the progress we are
making and the policies we have
implemented. Yet despite, all the
harsh rhetoric, Senator Kerry
endorsed many of the same goals
President Bush has been pursu-
ing in Iraq for months."
Cheney told the crowd Kerry
had said that under his leader-
ship, more of America's friends
would speak with one voice on
Iraq.
"That seemed a little odd com-
ing from a guy who donsn't speak
with one voice^ himself," lie said,

# Cranberry harvest


# expected to be down


# over last season :

WISCONSIN RAPIDS (APV
— Wisconsin cranberry growers
expect their harvest to be down
this season but still strong
despite the cool summer and a
warm fall.
The harvest is expected to
yield 3.31 million barrels,
according to the Wisconsin State
Cranberry Growers Association.
Last yonr growers harvested a;
record 3.6 million barrels, said.
Tom Lochner, the association's;
executive director. !
"I think like every other crop'
it's due to weather," Lochner,
said. "Most of what we do out
there ... is related to how moth-!
or nature treats us during the :
growing season." j
The fruit grows in size in
August and the lack of heat hin- :
derccl its growth, he said. •
But 3,31 million barrels is
still good for the state, Lochner;
said. i
Nationwide, more than 0.28]
million barrels will be harvested!
this fall, according to the USDAi
Cranberry Marketing Commit- 1
(ee. ;
The second highest producer!
is expected to be Massachusetts,'
which is projected to harvest 1.8j
million . barrels.- .Oth&r^atga j
that grow cranberries are New
Jersey, Oregon nnd Washington.
Harvesting begins in early
October and lasts about three
weeks.
Key Wisconsin cranberry
areas of La Crosse and Wausau
areas had temperatures that
were three degrees cooler than
average this summer. And there
has been a warm start to fall,
with a number of days in
September reaching 80 degrees.

■ Only Congress can make the decision to change over the U.S. Dollar to something new, but people are talking more and more
about the possibility. Is the time coming for one currency for all of the United States, Canada, and Mexico? Europe has already
replaced several countries’ currencies with the Euro, so the idea that the United States, Canada, and Mexico could come together
and create their own currency is very real. See below for what could be the new North American Union currency!

# Valuable Brilliant, Uncirculated State


# Quarters available for only the price of


# protective capsule - no matter what their value


# All have increased substantially in value and some by over 275%

This Important Notification has been released to inform
you of an opportunity for you to receive Uncirculated U.S.
Statehood Quarter Dollars FREE including the third
Quarter Dollar of 2003, Maine.
The United States Commemorative Gallery has
reserved a number of Statehood Quarter Dollars in their
vault for immediate release to the public. In addition, this
also allows you to be among the first to receive the new
Maine Statehood Quarter Dollar FREE.
These Statehood Quarter Dollars are all Uncirculated
and are in special protective capsules.
All of these Statehood Quarter Dollars have already
increased substantially in value and some by over 275%!
United States Commemorative Gallery Officials have
decided to offer these Uncirculated U.S. Statehood
Quarter Dollars FREE to select regions of the country to
ensure equal nationwide opportunity. Your region has
been specially selected to receive these Uncirculated
Statehood Quarter Dollars FREE. You only need to cover
the small price of the protective capsules for these Brilliant,
Uncirculated Statehood Quarter Dollars.
Uncirculated Collector coins have the highest value
because they are in mint condition. Circulated coins are
usually damaged through rubbing surfaces or other coins.
Also, acid from people's hands causes coin metal to cor-
rode. This is why it is important to seal uncirculated coins in
protective capsules while wearing gloves.
The United States Commemorative Gallery has put
these Brilliant, Uncirculated Statehood Quarter Dollars into
separate lots for each year the Quarter Dollars were
released. There is one Statehood Quarter Dollar for each
state.
The Brilliant, Uncirculated Statehood Quarter Dollar
lots that are available will include the highest appreciating
Uncirculated Statehood Quarter Dollars - Delaware and
Pennsylvania both over 275%.
The other 18 Statehood Quarter Dollars have increased
substantially in value and could appreciate much more in
the future. These are New Jersey, Georgia, Connecticut,
Massachusetts, Maryland, South Carolina, New
Hampshire, Virginia, New York, North Carolina, Rhode
Island, Vermont, Kentucky, Tennessee, Ohio, Louisiana,
Indiana and Mississippi. The newest release, Maine, is
also sure to substantially increase in value.
No matter what their value, every Statehood Quarter
Dollar is FREE. All you need to do to claim them is remit the
price of the protective capsule, which is only $2.20 each.
These protective capsules are critical so that the Statehood
Quarter Dollars will not be handled and therefore wiil main-
tain their highest possible uncirculated value. All of these
Statehood Quarter Dollars have been placed into the pro-
tective capsules by specially-trained United States
Commemorative Gallery Staff who wear special white place-
ment gloves to insert the quarters into the protective cap-
sules and seal them.
You may claim one or all 4 of the lots that are now
available. There are 5 Quarters in each lot. The U.S. Mint
only mints 5 Statehood Quarter Dollars each year. If you
claim one lot, you only pay $2.20 each for the protective
capsules in that lot which comes to only $11 plus $3 ship-
ping and handling. If you claim all 4 lots of Uncirculated
20 Statehood Quarter Dollars, you will get FREE Shipping
and Handling and -receive all 20 Statehood Quarter
Dollars for only $39. If you order all 4 lots you will also get
the new Maine 2003 Statehood Quarter Dollar FREE.
IMPORTANT: You must call Toll-Free 1-800-600-3227
or return the Statehood Quarter Dollar Claim Form on this
page immediately. You must claim your FREE Uncirculated
Statehood Quarter Dollars within 10 days from reading this
notification, because we will then offer them to another
selected region of the country.
These Statehood Quarter Dollars produced by the
United States Mint have been one of the biggest sensa-
tions in the history of coin collecting. We expect that they
will appreciate in value substantially in the future.
Remember, each Statehood Quarter Dollar is only
minted for 10 weeks of one year. After that, no more will
be minted. That is another reason why they are so valu-
able. And the Statehood Quarter Dollars are Uncirculated
and, therefore, you will not be able to find them in your
change. The Uncirculated Statehood Quarter Dollars that
have been put into a protective capsule have a greater
value. They also make great gifts.

Brilliant, Uncirculated condition and protected by our specially designed
Protective Capsules. You only pay for the protective coin capsules which is
$2.20 each.
To claim your FREE Statehood Quarter Dollars by phone call 1-800-600-3227
and ask for operator 90710 to get your Free Statehood Quarter Dollars
and Protective Capsules. Operators are on duty Monday through Saturday
7am - Midnight EST.
To claim your FREE Statehood Quarter Dollars by mail, fill out below and mail
this Claim Form to the address below. Check which lots you want. There are
5 Statehood Quarter Dollars for each year. Therefore, each lot contains 5.

Quarter Dollar protected in a special Protective Capsule for only $39.

FREE.

# VANBHHG STATE QUARTERS TO RE FOUNDI


# Limited supply of UNCIRCULATED State Quarter Dollars

# Early release State Quarters very hard to find.

By Lorraine Kamp,

## Silver Franklin Half Dollar

with any order from this Ad
Flonored as the first non-president American to
appear on a United States Coin, Benjamin
Franklin appeared on the Half Dollar beginning
in 1948 for 16 years. The minting of the Silver
Franklin Half Dollar was halted in 1963 when a
new design honoring the late President
Kennedy was rushed into circulation. This Half
Dollar was the last design to have all released
years minted in .900 Fine Silver.

Advertisement Media Services

# Official and authentic TITANIC historical


# items now available for a limited time


# They are: 1) copies of the passenger and crew log, 2) only known photographs


# taken by Father Brown aboard Titanic, 3) exact reproduction of a newspaper


# found at the wreck site, 4) historic photos of the 4 living survivors at a first


# ever meeting in 1996, and 5) actual pieces of coal from aboard Titanic

By John Whitehead,
Media Services
(Canton, OH) Official and
authentic Titanic historical items
are finally being made available
to the public for a very limited
period of time and while certain
supplies last.
These historic items are
being made available by the U.S.
Commemorative Fine Art
Gallery, a division of SCI. SCI,
along with RMS Titanic Inc.,
financed and carried out the now
famous 1996 Titanic Expedition
and Cruise, as seen on The
Discovery Channel and a
National Geographic television
special. This expedition fully
illuminated the Titanic wreck for
the first time using newly devel-
oped million-dollar light towers.
The Expedition brought up many
artifacts from the wreck site and
attempted to raise a large part of
Titanic. The historic 1996
Expedition for the first time
allowed the public to view a
Titanic dive via two large cruise
ships.
The 1996 Expedition also
was historic because for the first
time all four of the then-living
Titanic survivors were brought
together to meet each other, and
at this meeting historic pho-
tographs were taken of these sur-
vivors.
RMS Titanic, Inc. is the
exclusive and official legal
guardian of the Titanic wreck as
determined by the U.S. Federal
Court, and SCI is the exclusive
owner of the copyrighted portrait
pictures taken of the survivors at
the 1996 Titanic Expedition.
In total, there are 5 separate
Titanic historical items being
made available to the public at
this time.
The first historical Titanic
item is a combination art repro-
duction and photo collection put
together in a dramatic wall-size
display. This historic print is
titled "Tribute to Titanic
Survivors" in honor of the 4 liv-
ing Titanic survivors in 1996 and
in recognition of their participa-
tion in the 1996 Titanic
Expedition. Along with the pho-
tos, the print contains a certified
commemorative drawing of
Titanic entitled "Fairwell to
Queenstown." For this drawing,
U.S. Commemorative Fine Art
Gallery commissioned world-
renowned Titanic historian and
artist Michael V. Ralph, who cre-
ated this special-edition, original
painting of RMS Titanic moored
off Queenstown, England, her
last port of call before embark-
ing on her fateful voyage. The 4
photos in the print are historic
portraits of 4 of the survivors;
Millvina Dean, Eleanor Shuman,
Edith Haisman (who since
passed away), and Michel
Navratil. For historical accura-
cy, each of these survivors auto-
graphs appear below their por-
trait photos, which are then
shown below the "Farewell to
Queenstown" painting. This col-
lector's piece is highly recom-
mended for framing and will
definitely be the centerpiece of
attention in any home or office.
The second historical item
being made available is a set of 4
of the only known photographs
taken aboard Titanic by a Jesuit
Priest, Father Brown, who
boarded Titanic at Southampton,
England, but for some reason
left the ship when she arrived at
her last port of call, Queenstown,
before embarking on the fateful
voyage. These 4 photos histori-
cally show key parts of Titanic
and passengers aboard Titanic.
The photos show: the main deck
and passengers; the main dining
room; the actual bridge of
Titanic, with the helmsman
wheel pictured in the center with
engine and other controls on
either side; and one of the first-
class rooms, showing a beautiful
mirrored dresser and the beds on
either side. These collector prints
are recommended for framing.
The third item now available
is an exact full-color copy of a
page of the April 10, 1912,
Southampton, England, newspa-
per that was printed the
day Titanic departed from
Southampton, England, on her
fateful voyage. This newspaper
was brought on board Titanic by
either a passenger or crew mem-
ber. This page of the newspaper
was found in relatively good
shape and readable at the Titanic
wreck site more than 80 years
later at the pitch-black, 12,500-
foot depth of the wreck. The
stories on this page will be of
great interest to history buffs as
they can actually read what was
going on in the world when
Titanic departed for her voyage
on that day in 1912. This historic
collector's copy is also recom-
mended for framing.
The fourth sensational histor-
ical item is a special edition
reproduction of the actual list of
passengers and crew members
who were aboard Titanic. Also
included in this book is informa-
tion on the number of first-class,
second-class, and steerage-class
passengers and crew members
on board; how many survived;
what happened to the survivors
in the following years; pictures
of crew members and passen-
gers; facts of the disaster; how
the wireless call for help was
sent; stories of the event from
survivors; the music played as
Titanic sank; a section drawing
of the giant Titanic; and much
more.
The final historic item is
truly sensational. It is an authen-
tic piece of actual coal that was
aboard Titanic.
Recovering the Titanic arti-
facts and coal was a costly and
difficult process. The French
Navy's deep-sea submarine that
was used has an inside diameter
of only 6.9 feet and takes two
hours to descend 2-1/2 miles to
the ocean's floor. It costs
approximately $100,000 per day
for each dangerous dive. The
titanium-alloy crew sphere of the
deep-sea submarine must with-
stand pressures of up to 6,000
pounds per square inch.
Six external lights enabled
the dive team to search the bot-
tom of the ocean for the ship's
artifacts and coal, and two robot-
ic arms were used to pick up the
items found, as the crew viewed
through portholes that measured
less than 5 inches in diameter.
Each individual piece of coal
from Titanic is attractively dis-
played in a museum-style case
with a custom-engraved brass
plaque with the name of the coal
patron honored as a conserva-
tionist.
This exceptional collection
of historical items will only
be available by the U.S.
Commemorative Fine Art
Gallery for a very limited period
of time or while certain supplies
last. The photo and art repro-
duction "Tribute to Titanic
Survivors," with the commemo-
rative drawing of Titanic entitled
"Fairwell to Queenstown," and
This historic collector's print is titled "Tribute to Titanic Survivors," in

by a Jesuit Priest, Father Brown, who boarded Titanic at Southampton,
ipation in the 1996 Titanic Expedition. The photos in the print were
England, but left the ship when she arrived at her last port of call,
taken when the survivors were together for the first time at the
Queenstown, before her fateful voyage. These 4 photos historically show
Expedition. The art print itself is a certified commemorative drawing of
key parts of Titanic and passengers aboard Titanic including: the main
Titanic entitled "Fairwell to Queenstown," by world-renowned Titanic
deck and passengers, the main dining room, the actual bridge of Titanic,
historian and artist Michael V. Ralph, who created this special-edition,
and one of the first-class rooms. These photo prints are recommended for
original painting of RMS Titanic moored off Queenstown. Each of the
framing and shown here as they look in the gallery frames.
survivors autographed their portrait photos. This was then presented to
the National Archives and the Library of Congress in Washington, D.C.
You can find the names of any of your
unknown ancestors who may have been
aboard with this special edition repro-
duction of the actual list of passengers
and crew members aboard Titanic. Read about the first-class, second-
class, and steerage-class passengers and crew members; how many sur-
vived; what happened to the survivors in the following years; pictures of
crew members and passengers; facts of the disaster; how the wireless call
for help was sent; stories of the event from survivors; the music played as
Titanic sank; a section drawing of the giant Titanic; and much more.

brought on board Titanic by either a passenger or crew member. This
page of the newspaper was found in relatively good shape and readable at

Each individual piece of coal from Titanic is attractively displayed in a
museum-style case with a custom-engraved brass plague with the name
of those who purchase the coal honored as conservationists. Proceeds
from the coal will conserve the Titanic artifacts for history and help
build The Titanic Museum Ship, which is planned to tour the world.
Coal patrons' names will be immortalized in a Titanic Museum
Registry that will be displayed permanently in the museum.
