# AlWOUNCING THE TTIANIC EXPEDITION CRUISE


# You can witness history when


# for the first and only time a

J 9 • •V 1 i"

# section of Titanic is raised and


# the wrecli is fully illuminated


# Will depart from and return to New York City

A special invitation
from RMS Titanic,
Inc. President, George
Tulloch
(New York, NY) As presi-
dent of RMS Titanic, Inc.
I wish to offer you the
opportunity to be one of
the few people who will
receive an invitation to
personally witness the
first-ever raising of a
m£gor part of the sunken
Titanic.
This section of Titanic
that will be raised is
spectacular and pristine.
It is 2-1/2 stories by 3
stories in size and weighs
approximately 25 tons!
This section is a recog-
nizable and definitive
part of Titanic. It is from
the very top of the hull at
midship and houses 6
portholes. It still carries
the paint of Titanic as it
changes from the upper
white stripe to the lower
black hull color. Even
the White Star Line
trademark gold pinstripe
appears intact.
As you may already
know from news reports,
I have led the missions
over the past nine years
that have recovered
many artifacts from the
Titanic wreck which lies
in a depth of over 2-1/2
miles where there is no
light, and pressures are
so great that you could
not even explode a bomb
there. The bomb would
implode. To date we
have been able to recover,
for history, artifacts
which include items pas-
sengers had brought
aboard, decor items, utili-
ty items and part of the
coal that was aboard the
Titanic. But this is the
first time a piece of the
actual structure of the
ship will be raised, and it
will be the last.
These dives are being
done in a joint effort with
IFREMER (Institute of
France For Research and
Exploration of the Sea)
who has the only deep-
sea sub (technical y
called a submersible), the
Nautile, that can reach
the depth of the Titanic
wreck. My company,
RMS Titanic, Inc., by fed-
eral r.ourt order, is the
official legal guardian of
the Titanic wreck and no
one else is permitted to
dive to the wreck site and
take any items at all.
In this expedition you
lights illuminate the
entire bow section which
is an area the size of a
football fíeld. It is a sight
no one has actually ever
seen. To date, artists
have had to sketch the
wreck like a jigsaw puz-
zle from partially illumi-
nated sections of pho-
tographs and videos.
I have been in the
Nautile and personally
viewed Titanic on the
ocean floor, and I have
also been there as arti-
facts have been brought
to the surface. Words can-
not describe the emo-
tions. It is like meeting
Napoleon or being the
first to come upon the
great pyramids of Egypt.
I can testify to you that
when the major section of
Titanic breaks the sur-
face for the first time,
and when you see the
wreck fully lit for the first
time, it will be a once-in-
a-lifetime experience you
will never forget.
But you won't have to
experience the dangers
that the dive team and I
will encounter. You will
witness all of this while
eryoying a safe and luxu-
rious special cruise.
The main cruise from
New York will be aboard
the "SS Island Breeze"
departing on August 23rd
for the Titanic '96
Expedition Cruise and
wil go directly to the dive
site, taking about two
and a half days to arrive.
There will be expert
lecturers on board, and
many authentic artifacts
recovered from the wreck
will be on display
throughout the ship.
Other nostalgic events
will occur on the cruise
including music played
will also witness another
first occurrence concern-
ing the Titanic wreck.
For the first time the
main section of the
wreck, the bow section,
will be fully illuminated
to be videotaped and pho-
tographed. To date only
small sections of the
wreck have been illumi-
nated with low-pdWered
lights. You will be there
to witness for the first
time as high-powered
from copies of music
sheets played by the band
aboard Titanic. You will
also be on board with and
socialize with many of the
who's who of the world.
This event will receive
worldwide media cover-
age. Already, major trav-
el publications are plan-
ning front-page stories.
The Island Breeze will
rendezvous with the
"Titanic '96 Expedition
Fleet" on August 26th
and be part of the expedi-

tion to raise the Titanic
hull section and conduct
the first full illumination
of the Titanic wreck.

( N ew York) RMS Titanic,
Inc. president George Tulloch
drew immediate media atten­
tion from CNN, CNBC, FOX,
CBS, USA TODAY and hun­
dreds of other newspapers and
b r o a d c a s t e rs w h en he a n­
nounced the availability of the
a u t h e n t ic coal t h at w as
aboard the RMS Titanic when
it sank on April 15, 1912.
P h o ne c a l ls and l e t t e rs
began pouring in. A conserva­
tor in Michigan wrote, 'Your
efforts are certainly most
appreciated by TITANIC
enthusiasts like myself all over
the world. I personally can't
thank you enough for providing
this most sacred artifact...at
such an affordable price. You
are to be commended."
Expeditions to recover the
coal and other artifacts cost
$100,000 a day. Only a limit­
ed quantity of coal has been
salvaged. The danger of div­
i ng 2V2 m i l es b e n e a th t he
ocean s u r f a ce in a s u b­
m e r s i b l e, s u b j e ct to 6 , 0 00
pounds of pressure per square
inch, makes each dive a per­
ilous venture.

wealthy and notable personal­
ities of the early 20th century.

along with original blueprints
of the luxury liner and other
important documents.

## PHOTOGRAPHY CONTEST

95&^ .
y jW l-L
The Tour of Somerville, NJ. Photo b;
Herbert It Washerman of Hollywood,
ith the Olympic Games
beginning next month,
many of us will be
watching intently as the
world '5 top athletes
compete. But who are
the champions in your
life ? Most of us don V
know an Olympic star, of course. But
we've got plenty of other winners.
And this year we want to see yours—
with an entry in the Parade/Kodak
Champions '96 Photography Contest.
Whom should you photograph? It
could be a friend with a prize-winning
pie—or even, perhaps, the county pie-
eating champ. Your company s softball
team, maybe the worst in the league,
also could make a good picture. The
possibilities, like the range of "champs,"
are as limitless as your imagination.
So don
r
t delay! Keep your camera
loaded and ready to go. You could
take home $100, and your picture
could be seen by millions. That
championship photo may just be yours!

# Limited supply of commemorative U.S.


# Flags and Flag Pins available at I /2 price


# to help America's united campaign


# Each flag and flag pin is a historic collectable inscribed with "America


# United Sept. ll, 2001" and "Operation Enduring Freedom Oct. 7, 2001"

By LAURA FISHER
) Media News Service
Many United States citizens have pur­
chased Hags and flag pins to show we are
united against the terrorists who attacked
our great nation. But most stores ran out of
flags and flag pins leaving a large number
of citizens without.
Also, the flags and flag pins that are sold
in stores do not commemorate this histori­
cal event.
The board of Ryben Collectables, a
major U.S. company, has sanctioned a spe­
cial offering of their supply of collectable,
commemorative U.S. flags and flag pins at
~ half price to fill this void and commemo­
rate this historic event.
Ryben Collectables Executive Director,
Timothy Ditty said, “We think it is very
. critical that the nation display the flag and
flag pins to show our enemies that we are
united and to boost the nation’s morale.
Unlike other flags and flag ornaments, our
flags and flag pins are commemorative.

# Sjx 6.jnch

by g.jnCh commemorative The exclusive commemorative 14Kt. Gold-plated U.S. flag pin. The flag part of the flag
Ryben Collectable's exclusive commem-

## f|agS on p0je

holders. Pin ls the same tyP*that's worn President George W. Bush and Congress.
orative U.S. flags and U.S. flag pins are
not available in stores or from any other
source.
All flags and pins contain the following
. inscription: “America United Sept. ll,
2(K)l’’ and “Operation Enduring Freedom
Oct. 7, 2001.” Operation Enduring
Freedom is the official name for the
United States’ and it's allies' war on ter­
rorism which officially started on October
7, 2001. The flags are inscribed on the
binders where they are secured to poles.
Ditty explained, “Ryben Collectables is a
* division of one of the largest and oldest
home shopping services in the world. We
have been in business for over 33 years.
But, most people were unaware of our col­
lectable, commemorative flags and pins.
This is because our division caters to an
exclusive clientele which is limited in
- number.”
Ditty said, “Ryben Collectables is mak-
• ing this special offer to the public while
supply lasts.”
The special offer is a collectable, com­
memorative U.S. Flag and Flag Pin Kit at
one half their regular low prices. The kit
consists of: six 6-inch by 9-inch flags on
pole holders, four 12-inch by 18-inch flags
; on pole holders, one 3-foot by 5-foot large
! flag for outdoor or wall display and one
exclusive 14 Kt. Gold-plated U.S. flag pin.
I The flag part of the flag pin is the same
; type that is worn by President George W.
' Bush and Congress. All of the flags are on P0*® bo,<*ers-
; made of 100% wear resistant and weather-
• proof polyester.
Ditty said. “These commemorative U.S.
flags and flag pins will become treasured
and valuable collectables in the future.
They commemorate one of the most his­
toric events in United States history.”
All the items in this kit total $58 in reg-
; ular price. Today, with the Special
! Reader’s Discount Coupon on this page,
; you can get all the items for only $29. All
I Ryben Collectable products carry a 100%
; satisfaction, full money back refund guar-
• arnee.
Important: Because Ryben Collectables
•. wants as many households as possible to
have these collectable, commemorative
flags and flag pins, there is a strict limit of
two (2) kits per household - no exceptions
*■>
please.
; •' If your order is received after the supply
!: is exhausted, every cent of your money
; * will be returned promptly in 24 hours.

# 168




## WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 17, 2001 *


# War against tenurism


## DAILY HERALD SECTION 1 PAGE 7


# Man arrested in probe


# applied to be truck driver

. A former Boston cabbie arrested in
the terrorism investigation applied
for a driving job with a suburban
Chicago trucking company, a com-
pany official confirmed Tuesday.
Nabil Al-Marabh, 34, applied for
the job on Aug. 20 but was turned
down, the executive said, the U.S.
Customs Service has discovered fir
nancial transactions between al-
Marabh and Raed Hijazi, a suspect in
the failed plot to kill Americans in Jor-
dan during the millennium celebra-
tion, law enforcement officials have
told The Associated Press.
"He was driving on a ticket," said
Michael Parise, president and owner
of Container Express Inc. of Summit.
Such a job would have given Al-
Marabh, who had a Michigan com-
mercial driver's license that enabled
him to haul hazardous materials, ac-
cess to a truck. Authorities have ex-
pressed concern about so-called haz-
mat licenses for terrorists who might
use them to haul explosives.
Parise said the FBI had looked into
AI-Marabh's application for the
truck drivingjob. But FBI officials de-
clined to comment.
"I cannot comment on any ongo-
ing investigation, which is what this
Nabil Al-Marabh
i s ," F BI
spokesman Ross
Rice. said.
Al-Mara'bh,
who was on the
list of people the
FBI wanted to
question after the
terrorist- attacks,
was » arrested in
: •'; Burbank la'st,
month on an Immigration and Natu-
raJizatioa Service request and a
Massachusetts warrant issued .in
March charging him with a proba-
tion violation involving a stabbing.
He was transferred to New York after
several days of questioning.
Al-Marabh was, caught by U.S.;
border authorities trying to enter the
United States with a fake Canadian
passport in June, officials said. He
was handed ovpr to Canadian au-
thorities and'later released on bail
posted by his uncle. Me failed to show
upforhisSept. 13 court appearance,
officials said. • ,
About two weeks after the attacks,
police in Toronto searched three res-
idences and business linked to Al-
Marabh.
Parise said he spoke with Ai-
Marabh for only five or 10 minutes.

# DA VINCI'S MOST DEBATED AND FAMOUS


# WORK OF ART NOW AVAILABLE TO READERS


# OF THIS PAPER FOR THE NEXT 72 HOURS


# One of the most well-known and most valued paintings in the world

By B.J.Peters, Media Services
You can now be the one to
examine your very own canvas
reproduction of this phenomenal
. painting and decide for yourself
what all the debate is about.
Yes, you can now receive
your very own numbered limit-
ed edition Last Supper work of
art, plus 2 FREE items.
As you know, much deliber-
ation is taking place today re-
garding the interpretation of
Leonardo da Vinci's famous
work of art. So, to help you
decide for yourself what is
true or untrue, along with
your numbered limited edition
Last Supper work of art, we
will send you 2 FREE items.
Your first FREE item is a
copy of "Questions and Dis-
crepancies in Leonardo's Depic-
tion of The Last Supper." You
will have everything you need
to tell all your friends and fami-
ly members what the facts are.
Some of the important topics
of discussion are: What does the
clothing worn by the much-
debated figure next to Jesus
suggest? - What is the meaning
of the knife shown in this fa-
mous picture? - There are 13
people in the painting. Why are
there only 12 drinking cups? -
What is Judas holding in his
hand? - Why are Jesus and the
disciples shown seated on one
side of a long extended table?
Discover the answers to
these questions, separate fact
from fiction and more in your
FREE copy of "Questions and
Discrepancies in Leonardo's
da Vinci's Depiction of The
Last Supper".
Very little is known about the
events that took place at the last
supper because it was such a se-
cretive dinner just among
friends. So, Leonardo's amazing
4-ycar work of art came from
his own imagination and the
gospels according to Matthew,
Mark, Luke and John. This was
all he had to go on.
You see, The Last Supper
was originally a mural painting
by Leonardo da Vinci for the
patron Duke Lodovico Sforza.
And now it is one of the most
well known, most valued and
most debated paintings in the
world.
Your second FREE item is
your own reference copy of this
famous mural with the names of
each disciple clearly printed on
it as depicted by Leonardo da
Vinci. With this handy refer-
ence as set forth by da Vinci's
original notes, you will always
know how Leonardo individual-
ly portrayed each of the 12
disciples.
Just imagine the thrill of
owning such a magnificent
piece. Of course, The Last Sup-
per has never been privately
owned since it cannot be
moved. But, now you can have
all of that wonderful beauty be-
cause you have been entrusted
to receive one of the linest, most
exceptional canvas reproduc-
tions in the World.
Created with the most exquis-
ite attention to detail, profes-
sionally malted and framed by
hand with magnificent crafts-
manship, your beautiful Canvas
Reproduction is a true, original

## THE LAST SUPPER by Leonardo da Vinci

One of the world's most cherished paintings of all time, this work of art depicts the last meal of Jesus
Christ, just prior to His crucifixion. This oil painting was immediately embraced and loved around the
world because Da Vinci's talent truly captured the most realistic depiction of Christ's disciples and the
emotions on their faces.
No one since Da Vinci has ever been able to recreate this unforgettable moment, making this fine
World Famous Art reproduction on canvas a must have for everyone. A meaningful collector's item
to pass down through your family for generations, The Last Supper on canvas measures 16" by 24"
and features a rich goldtone frame.
masterpiece.
The history of this very popu-
lar and influential painting of
the Renaissance Era is quite re-
markable. Leonardo was a
painter, draftsman, sculptor, ar-
chitect and engineer whose ge-
nius led him to try many new
and different methods.
Thus he chose to paint this
exquisite masterpiece on a
dry plaster wall using a new
technique. Unfortunately his
technique proved to be a disas-
ter since this famous painting
started to deteriorate just a few
years after he finished it back in
1498. Because of this, at least 5
different painters have re-
touched the painting during the
past 500 years.
At asix.cof 29 feet by 15.1
feet, it took Leonardo 4 years to
paint his interpretation of The
l^axl Supper on the back wall of
the dining hall at the convent of
Santa Maria dcllc Grazic in Mi-
lan, Italy.
The esteemed Leonardo da
Vinci decided to paint the scene
of The I-Mst Supper at the exact
moment when Jesus announced
that one of his disciples would
betray him as staled in John
13:21 of the Bible. Each figure
in the painting has a different
reaction to the news as shown in
their expressions of horror,
shock, anger or surprise. Until
this version of The Last Supper
by Leonardo, other artists had
painted a serene quiet setting.
Please understand after our
very limited edition World Fa-
mous Art reproductions arc
claimed by their designated
owners, they will NEVER be re-
leased by us again. This beauti-
ful work of art will be retired to
our secured Gallery vaults
forever!
In all the shimmering,
sparkling light and tone, the
beauty ol this painting is truly
priceless. Something that was
once only a vision in the artist's
mind is now displayed in mag-
nificent colors.
There is nothing like the thrill
ol walking into a room and see-
ing an exquisite canvas repro-
duction on the wall. So you do
not want to miss out.
Much deliberation is taking
place today regarding the inter-
pretation of Leonardo's famous
-work. Now you can examine
this reproduction in the comfort
of your own home and decide
for yourself.
Respond today, this very
moment, before someone else
claims the canvas artwork
that could have been yours.
This extraordinary piece of
art will never be available
again from the United States
Commemorative Gallery.
Q: Is it true that readers can
view the Last Supper Risk
Free for 90 days at home?
A: At the United States
Commemorative Gallery, we
encourage you to experience
how wonderful and beautiful
this canvas reproduction really
is in your home, risk-free, and
we will even include free ship-
ping when order is placed
within 72 hours.
If you respond within the
next 72 hours you arc entitled to
a $20.00 discount and FREE
shipping and handling. Please
sec coupon on this page for ad-
ditional information.

## SCIENTIFIC ADVANCEMENT GIVES GENERAL PUBLIC ONE OF THE FINEST TECHNOLOGICAL DEVICES AVAILABLE FOR THE RELIEF OF KNEE PAIN


# GET IMMEDIATE RELIEF FROM KNEE PAIN WITHOUT PHARMACEUTICAL DRUGS

Hy: S. HulTord. Media Resources
The finest technological device
available for the relief of knee pain is
now being released lo the general public.
Known as The KnecdlT Pain
Relieving Knee Band, ibis device is
the first of its kind.
This amazing new pain relieving
band was designed by ihc renowned
orthopedic surgeon and sports medi-
cine specialist, Donald Farced, M.D.
As a long-lime consultant to fa-
mous athletes, members of the Davis
Cup and oihcr professional sports
teams. Dr. Farced successfully de-
signed KnecdlT based on 20 years of
clinical observation and treatment of
Repetitive Stress Injuries (RSIs).
Now Dr. Farced is bringing
welcome pain relief where so many
people need it, in ihe most delicate
area of your leg, your knee.
The KncedIT Pain Relieving
Knee Band is an innovative and
unique device scientifically designed
to assist in the relief of knee pain
commonly associated with Arthritis,-
Tcndonitis and Chondromalacia.
ll provides this breakthrough
pain relief by gently absorbing force
at the knee and by exerting concen-
trated compression and warmth
across the soft tissue in the front part
of your knee.
Its patcnied, revolutionary tech-
nology is a superior Ihcrapy featuring
a unique, non-constricti vc design of
cushioned ncoprcnc curved to the
shape of your knee.
, The KneedIT Pain Relieving
Knee Band is a treatment, not a
tourniquet.
The product is one of the only
specially designed devices which
works with the human anaiomy to aid
in the relief of knee pain without cut-
ting off circulation, hampering move-

# Officially Licensed New York State Quarters


# Featuring the Pride of the Yankees are Now


# Being Released to Local Sports Fans.


# Readers can claim this exciting collection for only the


# next 10-days as quantities are not expected to last long.


# All 16 uncirculated State Quarters are in Full Color.

THE HOTIEST SPORTS COLLECTIBLE TODAY
Readers of this Special Release Feature are now able to
request the United States Commemorative Gallery's only Offi
cially Licensed Baseball Quarter Collection to honor the true
Dynasty of the Major Leagues.
Fans everywhere are talking about the high level of athletes
that have been acquired by the New York Yankees over the
past few seasons.
Never before in Baseball history has there been a team with
so much All-Star Talent. Having a potential All-Star at every
position has become the Yankees unmatched recipe for suc
cess. Each and every year they invest in various Superstars in
hopes of winning their 27th World Series Championship.
HONORING THE MOST SUCCESSFUL TEAM
IN MAJOR LEAGUE HISTORY
The New York Yankees are without a doubt the most suc
cessful team in history. Since 1903, they have won 26 World
Series Championships and 39 American League Pennants. By
far the most in the Majors.
The Yankees have also bred some of the greatest athletes to
ever play the game. Hall of Famers like Ruth, DiMaggio and
Mantle were the building blocks for baseball's only Dynasty.
However it's the current Superstars like Jeter, Rodriguez,
Williams and Torre that have to continue the winning tradition.
For the first time in sports history the All-Stars of the New York
Yankees can be collected using official U.S. Minted State Quar
ters. These Officially Licensed, fully colored New York State
Quarters feature the 16 most prominent members of the 2004
New York Yankees:
MILLIONS TRY To COLLECT STATE QUARTERS
You may not be aware that over 130 Million people are cur
rently trying to get their hands on U.S. Minted State Quarters.
That means there will only be a limited quantity of State Quar
ters highlighted with detailed Full Color. Therefore, it is impor
tant for you to request your favorite All-Star Player now because
no one can be sure how many of these valued quarters will be
produced.
This superb collection of Officially Licensed New York State
Quarters will be issued' directly to you in the Gallery's Official
Wooden Display Case. This allows you to showcase your com
plete collection for every baseball fan to admire. Plus, it includes·
special Protective Capsules that will keep each valued, full color
State Quarter in mint condition.
SAVE 40% OFF REGULAR PRICE
IMPORTANT: You must call Toll-Free 1-800-867-9993 or
return the 2004 Yankees Superstar Discount Claim Form within
1 O days of reading this notification to receive OVER 40% OFF
the regular price of $224 plus $9 Shipping and Handling. This
allows you to get the complete New York Yankees Superstar
State Quarter Collection for the special discount price of only
$141. Plus, you will receive FREE engraving along with FREE
Shipping and Handling when you make your request over the
phone.
These Legal Tender Statehood Quarter Dollars produced by
the United States Mint have been one of the biggest sensations
in the history of coin collecting. And now that they feature the
Superstar Players of the New York Yankees, it is assured that
their popularity will continue to rise substantially in the future.
Remember, each New York State Quarter Dollar was minted
for only 10 weeks in 2001. You must also realize that no more
will ever be minted. That is another reason why these Officially
Licensed State Quarters are so valued. ·
And since they were only minted for 10 weeks, we cannot
guarantee exactly how many uncirculated New York State Quar
ters will be carefully detailed in Full Color. Therefore, you must
act now to get the hottest collection that U.S. Coin collectors and
Baseball Fans everywhere are trying to get their hands on. They

# Ohio Man Discovers The


# Secret of How to


# The American Rat Race


# 7 Step System Nets This Working Man Without


# Any Money Or Experience $145,000 In First Year

By John Whitehead,
Special Features Writer
• (Canton, OH) The secret to making
a quick fortune in America has been
reduced to a simple 7 step system by
an entrepreneur from Canton, Ohio. It
requires little or no money, a minimum
of time and no elaborate plant or equip­
ment. In fact, you can do it in your
home or anywhere.
There are hundreds of rags to rich­
es stories each year in America.
However, this one is very unique.
• Most successful get-rich ventures
were spin-offs of a former business or
occupation. Or, someone was in the .
right place at the right time. You don't
usually find a guy getting rich who was
working for a big company, had no
money, had no experience outside his
specialized job at the company, and did
not even know where he was going to
start. You also don't find many people
making this much money this quick.
• The man who did it is Ben Suarez
who is a real, living, breathing human
being living in Canton, Ohio, the Pro
Football Hall of Fame city.
; As you fly into this medium sized
Midwestern town you can see that it's
a compact community surrounded by
farmland. As you drive from Akron-
Canton Airport on the I-77 Freeway
into town you can see the Pro Football
Hall of Fame just off the freeway to the
right. The Hall of Fame is situated in a
complex which also houses the city's
biggest high school, a field house, one
of the biggest natatoriums in the nation
and one of the biggest high school
football stadiums in the country, the
20,000-seat concrete-constructed
Fawcett Stadium. This is the same sta­
dium where ABC's Frank Gifford, Al
Michaels and Dan Dierdorf broadcast
the Hall of Fame Game each year.
; Suarez's neighbors here in Canton
call it a movie script. One of the neigh­
bors interviewed had this to say: "It
was unbelievable. One day he's driving
around in a rusted out Pontine station
wagon, living in an uncarpeted house
that didn't even have a color T. V., and
struggling to make ends meet like the
rest of us. The next day lie's driving in
a brand new luxury car, a luxury
motorltome. his house is like a palace,
and he's traveling all over the coun­
try."
A close friend of Suarez's com­
ments: "When he first started saying
he was going to think up a way to get
rich quick we all laughed and thought
he was crazy. Man we're not laughing
anymore. He wouldn't tell everybody
haw he was doing it for 3 years. I knew
he would eventually. Everyone who
makes a big accomplishment sooner or
later has to tell everyone how he did
it:':
' So, how was all this accomplished.
For that answer I went right to the
horse's mouth, my main interview with
the creator of the system, Ben Suarez.
QUESTION: OK, Ben, so how in
the world did this all start.
' 'ANSWER: I was typical of a good
majority of Americans — working at a
job for 10 years that I hated, worrying
about layoffs, living from pay to pay
with no money to do anything except
buy the bare essentials. Work held little
chance for advancement. Big compa­
nies are immovable and impersonal.
I found out quick that moving up
had little to do with talent and hard
work and more to do with politics,
lodge membership, being a friend or
relative of a high executive, or riding
on the coattails of an executive and
hoping he ascends the organization. I
wanted out but I saw no way to escape
the proverbial rat race.
Then one Monday morning a trau­
matic last straw came. I had invented a
solution to a problem in 3 months that
the company's research division had
been trying to come up with for 5
years. I was told that morning that my
solution was being shelved because in
so many words it had "shown up" the
research division.
the whole premise on which this coun­
try was built. As my brother
approached, I turned to him and said,
"Rick, I don't know how I am going to
do it or what I am going to do but I'm
going into something for myself and
become independently rich." That
point was the start of it all.
QUESTION: Excellent point. Is
this when you developed your sys­
tem?
ANSWER: No, that came a little
later — after some, hard knocks trying
to do things the orthodox way. I tried
to start the standard types of businesses
that require buying buildings, equip­
ment, hiring people, etc. But, since I
had no money I had to try to borrow to
do this. I soon found out that banks
don't lend you money for new busi­
nesses or buying established businesses
for that matter. The only time banks
lend you money is when you don't
need it. The fact is that no one is going
to lend you money to start a business. I
tried to start a couple small businesses
that could be started by renting the
necessities but wound up losing money
and owing the money for the rentals,
which I didn't have.
Then I started to buy the "get rich
books." I quickly found out that these
are nothing but franchise schemes, sell­
ing jobs or stupid real estate deals that
lake you forever to make money.
I was also learning another impor­
tant fact. When you work a full-time
job, it doesn't leave you time for much
else. When you consider preparation
time to go to work, driving time, lunch,
the 8 hours on the job, and cleaning up
and unwinding when you get home,
that takes 12 hours out of your day.
With 8 hours of sleep that only leaves
you 4 hours out of the day plus week­
ends. And that time usually must be
spent maintaining the house and car,
and spending some time with the fami-
ly.
I'll never forget that afternoon
when I came home from work. A few
of my relatives and some neighbors
were coming over to my house for a
basketball game in my driveway. My
brother was the first to arrive. I was
standing in the middle of the drive
bouncing the basketball, dejected and
pondering my future. I started thinking
of applying elsewhere for a job. Then a
thought struck me. "Jobs, get a good
job, a better job, create more jobs," is
all you hear. My God, has everyone
forgotten this is the land of free enter­
prise, the land of opportunity. That's
;
So I was faced with this cold hard
fact. If the typical American in my
condition is going to escape the rat
race, he will have to do it with a
method requiring very little time and
money, no experience or skills and the
system must make a lot of money
quickly. On the surface it sounded
impossible, to make an understatement.
But, when you're desperate you find
that necessity is indeed the mother of
invention.
Then another thing spurred me on.
I read an article by the Social Security
Administration. It related, "out of
every 100 persons reaching,65 years
old: 50 are flat broke, 30 are dead, 15
have a little money and only 5 are
rich." I wasn't going to work 40 years
and end up in a "home," flat broke.
QUESTION: So this is when you
came tip with the big system?
ANSWER: Yes, but there is a little
more to it. I pondered and researched
how to do it for months, always run­
ning into stone walls. They say your
subconscience does all your heavy cre­
ative work. It does. One night of all
times when I was lifting weights in my
basement the solution rolled out of my
mind like a computer printout. It was
the simplest, fastest and most direct
way to make a lot of money. I quickly
wrote it down and after analyzing it,
the system boiled down to a few simple
rules or steps.
You use these steps to assemble
what I call a Net Profit Generation
System or N.P.G.S. for short. That
night I stayed up late and assembled
N.P.G.S.-1.
I tested N.P.G.S.-1. It took about 2
weeks, but it failed miserably. But.
even though it failed I could see what
made it fail was something not that
severe. It simply had a few bugs to be
worked out. I modified the system and
assembled N.P.G.S.-2.
N.P.G.S.-2 made a significant turn
around in the system and it almost
broke even. But. it still lost money. I
refined the system again and assembled
N.P.G.S.-3. N.P.G.S.-3 took a Ionnore
time than I expected, about 6 months,
to work out the bugs left in the system.
But. N.P.G.S.-3 was a success making
a better than fair amount of money.
But. not enough, and hardly enough to
pay off the huge debt I had incurred by
now. However. I could now see the pot
of gold at the end of the rainbow. 1
then assembled a more perfected sys­
tem, N.P.G.S.-4.1 had a feeling that
N.P.G.S.-4 would make it big, but I
also knew that, nothing is totally pre­
from the research in developing the
prior systems. If N.P.G.S.-4 failed I
would be $70,000 in debt and no way
to repay.
QUESTION: That must have
been a tense moment waiting for the
results?
ANSWER: It was real life drama
at its highest intensity.
It was a muggy mid-August morn­
ing when I waited for the results to
come in. I would know that day if it
worked or not.
The phone rang with the results. I
stood there with sweaty palms and my
heart in my mouth. The news?
N.P.G.S.-4 was a smashing success!
The first rollout paid off all my
debts with money left over.
QUESTION: Incredible. When
did you start making your first big
money for yourself?
ANSWER: Very shortly after­
ward. I projected a way to do big roll­
outs without having to put up any
money. My second rollout of N.P.G.S.-
4 netted my first big payday. I made
$80,000 clear!'
QUESTION: I can't even imag­
ine getting that much money all at
once. What was it like?
ANSWER: It's almost impossible
to verbalize. My wife and I just stared
at the check for a long time. We had a
two-day celebration with a dinner and
party. It's a fantastic experience to go
into work and tell them you quit. 1 had
a little fun doing it. The chance to say
what you truly feel to people who have
had you under their thumb for 10 years .
is truly a pleasant release of frustration.
The first thing I did was pay off all
my bills. Then I paid off my house. I
took the money to the savings and loan
company, who held the mortgage per­
sonally to. see the looks on their faces.
The teller did a double take. The man?
ager came out and smiled insincerely
and said, "We don't get too many peo­
ple paying off a $20,000 mortgage all
at one time." Then we had a "burn the
mortgage", party. Then I went to the
showroom of the local luxury car deal­
er and laid down the cash for a new
car. I never had a new car in my life. I
never even had the experience of a new
car even as a kid at home.
I can still remember driving it from
the dealer and the new smell. When I
got home I picked up the hood and
looked at the gleaming new engine
without a speck of dirt or grease.
Then my wife and I went on a
shopping spree for clothes. We had
been making do with old clothes for so
long they had become worn out, She
always had to shop for clothes with
very little money. I always joked with
her that "If I gave you $5,000 to spend
on clothes could you do it in one day?"
She used to say "I could do it in a cou­
ple hours." So that's what I did. I gave
her $5,000 to go out and buy clothes. It
ended up taking all day and she said
she relished every minute.
We then took a long trip. In New
dictable. I then roiled out with
N.P.G.S.-4.
I still owed a great deal of money
York I bought her a large diamond at
Tiffany's — another thing we always
dreamed about.
But I'll tell you the money was not
the most important benefit in this.
What money really does is give you
precious time. Time to be with your
wife, to be with your children, since
they grow up before you know it, and
time to do things you really want to do.
Another priceless thing you gain is
your self- respect. No more crawling
and kneeling because you're depen­
dent. The words of Frank Sinatra's
song "My Way" says it all.
QUESTION: This definitely
could be a movie script. How did
things go after that?
ANSWER: Excellent. The first
year I assembled two more systems
and made a total of $ 145,000.
QUESTION: You've been at it
for 20 years now. Were the succeed­
ing years as good?
ANSWER: Better. The amount I
made increased each year and in time I
was making millions per year. I have
audited financial statements from certi­
fied public accountants and income tax
returns to verify this to any agency or
investigator of any kind who wishes to
challenge me on this. My enterprise,
SCI, is also listed in Dunn and
Bradstreet.
QUESTION: That's quite a sys­
tem. I understand you tell everyone
how vou did it in vour book.
ANSWER: Yes. The first book
was published a while back and now I
have a new book which is much better.
QUESTION: What can you tell

me about the new book?
ANSWER: It's called "7 Steps to
Freedom II, How to Escape the
American Rat Race." It contains the
complete step-by-step details on how
to assemble a N.P.G.S. system which
can be done in just 7 simple steps. Here
are some highlights of the qualities of
the system:
• The system requires a minimum of
time and money to start.
• You don't need special experience or
skills to do it.
• You don't need to buy or rent build­
ings, buy or rent equipment and you
don't have to hire people.
• You can do it anywhere — in your
house, while you travel or a favorite
vacation spot or retreat.
• With the system you can earn
$10,000 to $500,000 in a matter of
months, in days and even faster
depending on which system you
assemble.
• The money you make is all earned
income which is subject to one of the
lowest tax bites.
My hew book is much better than
the first because of many new
advances in the system I developed
over the past years since the first book
was published. I also show you how to
use a personal computer, if you wish,
to make the system even faster and eas­
ier. But you can also do it without a
personal computer.
QUESTION: Did you say you
can make big money within days?
ANSWER: No, that's an under­
statement. You can actually do it with­
in hours in some cases. You can
assemble a system in the morning and
start having spendable money in your
hands produced by the system in 2
hours that afternoon!
QUESTION: How much money
do you average on a system?
ANSWER: My average now is
about $750,000.00. That's the type I
usually stick to. I'll tell you this, doing
a system to make $10,000 to $20,000
is literally duck soup. I have these
available all the time but don't even
use them.
QUESTION: You said you need
no special talent, training or experi­
ence. Who all can do it?
ANSWER: Just about anyone.
First on special talent, I really don't
know if that exists. In my experience I
have found people are as talented as
they think they are. The benefit about
this system is the skull drudgery and
sense of pride in your work and the ANSWER: If it was priced at what

A number of years ago I taught one of
the earliest and crudest forms of the
system to my brother and brother-in-
law. At that time my brother was 19

# 174



2 People/Section I

## Daily Herald

Wednesday, June 15,1994

## PEOPLE


# Locals take fizz out of


# new humorous beer ads

Beer companies are strange.
But they add to the comic mix of
human life and we would be not
nearly as amused by the world if
they did not exist.
Take Old Style.
It is brewed in Lacrosse in
northwestern Wisconsin. There
are six grain elevators in a
bunch on the Old Style property
up there and they are painted to
resemble six cans of Old Style in
a six pack. This is a landmark in
the area, bigger than other Wis­
consin biggies like The House
On the Rock and The World’s
Largest Penny. They are all
very funny tourist attractions
rendered more humorous by the
fact the locals don’t get it.
Old Style sells very well in the
Chicago area, though its share
of the market is not as great as
it once was.
In a bold move to identify itself
more closely with Chicago, the
brewery hired an ad agency and
the agency thought it would be
the height of hilarity to run a se­
ries of billboards throughout the
urbanscape depicting historic
Chicago figures endorsing Old
Style.
The first was Alphonse Cap-
one.
A billboard went up in Melrose
Park — pronounced “mel-
ROSE” by the way — showing
Big Al and proclaiming back in
1929, Big Al persuaded his
friends to try Old Style.
This is not true, of course.
Beermaking was illegal and Big
made his own beer and it
didn’t have anything to do with
Lacrosse. But what the hey,
that’s the joke of the thing and
we all know a joke is much fun­
nier when the locals don’t get it,
like the giant Old Style six-pack
in Lacrosse.
So what happened?
Sun-Times columnist Ray Cof­
fey waxed wroth about the slur
on the city (not Melrose Park)
and the Chicago mayor, bless

## Bill


# J


## Granger „ *'

his heart, chimed in with his
own critique. In the new, sani­
tized Chicago, there is selective
memory. Al Capone’s Chicago,
with organized crime both in­
side and outside of city hall,
does not exist, nor did it, and be­
sides, why bring it up anyway?
Why, indeed? If you want to
sell beer to a populace, begin by
making fun of them. Put up bill­
boards recalling Prohibition
when the submachine gun was
the rule of law and beer-drinking
was illegal. That’s certainly the
way I d sell beer. It is the equiv­
alent of going to Lacrosse and
baiting a bar full of flannel shirts
by calling them “cheeseheads ”
They will enjoy the joke on
them, I guarantee you.
Other historic figures the ad
agency plans to use include the
woeful Shoeless Joe Jackson.
Whether he did it or not, he was
banned from baseball for life af­
ter being accused of taking part
in the infamous Black Sox scan­
dal in which the 1919 World Se­
ries was thrown by the White
Sox for money.
What are we to get from Shoe­
less Joe? Possibly, “Back in
1919,1 would have fixed a game
for a six-pack of Old Style.”
Beer is funny and beermaking
is a funny business. Breweries
have produced great ads like
those old Mickey Spillane things
for Lite beer and incredibly stu­
pid stuff like the Bud Bowl. Old
Style now has given us the Six
Pack That Ate Lacrosse and Al
Capone. Not to mention media
self-righteousness and mayoral
revisionism. All in one shot. As
the cheeseheads say, it don’t get
no better dan dis, heh?

# Rioting may help spur


# soccer interest in U.S.

While surveys show that most
Americans are unaware that
World Cup soccer will be held in
nine U.S. cities this week, in­
terest ought to perk up once the
rioting begins.
Violence is not unknown at
American sporting events, but it
is rare.
Violence and soccer, however,
go hand in hand. (Or foot in
toot.)
The U.S. Defense Department
is spending $15 million on secu­
rity for the games, an amount
many countries consider laugh-
ablv small.
But Alan Rosenberg, chair­
man of World Cup U S A., orga­
nizer of the event, is not worried
in the least.
“I think there s been a huge
overemphasis on security,” he
said.
Rosenberg plans to start a
pro soccer league in 12 Ameri­
can cities next year, and so he
wants nothing to give soccer a
black eye.
Like fences. In other coun­
tries, soccer fields are com­
monly enclosed by fences in or­
der to keep fans from harming
the players, the officials, the po­
lice and others.
And Dallas and Washington
have erected fences around
their soccer fields.
But Rosenberg is against
this.
“If you treat people like ani­
mals, they’ll act like animals,”
he has said.
To which I say: “It’s time to

## Roger


## Simon

wake up and smell the pepper
gas, Al!”
While it is true that people
sometimes get crushed against
fences, that is not what worries
Rosenberg.
He is worried that fences look
bad.
But consider the histoiy of
soccer violence: On April 15,
1989, in Sheffield, England, a
soccer riot left 95 dead and 200
injured.
On May 29,1985, in Brussels, a
soccer riot (started by English
fans) left 39 dead.
“But outside of England, and
they’re not coming, there hasn’t
been exporting of hooliganism to
World Cup matches,” Rosen­
berg said.
“We just don’t expect it” he
added.
But he should expect it. Be­
cause although England is not
coming here, Argentina is.
“Argentines are in a slightly
scary class of their own,” Se
Los Angeles Times reported re­
cently.
“They’ve all but insti­
tutionalized soccer hooliganism,
and even Se most peaceful fans
bliSely toss bottles and cherry
bombs.”

## Celebrities host benefit


## for Walden Paid area

BOSTON — Henry David
Thoreau probably wouldn’t have
been able to afford a ticket. Still,
Jack Nicholson, Ted Danson and
Don Henley drew more than 600
guests who each paid $250 to save
some land celebrated by the
philosopher of simplicity. The
event staged Monday night at the
Park Plaza Hotel was organized
by the Walden Woods Project,
which is conserving land around
Walden Pond. Thoreau lived in a
cabin by the pond in Concord
from July 4,1845, to Sept. 6,1847,
and wrote about it in his 1854 clas­
sic “Walden.”

## Appeals court: Merrick


## can’t be a forced father

lawyer, Alan Dershowitz, had ar­
gued that a man couldn’t be legal­
ly compelled to be a father.
Lawyers for the children and Mrs
Merrick said the children would
lose lots of money if Merrick did
not complete the adoption.
ALBANY, N Y. — With Father’s
Day looming. Broadway producer
David Merrick was spared a close
call with unwanted paternity
Tuesday when the state’s highest
court ruled he cannot be forced to
adopt two children. The New York
Court of Appeals ruled against

## AT&T hires Houston


## for 1-year ad camj

Ii?I ill
NEW YORK — Speaking of en­
hanced voices, AT&T has signed
Whitney Houston to promote its
new voice enhancement service.
The long-distance phone compa
ny said Tuesday that the five-time
Grammy Award winner has
signed a one-year contract to be
featured performer in its national
ad campaign for AT&T
TrueVoice. Precise monetary
notes struck in the deal were not
disclosed. AT&T TrueVoice,
which started last year, improves
sound quality on long-distance
calls. Commercials starring
Houston will air in July.

# Mancini, 70, leaves behind legacy of song

Reuters___________
LOS ANGELES — Four-time Os-
car-winner Henry Mancini, com­
poser of soundtracks for the “Pink
Panther” movies, “Days of Wine
and Roses” and dozens of other hit
films, died Tuesday at age 70.
He died at his Beverly Hills home
of complications from liver and pan­
creatic cancer, with his wife of 47
years, Ginny, at his bedside, his
spokeswoman said.
In addition to the four Academy
Awards, Mancini won 20 Grammy
awards and his work is found on
over 90 musically diverse albums.
He was diagnosed with inop­
erable cancer in February, but de­
voted his final days to working on
his first stage project, a Broadway
version of “Victor/Victoria.”
The movie, directed by his long-
Henry
Mancini
time collaborator The tall, balding musician re­
Blake Edwards, defined the Hollywood movie score
garnered Manci- during a career that spanned over
ni’s fourth and fi­ 50 years and involved work on more
nal Oscar for Best than IOO movies.
Original Song He won two Oscars in 1962 for his
Score in 1982. work on “Breakfast at Tiffany’s,” in­
Mancini worked cluding Best Song for “Moon Riv­
steadily in his fi­ er,” and another in 1963 for the
nal days, con­ score for “Days of Wine and Roses.”
tributing com­ His creativity extended to con­
plete musical ducting symphony orchestras in
scores for such 1990s movies as over 50 engagements annually.
“Ghost Dad,” “Switch” and last He also worked on projects with
year’s “Son of the Pink Panther.” Pavarotti, Johnny Mathis, as well as
In an emotional birthday charity Irish flautist James Galway with
gala in April in Los Angeles, such whom he went on tour in 1985.
stars as Luciano Pavarotti, Dudley Mancini first came to prominence
Moore, Julie Andrews and Quincy with his theme for the TV series
Jones performed and paid tribute. “Peter Gunn,” which won two
When not steeping himself in mu­ Grammys, including Album of the
sic, Mancini was an avid skier, pho­ Year in 1959.
tographer and art collector. His score for the detective series,
with its cool and driving jazz idiom
written for a small cornix) and keyed
to dramatic action, opened the door
for a new style of television.
One-third of the show’s fan mail
was addressed to Mancini.
He began work on movies in 1952
at Universal-International Studios’
music department where he con­
tributed to over IOO films over six
years, including “The Glenn Miller
Story” for which he received his first
Academy Award nomination in 1954
He went freelance in 1958 and
won his first two Oscars in 1962 for
“Breakfast at Tiffany’s” — Best
Song for the Audrey Hepburn-sung
“Moon River,” whose lyrics were
written by Johnny Mercer, and
Best Musical Score. The movie also
won him five Grammys, including
Song of the Year and Record of the
Year.

# Latest negotiations break off


# in Round Lake teachers strike

BY ERIC KROL
Daily Herald SUB Writer
The Hound Lake teachers strike
enters its eighth week today with
no new talks scheduled after nego-
tiations broke off early Sunday
mornng when union officials
walked away from the table claim-
ing they were yelled at by the
school board's attorney
The latest round of charges and
counter charges in the labor dis-
pute that saw teachers in Round
Lake I'nit District 116 go on strike
Oct 18 left the dispute no closer to
a settlement, officials from both
sides said
Federal mediators, called in last
week to help broker a settlement,
suggested the teachers offer a new
proposal to the school board as a
means of breaking the logjam over
a laundry list of salary and educa-
tional issues
The teachers union spent about
nine hours on Saturday revising
their offer and presented it to the
school board around midnight
But before any substantive dis-
cussion could take place, Robert
Trevarthen, an attorney for the
school board, began yelling at
teachers union negotiators for tak-
ing so long to write a proposal, said
Tern Zumbrook, president of the
Education Association of Round
Lake
"The outburst was completely
uncalled for in light of the hard
work we had done during the ses-
sion," Zumbrook said
But Round Lake school board
President James Hull said the
union's most recent offer was
essentially the same as its previ-
ous proposals.
"And there was no screaming or
yelling," Hull added
Zumbrook acknowledged that the
union's latest offer still proposes
salary hikes of 5, 5 and 6 percent,
as well as step increases based on
seniority, over three years
Hull said the school board is
sticking by its offer of a 12 percent
hike, including the step increases,
spread over three years.
But he also said the teachers
union must drop the handful of
unfair labor practice claims it has
filed with the state as part of any
settlement, a point Zumbrook said
the union cannot agree to
The school board has reopened
Round Lake High School for
juniors and seniors and kinder-
garten through third-grade classes
using a combination of substitutes,
administrators and teachers who
have crossed the picket line to
teach classes
Hult also said today marks the
last day teachers could have come
back without pushing the school
calendar past June 30

# Muses, doctors see living proof of hard work

Bv DE iDRA LAWHEAD
Daly Ha»M SUB Wnter
Eigr teen months ago, Marge and
Jim r oma were fearfully waiting
for the medical condition of their
only s irvrving triplet to improve
Today, Kevin, who was born
three weeks premature, is a
healthy, vibrant toddler eagerly
awaiting his second Christmas.
The Homas returned Sunday to
the W infield hospital and nurses
they say gave them the emotional
support necessary to get through
those Brst difficult months
The Carol Stream couple's son
was one of 240 former patients of
Central DuPage Hospital's inten-
sive care nursery, and their par-
ents, I o attend the nurser, 's annual
Christmas party
"It's nice; he can walk in and he's
OK," Jim Homa said. "All the time
he's in ICN, you're just worried
about him getting out and being
OK."
The Mth-annual party is
designed to give nurses and doc-
tors a chance to see the progress
the children they cared tor have
made. Parents also renew acquain-
tances with others who have
endured similar experiences.
'People come to see their nurses
and show off their babies and the
progress they have made over the
years," said Leslie Ryan, the man-
ager of the neonatal intensive care
unit "It's a thrill for the nurses to
see how they've grown and blos-
somed."

# DailyHeraid

Monday, December 5,1994

# Time will tell if Jim Bakker is new person

Former televangelist Jim Bakker
was released last week from federal
prison after spending five years
behind bars
Naperville resident Jerry Rose, a
Chicago televangelist who runs
WCFC-TV Channel 38 and knows
Bakker, thinks the disgraced
Assemblies of God clergyman
deserves another chance at the pul-
pit — only not on TV right away
"Jim Bakker has charisma, and he
still has a following," Rose said "If
he started a new TV ministry, it
could be very successful But Fm not
sure that's the best thing for him
That was the thing, the exposure
and fame, that played on his weak-
nesses."
Since Bakker has been in prison,
Rose has kept in touch by letter The
two have known one another since
the early 1970s when both worked
for the cable Christian Broadcasting
Network. Rose later served on
Bakker's Heritage USA board of
directors
"The most profound effect prison
has had on Jim Bakker is his isola-
tion," Rose said "He wasn't building
buildings and wasn't being chased
around by 14 people asking him to
make a decision
"He's spent his time praying to
God and coming to grips with who
he is and what he did. That's a luxu-
ry many people don't get Hopefully,
you'll see a different Jim Bakker "
Bikinis In January?
Richard Korber, who lives in
Arlington Heights, has become a
regular media gadfly of late, promot-
ing his idea of women ("girls," he
calls them) in string bikinis toweling
down cars in his planned Chicago
car wash
Without doubt, Korber's Bikini
Carwash Co has touched a nerve on
the city's Northwest Side In spite of

## Dave


## McKinney


## Who we are

the public outcry to his plan, Korber
said his idea is one that will sell big,
especially among suburbanites It'll
be visible right off the Kennedy
Expressway, near the Edens junc-
tion About 800,000 cars a day will
drive by
"You pick up any hot rod maga-
zine and on every front cover is a
pretty girl with a bikini standing
next to the car," he said "I'm trying
to make something where cars and
girls come together like that"
Critics think it'll tarnish the farmly-
onented neighborhood
"This will bring sleaze," Alderman
Carole Bialczak said "Our city has a
big problem with crime and a loss of
human dignity This is just another
way of saying we don't have any val-
ues"
Korber has all of the city permits
he needs to open the car wash,
which will have a tropical motif,
complete with a tank of live sharks
The women ("girls") will be working
in a balmy 75-degree environment
insulated from Chicago's harsh win-
ters
"We want very beautiful girls," he
said "We're not looking for normal-
looking girls or dancers from the
clubs "
Korber plans to charge patrons a
$20 annual membership fee and
then $20 for each car wash He said
he's not exploiting women ("girls")
"They won't be working odd hours
or be asked to get into special situa-
tions," Korber said "I don't believe
this is exploitation because the girls
are getting paid for what they're
doing " "If I had a sponsor, I'd buy what
they want to sell, lav it on the street
Burying Dahmer's memory
then roll over it with a crusher,"
Naperville resident Joe Roth is Roth said
amazed the families of Jeffrey

his belongings, then keep the take The research department at

dent Walter Knoebel, Roth spent to raise the price of a first-class
$7,300 to purchase 21 of Gacy's stamp by 3 cents will cost the aver
paintings and then gave the moms age household 84 cents more in
and dads and brothers and sisters a postage next year
dose of therapy a match That's not much until vou consider
Everything was set afire by Hallmark's estimates, that ilbil

# N ew book r e v e a ls t he real


# reasons you're g o i ng b r o ke


# and a sure way to a v o id it

By John Whitehead
(Canton, Ohio) Statistics for the
past 10 years show that you. the aver-
age American, are going broke.
You don't have to be told this. You
know that over this period you have
lost a lot of ground financially, and it
is getting worse. Why is it happening
and what can you do about it?
A new book, SuperBiz by Ben-
jamin D. Sware7, gives the two real
reasons you're going broke, and, a
tried and tested way to reverse the
trend immediately.
You are told by the government
and media that the biggest reason you
are going broke is because of inflation
caused by deficit government spend-
ing. That's nol true. Government defi-
rii spending is a contributing factor.
but, it is not the biggest reason you
are going broke. It is not even the
second biggest reason.
Also, they haven't told you how
bad it is going to get. Instead of wor-
rying about not being able to afford
luxuries, the average American will
soon be struggling just to eat and
survive! Financial wipe-outs will
become commonplace.
You don't have to be among the
unforlunate struggling to survive. In
fact, you can live in comfortable lux-
ury during these hard times Here's
how.
SuperBiz gives the following as the
two biggest reasons you're going
broke.
1. There is a well camouflaged,
underground organization within fed-
eral and state governments that is
destroying the American economy.
This organization suppresses and sabo-
tages new advanced products and

and taxation?

ernment providing bailouts, not
securing the borders and not
drilling for oil in our own coun­
try, which would hold down en­
ergy prices?
Many I S. citizens who em­
brace the American way have
engaged in “tea parties" recent­
ly. That's a good start, but it is
not enough.
Now there is an effective way
for you to do something about
government that does what they
want rather than what you want.
You can join the I S. Citizens As­
sociation.
The I S. Citizens Association
represents the biggest and most
effective organized effort on the
part of I S. citizens to produce
government that works in the
interest of I .S. citizens and not
in the interest of special inter­
ests and government officials.
The I S. Citizens Association
will turn you into an empow­
ered Citizen.
Both parties. Republicans
and Democrats, have been
guilty of taking our country in a
direction to which most I S. cit­
izens do not want it to go. They
have taken it in the direction of
socialism.
However. President Barack
Obama and the Democrats have
recently engaged in a push for
socialism at a rate that has nev­
er occurred in the history of the
United States. Barack Obama ' n
and the Democrat s agenda is
on the verge of changing the
United States from a capitalist
country to a socialist country It
is capitalism that has made the
United States the most prosper­
ous and most powerful nation
in the history of the world. Capi
talism means free enterprise
and freedom for citizens. So­
cialism means government con­
trols everything and no freedom
for anyone else.
Barack Obama and the De­
mocrats now want to change the
country to a socialist country
more like (Alba and North Ko­
rea. Socialism has never worked
in history for any nation that has
ever tried it to produce prosper­
ity for its citizens. Socialism only
produces prosperity for the so­
cialistic government rulers
In capitalist countries, only
10% of the people temporarily
live in poverty In socialist coun­
tries, 99% of the people live in
poverty permanently. The 1% so­
cialist rulers permanently live a
life of pros|x*rity and ultra luxury
Barack Obama and the De­
mocrats know this. Why do they
want to impose socialism on
our country when they know
that history shows it does not
work for the citizens? Because
socialism provides much more
power to the government rulers
than capitalism. It prolongs
their power and, in many cases,
makes their power permanent.
Their reasons are totally selfish.
They don 't care about you; they
care about themselves.
Barack Obama’s and the
Democrat’s agenda for bailouts,
nationalizing private corpora­
tions. their socialized medicine
and socialist energy taxes, for­
merly called Carbon Cap-and-
Trade and now called “The Cli­
mate Bill", will produce the fol­
lowing for you very shortly : you
will soon see great increases in
your income taxes, property
taxes, utility bills, and gasoline
prices. You will be denied med­
ical care. Your Social Security
checks will be greatly reduced.
Barack Obama and the De­
mocrats, using their accom­
plices in the left wing of the
news media, have been publish­
ing a great deal of lies to the
public. This deep recession was
not caused by the Bush tax cuts,
Republican lax regulation or
Wall Street greed. The following
is the truth concerning these

up off S. citizens.
I S. Citizens Association Membership is free. As a member,

ment that affects you that you will not get in the leftist news

# Abbas spurns U.S.


# effort to revive


# Mideast talks

BenHubbard
TheAssociatedPress
RAMALLAH, West Bank —
Palestinian President Mah-
moudAbbasandIsraeliPrime
MinisterBenjaminNetanyahu
on Tuesday spurned a new
U.S. effort to revive Mideast
peace talks, sticking to posi-
tions that blocked a return to
thetableinthepast.
Abbas said he would not
resume negotiations unless
Israel freezes settlement con-
struction. Netanyahu said he
wouldneveragreetopartition
of Jerusalem, whose eastern
halfissoughtbythePalestini-
ansasafuturecapital.
Jerusalem,landsthePalestini-
answantfortheirstate.Israel
captured both from Jordan in
the1967Mideastwar.
Instead, Netanyahu has
agreedtoa10-monthmorato-
rium on new construction in
justtheWestBank.Israelcon-
tinuestobuildforJewsineast
Jerusalem.
Abbas said on Tuesday he
will not resume talks under
the current conditions. “No
negotiations,” he told
reporters at his headquarters
in Ramallah.“We won’t agree
to resume negotiations with-
out a full settlement freeze,
With both sides digging in,
anupcomingdiplomaticmis-
sion by U.S. Mideast envoy
George Mitchell could be
doomedfromtheoutset.
Mitchell has said he is try-
ing to persuade both sides to
resume talks aimed at ending
their decades-old conflict.
Previous talks broke off in
December2008.
However,theU.S.hasfailed
to get Israel to halt construc-
tion of Jewish settlements in
the West Bank and east
especially in Jerusalem, for a
certainperiod.”
The Obama administration
has recently suggested
bypassing the settlement
issue by getting the two sides
to discuss the borders of a
Palestinian state, including a
partitionofJerusalem.
U.S. Secretary of State
Hillary Clinton has said that
by focusing on the endgame,
such talks would defuse the
problemofsettlements.
But Abbas’ insistence on a
settlement freeze suggested
theU.S.approachwon’twork.

ing. All this plus, add to that list

those with sensitive skin or chemi-­
cal allergies.

Q. Do I still need to use hot wa-­
ter?
A. No. The EcoWasher® requires
cold water only. (Note: By elimi-­
nating hot water, there is no more
need to sort colors and whites and
no more worrying about shrinking
due to hot water!)
Q. How much energy does it con-­
sume?
A. 25 watts of electricity (less than
the average light bulb). The EcoW-­
asher® only uses electricity when
your washing machine calls for
water.
Q. Is it Eco-­Friendly?
A. Yes, there is zero chemical dis-­
charge from EcoWasher® waste-­
water. That’s a pretty big difference
from the staggering 35 billion gal-­
lons of toxic wastewater that laun-­
dry detergents dump into our
rivers lakes and streams
every year in the
US alone.

A. Not at all. The EcoWasher® is
a simple one time installation in 1
and takes 10 minutes! All you need
is the included screws and template
to mount it behind your washing
machine.
Q. Will it work with any washing
machine?
A. It will work with all residential
washing machines. There are two
different models. One for Hard
Water and one for municipal/city
water.

# !

Phil Canfora, Norton, OH says “I hooked
up my new EcoWasher®. I wanted to see if
it really worked, like the President of the
EcoWasher® company said that it would.
I took a load of clean clothes out of my
drawer and washed them using no detergent just like the
instructions said What I saw in the rinse water made my
skin crawl! The pure ecowater rinsed a slimy looking gunk
out of the clothes that I had previously washed in de-
tergent. I thought they were clean! I put the stuff into a
bottle and sent it to the EcoWasher® company. I washed
the clothes again but this time the rinse water was clean
and clear! I was so incensed by what the EcoWasher® got
out of my ‘detergent’ cleaned clothes that I took every-
thing out of my dresser and rewashed them. I will never
use detergent again! My clothes are clean and smell super
fresh and I am saving money with every load of wash.”
Sue Street, Mother of Two in Massillon,
Ohio says “I took the EcoWasher® 30 day
trial and I am now an EcoWasher® believer.
Your NOT getting it back!! My towels were
old and now they look new again! Now I
can see how chemicals have been gunking up my clothes!
My jeans feel so soft and clean, and my sheets (the ulti-
mate test!) came out amazingly clean and feel awesome!
I used to use dryer sheets, not now! Now I have less static
every time I wash my silky garments. The smell of the
water going into the washer is heavenly! I love the way my
clothes feel and smell with the EcoWasher®!”
Randy Farnsworth, Appliance Expert,
Hartville Hardware says “I’ve sold ap-
pliances for over 20 years and the one
thing I always tell people is they’re using
TOO MUCH DETERGENT! It’s hard on the
machine, and it doesn’t rinse out properly and leaves a film
on their clothes. I was a skeptic at first, so I tried it on my
dogs old blanket that I should have thrown away years ago!
EcoWasher® SOLVED the problem! The blanket came out
smelling fresh, and feeling soft again after one wash! I’m
happy I tried EcoWasher®.”

SPECIAL REPORT - Wisdom
may come with age, but so do
wrinkles.
And at 91 years of age, Ellen
Velchante has got a lot of both.
That was until she tried the
new clinical breakthrough
instant facelift serum that made
her feel 20 years younger.
“I don’t know how else to say
it, but I feel sort of sexy. I feel
like I look 20 years younger,”
Ellen said.
This remarkable new serum
works in just three minutes right
in front of the bathroom mirror.
And today’s readers who
beat the 48-hour order deadline
will be among the first to get
an ampule of this remarkable
Instant Face Lift Serum free.
Developed under the direc-
tion of Dr. Marta I. Rendon, the
famed dermatologist recognized
internationally for her work in
dermatologic medicine. The
serum, called Hydraplasty Face,
is causing a firestorm with peo-
ple trying to get it. It’s the first
ever Lift Serum available only
through Universal Physicians,
and we’ll tell today’s readers
how to be among the first to get
an ampule of the serum free.
“I have so many high-profile
patients, but I also see hundreds
of normal, everyday people and
no matter who they are, one of
the first questions I get is, ‘How
would I look with a face lift?,” Dr.
Rendon said.
“Patients always underes-
timate the pain, expense, and
sometimes unwanted, and unex-
pected, results,” she said.
“That’s why my medical team
and experts from Boca Raton
and Los Angeles, developed
this remarkable new instant lift
serum,” she said.
“What I found is that this
serum can put off the need for
face lift surgery. Individual
results will certainly vary and
so will your results but it’s abso-
lutely safe, painless, temporary,
and inexpensive,” Dr. Rendon
said.
“Men and women who have
used Hydraplasty Lift Serum say
it instantly delivers the look and
feel of a face lift without surgery.
You actually feel the tightening.
Face lines seem like they’ve been
erased and pores shrink not over
time, but instantly. In fact, you
see the remarkable results in
just three minutes,” she said.
The response from her
patients and associates is over-
whelming. “After the first con-
sumer trials, my office received
a volume of calls from patients
trying to get the serum they had
just tried,” Dr. Rendon said.
Kimberlea Guerra, 41, won a
spot in one of the first groups
to try Hydraplasty. “There are
a million products out there and
nothing ever really worked until

remarkable results on real people in both lab and in-home use. This newly-developed lift serum is now being released for home use under
the trade name Hydraplasty. The remarkable non-prescription lift serum provides an instant in-home face transformation where slack-
ened skin appears visibly tightened within seconds, using no needles and requiring no office visits. Today’s readers who beat the 48-hour
order deadline for the Instant Face Lift Serum will be among the first to get a free ampule of Hydraplasty Instant Face Lift. Look for the
“How To Get It” section at the bottom of this page.

## How To Get It

Shipments to drugstore shelves are
now being delayed until Christmas. But
today’s readers have the next 48 hours
to get Hydraplasty Instant Lift Serum
now direct from the Universal Physi-
cians mail delivery center.
And everyone who beats the 48-hour
deadline won’t pay the $176 over-the-
counter price because Universal Physi-
cians automatically takes 50% off, so it’s
a real steal at just $88. Everyone who
beats the 48 hour deadline gets a sec-
ond ampule of Hydraplasty Instant Lift
Serum FREE with every phone-in or
mail order. But not at drugstores.
So, until it hits store shelves, there are
only two ways to get Hydraplasty Lift
Serum and beat the 48-hour offer dead-
line to get one free. The toll free hot-
lines are extremely busy, but try calling
1-800-708-3912
. Ask for Dept. HP140.
Or if you can’t get through, just use a
blank sheet of paper and print your name,
address, and for delivery confirmation, a
phone number or e-mail address.
Then just enclose a check or money
order, payable to Universal Physicians
for $94.84, which covers both the Lift
Serum and Invisible SPF 30 Primer and
just add $6.84 shipping and processing
then mail to:
Universal Physicians Dept. HP140
P.O. Box 950
Plattsburgh, NY 12901
Remember, for faster service,usea
credit card or debit card, and call Uni-
versal Physicians at

Ask for Dept. HP140.
Standard shipping is just $6.84, but
lots of people aren’t waiting and are
springing for an extra $11 for priority
shipping. Either way, everything arrives
packed in a discreet drugstore bag.
Please note that any order post-
marked beyond tomorrow’s deadline
date will not be accepted for the free
offer.(cid:1)

# A DOCTOR DISCOVERS VERMONT'S SECRET


# WHY PEOPLE IN VERMONT ARE HEALTHIER,LESS OVERWEIGHT,STAY YOUNG


# LONGER, LIVE LONGER THAN PEOPLE OF ANV OTHER STATE IN THE UNION

Did you know that:
• You were designed by nature to live an average age of

## mr

• A high pro lien - low carbohydrate diet is unnatural for
you and dangerous to your health?
• Overweight is caused by bad nutrition which also causes
other common visible disorders such as loss of hair, acne,
arthritis, nervous tension, insomnias, etc,
• Senility, and loss of productivity in old age can be
avoided and in fact is not in natures plan ?
• The average A merican diet creates a perfect blood chem-
istry for the growth of harmful bacteria that leads to
both minor and serious diseases?
• You can get high without the use of drugs if you have
the proper nourishment that nature intended.
• Everything you need to lead a longer, healthier life is
right at your local grocery store?
These are just a few of the discoveries made by Vermont
doctor, D. C. Jarvis M. D., in his life-long study of the un­
believably effective health practice known as VERMONT
FOLK MEDICINE. VERMONT FOLK MEDICINE is a
practice that can help you achieve good health, increase
your life span, avoid senility, become more attractive and
more productive. For 200 years, before publications by
Dr. Jarvis, this practice has been known only to native Ver­
monters.
VERMONT FOLK MEDICINE was developed by count­
less generations of Vermonters. Dr. Jarvis M. D., a respected
doctor of modern medicine himself, states that VERMONT
FOLK MEDICINE is superior to modern medicine for the
following reasons:
• VERMONT FOLK MEDICINE was developed from na­
tures plan - not man’s. It was developed by watching the
animals who rely on their instincts provided by nature.
This is the same way the super-effective defense arts of
Judo, Karate and Kung Fu were developed. How can
anyone expect to develop a better system than the de­
signer of it all - Mother Nature 7
• VERMONT FOLK MEDICINE works. It was developed
by trial and error over a 200 year period. This also
means it has passed the acid test of time.
• Although it contains some of the best cures known to
man, which cannot be found in medical books, the main
design of VERMONT FOLK MEDICINE is to prevent
disease before it starts. Modern Medicine is directed,
mainly at curing disease - diseases you should never have
in the first place.
VERMONT FOLK MEDICINE is
based on the premise that man does
not have to undergo psysical impair­
ment and weakening with age. In
Vermont you can see people in their
eighties putting in a lull days work
in the fields with sound minds and
bodies.
VERMONT FOLK MEDICINE
was designed to help man live his full
average life which by nature’s plan
should be 10S years. Yes, ifs true. It
is a common fact that an animal lives
to be 5 times his maturity age. A
horse matures at 4 years and easily
lives to be 20. A cow matures at
2% years and easily lives to 12. A dog
matures at IV* years and easily lives
to be over 8-many live much longer. Man is an animal. B
HIF AVERAGE MAN IN THE IJS WHO

## rni ■

D. C. Jarvis
HIS TIME! With the exception of a notorious retirement
state, which can’t be counted, Vermont leads all other
states rn percentage of population over 65. Not long ago
it had 40,000 persons over that age, and each year nearly
2500 reach it!
Short lives, senility, excessive disease, and so on are not
in nature’s plan. What’s the culprit? The free will of man!
Although free will is indeed a wonderful thing, it does have
some bad side effects. Unlike the animals, we stray away
from our childhood instincts.
The first thing discovered by VERMONT FOLK MEDI-
( INE is that each species is designed to have a different
diet. Nature’s plan for man’s diet requires a high intake of
carbohydrates represented by fruits, berries, edible leaves,
and honey, and a low in take of protien represented by
'*ggs, meat, milk, cheese, etc. Man was also designed to eat
foods derived from corn and rye and not from wheat,
which produces an alkaline blood condition.
Nature designed nearly all animals to have a natural blood con-
Tucked away in the rustic northeast
portion of the United States, Vermont
is indeed a beautiful state where peo­
ple live close to the land and the aru-
mats. **Pht*to» courtesy of the Greater
Vermont Chamber of Commerce.
dition on the acid side. Blood
which leans toward the alkaline
side is a health hazard. Medical
school studies show that harmful
bacteria breeds best in an alka­
line environment. Also, alkaline
blood is thicker than acidic blood.
This makes it more difficult for
the blood to nourish cells, and in­
creases the probability of heart
attacks.
So, everyday Americans are
killing themselves with high pro-
tien-low carbohydrate diets which
contain many wheat products. In
turn, the diets are I) hard on the
system, 2) do not provide the cor­
rect elements for nutrition.J IPro-
duce the bad alkaline condition in the blood. Many of these
diets are the result of weight watching. Little do these peo­
ple know that overweight is caused by I) bad nutrition of
the glands, 2) a blood condition which is not acidic and 3) a
wrong carbohydrate consumption. The correct carbohy­
drates burn up instantly and do not turn in to excess fat.
The following are symptoms of bad nutrition and a diet
that is contrary to nature’s plan: low energy, depression,
insomnia, itchy skin, chronic headache, sensitivity to tem­
perature, sinus trouble, lingering colds, dizzy spells, gas
and indigestion, poor blood circulation, falling hair, dan­
druff, blemishes, lack of skin color, and overweight.
Two important foods in VERMONT FOLK MEDICINE
are honey and apple cider vinegar. They are a major contri­
bution toward providing the correct carbohydrates, the cor­
rect nutrition and an acidic blood condition.
Why honey? Nature intended that we have a trickle of
sugar passing thru the intestinal walls at all times and one
teaspoonful of sugar in our blood stream at all times. The
latter is ultra critical In honey, the sugar has already been
digested in the bee’s stomach. Therefore, honey contains
two natural sugars dextrose and levatose, which go into the
blood stream immediately. Honey is free from bacteria. Ex­
periments show that bacteria will not live in honey. Honey
is a mild, natural sedative which is calming to the body.
Honey is a mild, natural laxitive. Honey is nonirritating to
the digestive track. Honey is, of all sugars, handled best by
the kidneys. Honey is rich in vital minerals from the flow­
ers. By an infallible instinct, the bee has a way of knowing
which flowers are high in quality and which are not. There­
fore, honey is a perfect food derived from only the health­
iest plants. In Vermont there is a saying, “We’ve got to
trust someone - why not let it be the bee?**
Why apple and vinegar? Apple cider vinegar carries with
it all the vital life elements of the apple. Apple cider vine­
gar maintains the proper acid level of the blood. Apple
cider vinegar can maintain your proper weight. Dr. Jarvis’s
book describes a method using apple cider vinegar where
the person eats normally but will gradually lose weight
until the body finds its proper weight level. With this meth­
od women will gradually see their dress size go from 20, to
18, to 16 and gradually stop at then natural body size. Men
will see a 40 inch waist line drop to 38, then 36 finally to
the proper size as the body properly burns off excess fat
because it is in the proper chemical state that nature in­
tended.
It is not enough, however, to only know the ingredients
of good health. You must know how and in what combin­
ation to use honey, vinegar and other vital products of
nature. Dr. Jarvis has published the results of his life long
study of VERMONT FOLK MEDICINE in his book titled
FOLK MEDICINE. In every day language he tells you how
to use VFft MANT FOLK MEDICINE. Here are some of
the highlights of this book:
• How VERMONT FOLK MEDICINE can help you live
a longer, fuller, healthier life.
• How to use honey and apple cider vinegar to: I) gam
renewed health and energy, 2) as pan of prenatal care
to produce a strong healthy baby, 3) Produce sound
sleep, 4) Reduce your susceptibility to disease, 5) Re­
duce the chance of heart attacks, 6) Produce a relaxed
comfortable state of being.
• How to use honey and apple cider vinegar to cure:
I) morning sickness, 2) sinus, 3) migrain headaches, 4}
dizziness 5) arthritus, 6) bed-wetting, 7) coughs, 8) hay
r ¥8 \4L.
fever, 9) colic in babies.
* How to use apple cider vinegar, castor oil, and corn oil
to lf produce beautiful healthy skin and bring color back
2f clear up blemishes, 3) get rid of dandruff, 4) grow
healthy hair and eyelashes, 5) cure hives, 6) cure hemor-
roids, 71 get rid of moles. 8) relieve lameness, 9/ treat
poison ivy, IO) treat shingles, ll) eliminate night sweats,
12) get rid of varicose viens, 13) get rid of ring worm, 14)

* How to get your daily requirement of the all important
